sanskrit: अथैकदा मुनिश्रेष्ठं त्रिकलज्ञं पराशरम् । पप्रच्छोपेत्य मैत्रेयः प्रणिपत्य कृताञ्जलिः ॥१॥
transliteration: athaikadā muniśreṣṭhaṃ trikalajñaṃ parāśaram . papracchopetya maitreyaḥ praṇipatya kṛtāñjaliḥ.
separated_sandhi: atha ekadā muniśreṣṭham trikalajñam parāśarampapraccha upetya maitreyaḥ praṇipatya kṛtāñjaliḥ
english: Then, one day, Maitreya, having approached the foremost sage Parashara, who was a knower of the three divisions of time (past, present, and future), bowed down with folded hands and questioned him.

sanskrit: भगवन् परमं पुण्यं गुह्यं वेदाङ्गमुत्तमम् । त्रिस्कन्धं ज्यौतिषं होरा गणितं संहितेति च ॥२॥
transliteration: bhagavan paramaṃ puṇyaṃ guhyaṃ vedāṅgamuttamam . triskandhaṃ jyautiṣaṃ horā gaṇitaṃ saṃhiteti ca.
separated_sandhi: bhagavan paramam puṇyam guhyam vedāṅgam uttamamtriskandham jyautiṣam horā gaṇitam saṃhitā iti ca
english: O venerable one, (Jyotisha is) the supreme, sacred, profound, and excellent limb of the Veda, comprising three branches: Hora, Ganita, and Samhita.

sanskrit: एतेष्वपि त्रिषु श्रेष्ठा होरेति श्रूयते मुने । त्वत्तस्तां श्रोतुमिच्छामि कृपया वद मे प्रभो ॥३॥
transliteration: eteṣvapi triṣu śreṣṭhā horeti śrūyate mune . tvattastāṃ śrotumicchāmi kṛpayā vada me prabho.
separated_sandhi: eteṣu api triṣu śreṣṭhā horā iti śrūyate munetvattaḥ tām śrotum icchāmi kṛpayā vada me prabho
english: O sage (mune), it is said that among these three (branches), Hora is the best. O Lord (prabho), I wish to hear about it from you; please tell it to me out of kindness.

sanskrit: कथं सृष्टिरियं जाता जगतश्च लयः कथम् । खस्थानां भूस्थतानां च सम्बन्धं वद विस्तरात् ॥४॥
transliteration: kathaṃ sṛṣṭiriyaṃ jātā jagataśca layaḥ katham . khasthānāṃ bhūsthatānāṃ ca sambandhaṃ vada vistarāt.
separated_sandhi: katham sṛṣṭiḥ iyam jātā jagataḥ ca layaḥ kathamkhasthānām bhūsthatānām ca sambandham vada vistarāt
english: How did this creation come into being, and how does the dissolution of the world (jagat) occur? Please explain in detail the connection between celestial bodies (khasthānām) and terrestrial beings/phenomena (bhūsthatānām).

sanskrit: साधु पृष्टं त्वया विप्र लोकानुग्रहकारिना । अथाहं परमं ब्रह्म तच्छक्तिं भारतीं पुनः ॥५॥
transliteration: sādhu pṛṣṭaṃ tvayā vipra lokānugrahakārinā . athāhaṃ paramaṃ brahma tacchaktiṃ bhāratīṃ punaḥ.
separated_sandhi: sādhu pṛṣṭam tvayā vipra lokānugrahakāriṇā athaaham paramam brahma tat śaktim bhāratīm punaḥ
english: O Brahmin (vipra), you, who are a benefactor of the world, have asked a very good question. Now I (will proceed, after bowing to) the supreme (brahman) and His power (śakti), Bharati, once again.

sanskrit: सूर्य नत्वा ग्रहपतिं जगदुत्पत्तिकारण्म् । वक्ष्यामि वेदनयनं यथा ब्रह्ममुखाच्छ्रुतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sūrya natvā grahapatiṃ jagadutpattikāraṇm . vakṣyāmi vedanayanaṃ yathā brahmamukhācchrutam.
separated_sandhi: sūryam natvā grahapatim jagadutpattikāraṇamvakṣyāmi vedanayanam yathā brahmamukhāt śrutam
english: Having bowed to the Sun (sūrya), the lord of planets and the cause of the world's creation, I will now speak (this knowledge, which is) the eye of the Veda, just as I heard it from the mouth of Brahma.

sanskrit: शान्ताय गुरुभक्ताय सर्वदा सत्यवादिने । आस्तिकाय प्रदतव्यं ततः श्रेयो ह्यवाप्स्यति ॥७॥
transliteration: śāntāya gurubhaktāya sarvadā satyavādine . āstikāya pradatavyaṃ tataḥ śreyo hyavāpsyati.
separated_sandhi: śāntāya gurubhaktāya sarvadā satyavādineāstikāya pradatavyaṃ tataḥ śreyaḥ hi avāpsyati
english: This [knowledge] should be imparted to one who is peaceful, devoted to their spiritual teacher (guru), always truthful, and devout. From this, indeed, one will achieve well-being.

sanskrit: न देयं परशिष्याय नास्तिकाय शठय वा । दत्ते प्रतिदिनं दुःखं जायते नात्र संशयः ॥८॥
transliteration: na deyaṃ paraśiṣyāya nāstikāya śaṭhaya vā . datte pratidinaṃ duḥkhaṃ jāyate nātra saṃśayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: na deyaṃ paraśiṣyāya nāstikāya śaṭhāya vā dattepratidinaṃ duḥkhaṃ jāyate na atra saṃśayaḥ
english: This [knowledge] should not be given to another's student, nor to a non-believer, nor to a deceitful person. If it is given, sorrow will arise every day; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: एकोऽव्यक्तात्मको विष्णुरनादिः प्रभुरीश्वरः । शुद्धसत्वो जगत्स्वामी निर्गुणस्त्रिगुणान्वितः ॥९॥
transliteration: eko'vyaktātmako viṣṇuranādiḥ prabhurīśvaraḥ . śuddhasatvo jagatsvāmī nirguṇastriguṇānvitaḥ.
separated_sandhi: ekaḥ avyaktātmakaḥ viṣṇuḥ anādiḥ prabhuḥ īśvaraḥśuddhasattvaḥ jagatsvāmī nirguṇaḥ triguṇānvitah
english: Vishnu is the one unmanifest (avyaktātmaka) Self (ātman), without beginning, the Lord, the Supreme Controller, pure in nature (sattva), the master of the universe, simultaneously without attributes (guṇas) and endowed with the three attributes (guṇas).

sanskrit: संसारकारकः श्रीमान्निमित्तात्मा प्रतापवान् । एकांशेन जगत्सर्व सृजत्यवति लीलया ॥१०॥
transliteration: saṃsārakārakaḥ śrīmānnimittātmā pratāpavān . ekāṃśena jagatsarva sṛjatyavati līlayā.
separated_sandhi: saṃsārakārakaḥ śrīmān nimittātmā pratāpavānekāṃśena jagat sarva sṛjati avati līlayā
english: That glorious and mighty one, who is the instrumental cause and the creator of the cycle of worldly existence (saṃsāra), creates and sustains the entire universe effortlessly, merely by a single fraction of Himself.

sanskrit: त्रिपादं तस्य देवत्य ह्यमृतं तत्त्वदर्शिनः । विदन्ति तत्प्रमाणं च सप्रधानं तथैकपात् ॥११॥
transliteration: tripādaṃ tasya devatya hyamṛtaṃ tattvadarśinaḥ . vidanti tatpramāṇaṃ ca sapradhānaṃ tathaikapāt.
separated_sandhi: tripādam tasya devatya hi amṛtam tattvadarśinaḥvidanti tatpramāṇam ca sapradhānam tathā ekapāt
english: Indeed, the seers of truth (tattvadarśinaḥ) understand His three-quarters to be divine and immortal. They also comprehend its true extent, and similarly, the one-fourth part that exists with primal nature (pradhāna).

sanskrit: व्यक्ताव्यक्तात्मको विष्णुर्वासुदेवस्तु गीयते । यदव्यक्तात्मको विष्णुः श्क्तितद्वयसमन्वितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyaktāvyaktātmako viṣṇurvāsudevastu gīyate . yadavyaktātmako viṣṇuḥ śktitadvayasamanvitaḥ.
separated_sandhi: vyaktāvyaktātmakaḥ viṣṇuḥ vāsudevaḥ tu gīyateyat avyaktātmakaḥ viṣṇuḥ śaktitadvayasamanvitaḥ
english: Vishnu, whose nature (ātman) is both manifest and unmanifest, is indeed called Vasudeva. This is because Vishnu, in his unmanifest (avyaktātmaka) aspect, is endowed with two powers (śakti).

sanskrit: व्यक्तात्मकस्त्रिभिर्युक्तः कथ्यतेऽनन्तशक्तिमान् । सत्त्वप्रधाना श्रीशक्तिर्भूश्क्तिश्च रजोगुणा ॥१३॥
transliteration: vyaktātmakastribhiryuktaḥ kathyate'nantaśaktimān . sattvapradhānā śrīśaktirbhūśktiśca rajoguṇā.
separated_sandhi: vyaktātmakaḥ tribhiḥ yuktaḥ kathyate anantaśaktimānsattvapradhānā śrīśaktiḥ bhūśaktiḥ ca rajoguṇā
english: In his manifest (vyaktātmaka) aspect, he is said to be endowed with three [powers/aspects] and possesses infinite power (śakti). The Śrī (śakti) is predominantly sattva (guṇa), and the Bhū (śakti) is characterized by rajas (guṇa).

sanskrit: शक्तिस्तृतीया या प्राक्ता नीलाख्या ध्वान्तरूपिणी । वासुदेवश्चतुर्थोऽभूच्छ्रीशक्त्या प्रेरितो यदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: śaktistṛtīyā yā prāktā nīlākhyā dhvāntarūpiṇī . vāsudevaścaturtho'bhūcchrīśaktyā prerito yadā.
separated_sandhi: śaktiḥ tṛtīyā yā prāktā nīlā ākhyā dhvānta rūpiṇī |vāsudevaḥ caturthaḥ abhūt śrī śaktyā preritaḥ yadā
english: The third "śakti" (power), which was described earlier, is named Nīlā and embodies darkness. When Vāsudeva, the fourth (form), was impelled by the "śrīśakti" (power of prosperity/beauty)...

sanskrit: संकर्षणश्च प्रद्युम्नोऽनिरुद्ध इति मूर्तिधृक् । तमःश्क्त्याऽन्विता विष्णुर्देवः संकर्षणाभिधः ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṃkarṣaṇaśca pradyumno'niruddha iti mūrtidhṛk . tamaḥśktyā'nvitā viṣṇurdevaḥ saṃkarṣaṇābhidhaḥ.
separated_sandhi: saṅkarṣaṇaḥ ca pradyumnaḥ aniruddhaḥ iti mūrti dhṛk |tamaḥ śaktyā anvitā viṣṇuḥ devaḥ saṅkarṣaṇa abhidhaḥ
english: Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha thus manifest in forms. Viṣṇu, the deity known as Saṅkarṣaṇa, is endowed with the "tamasśakti" (power of inertia/darkness).

sanskrit: प्रद्युम्नो रजसा शक्त्याऽनिरुध्हः सत्त्वया युतः । महान् संकर्षणाज्जातः प्रद्युम्नाद्यदहंअकृतिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pradyumno rajasā śaktyā'nirudhhaḥ sattvayā yutaḥ . mahān saṃkarṣaṇājjātaḥ pradyumnādyadahaṃakṛtiḥ.
separated_sandhi: pradyumnaḥ rajasā śaktyā aniruddhaḥ sattvayā yutaḥ| mahān saṅkarṣaṇāt jātaḥ pradyumnāt yat ahaṅkṛtiḥ
english: Pradyumna is associated with the "rajasśakti" (power of activity/passion), and Aniruddha with the "sattva" quality (power of goodness/purity). From Saṅkarṣaṇa, Mahat (the Cosmic Intellect) was born, and from Pradyumna, "ahaṅkṛti" (the ego or principle of individuation).

sanskrit: अनिरुद्धात् स्वयं जातो ब्रह्माहंकाकमूर्तिधृक् । सर्वषु सर्वशक्तिश्च स्वशक्त्याऽधिकया युतः ॥१७॥
transliteration: aniruddhāt svayaṃ jāto brahmāhaṃkākamūrtidhṛk . sarvaṣu sarvaśaktiśca svaśaktyā'dhikayā yutaḥ.
separated_sandhi: aniruddhāt svayam jātaḥ brahmā ahaṅkāraka mūrti dhṛk| sarveṣu sarva śaktiḥ ca sva śaktyā adhikayā yutaḥ
english: From Aniruddha, Brahmā himself was born, who embodies the form of ego ("ahaṅkāra"). He is all-powerful in all beings, and endowed with his own greater "śakti" (power).

sanskrit: अहंकारस्त्रिध भूत्वा सर्वमेतद्विस्तरात् । सात्त्विको राजसश्चैव तामसश्चेदहंकृतिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ahaṃkārastridha bhūtvā sarvametadvistarāt . sāttviko rājasaścaiva tāmasaścedahaṃkṛtiḥ.
separated_sandhi: ahaṅkāraḥ tridhā bhūtvā sarvam etat vistarāt |sāttvikaḥ rājasaḥ ca eva tāmasaḥ ca it ahaṅkṛtiḥ
english: This "ahaṅkāra" (ego), having manifested in three ways, expands all of this (creation). Indeed, "ahaṅkṛti" (the ego) is Sāttvika, Rājasa, and Tāmasa.

sanskrit: देवा वैकारिकाज्जातास्तैजसादिन्द्रियाणि । तामसच्चैवभू तानि खादीनि स्वस्वशक्तिभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: devā vaikārikājjātāstaijasādindriyāṇi . tāmasaccaivabhū tāni khādīni svasvaśaktibhiḥ.
separated_sandhi: devāḥ vaikārikāt jātāḥ taijasāt indriyāṇitāmasāt ca eva bhūtāni khādīni sva-sva-śaktibhiḥ
english: The deities are born from the sattvic principle (vaikārika), the senses from the rajasic principle (taijasa), and indeed, the gross elements, beginning with ether, arise from the tamasic principle (tāmasa) through their respective powers.

sanskrit: श्रीशक्त्या सहितो विष्णुः सदा पाति जगत्त्रयम् । भूशक्त्या सृजते ब्रह्मा नीलशक्त्या शिवोऽत्ति हि ॥२०॥
transliteration: śrīśaktyā sahito viṣṇuḥ sadā pāti jagattrayam . bhūśaktyā sṛjate brahmā nīlaśaktyā śivo'tti hi.
separated_sandhi: śrī-śaktyā sahitaḥ viṣṇuḥ sadā pāti jagat-trayambhū-śaktyā sṛjate brahmā nīla-śaktyā śivaḥ atti hi
english: Vishnu, accompanied by the power (śakti) of Śrī, always protects the three worlds. Brahmā creates by the power (śakti) of Bhū, and indeed, Shiva destroys by the power (śakti) of Nīlā.

sanskrit: सर्वेषु चैव जीवेषु परमात्मा विराजते । सर्वं हि तदिदं ब्रह्मन् स्थितं हि परमात्मनि ॥२१॥
transliteration: sarveṣu caiva jīveṣu paramātmā virājate . sarvaṃ hi tadidaṃ brahman sthitaṃ hi paramātmani.
separated_sandhi: sarveṣu ca eva jīveṣu paramātmā virājate sarvamhi tat idam brahman sthitam hi paramātmani
english: And indeed, the Supreme Self (ātman) manifests in all living beings. O Brahmā (brahman), all of this, indeed, is situated in the Supreme Self (ātman).

sanskrit: सर्वेषु चैव जीवेषु स्थितंह्यंशद्वयं क्वचित् । जीवांशो ह्यधिकस्तद्वत् परमात्मांशकः किल ॥२२॥
transliteration: sarveṣu caiva jīveṣu sthitaṃhyaṃśadvayaṃ kvacit . jīvāṃśo hyadhikastadvat paramātmāṃśakaḥ kila.
separated_sandhi: sarveṣu ca eva jīveṣu sthitam hi aṃśa-dvayam kvacitjīva-aṃśaḥ hi adhikaḥ tad-vat paramātma-aṃśakaḥ kila
english: Indeed, in all living beings, a certain dual aspect is present. The aspect of the individual soul (jīva) is certainly more dominant, and likewise, it is also said, is the aspect of the Supreme Self (ātman).

sanskrit: सूर्यादयो ग्रहाः सर्वे ब्रह्मकामद्विषादयः । एते चान्ये च बहवः परमात्मांशकाधिकाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sūryādayo grahāḥ sarve brahmakāmadviṣādayaḥ . ete cānye ca bahavaḥ paramātmāṃśakādhikāḥ.
separated_sandhi: sūrya-ādayaḥ grahāḥ sarve brahma-kāma-dviṣa-ādayaḥete ca anye ca bahavaḥ paramātma-aṃśaka-adhikāḥ
english: All the planets, beginning with the Sun, and these and many other beings like Brahmā (brahman), Kāma (desire), and Dviṣa (hatred), are those in whom the aspect of the Supreme Self (ātman) is dominant.

sanskrit: शक्तयश्च तथैतेषमधिकांशाः श्रियादयः । स्वस्वशक्तिषु चान्यासु ज्ञेया जीवांशकाधिकाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: śaktayaśca tathaiteṣamadhikāṃśāḥ śriyādayaḥ . svasvaśaktiṣu cānyāsu jñeyā jīvāṃśakādhikāḥ.
separated_sandhi: śaktayaḥ ca tathā eteṣām adhikāṃśāḥ śriyādayaḥsvasvaśaktiṣu ca anyāsu jñeyāḥ jīvāṃśakādhikāḥ
english: Similarly, the powers (śakti) of these (deities/planets), such as Śrī (Lakshmi) and others, are predominant aspects. They are to be understood as superior to the individual soul's (jīvāṃśa) portions, both in their respective capacities and in others.

sanskrit: रामकृष्णादयो ये ह्यवतारा रमापतेः । तेऽपि जीवांशसंयुक्ताः किंवा ब्रूहि मुनिश्वर ॥१॥
transliteration: rāmakṛṣṇādayo ye hyavatārā ramāpateḥ . te'pi jīvāṃśasaṃyuktāḥ kiṃvā brūhi muniśvara.
separated_sandhi: rāmakṛṣṇādayaḥ ye hi avatārāḥ ramāpateḥ teapi jīvāṃśasaṃyuktāḥ kim vā brūhi muniśvara
english: O chief of sages, tell me, are Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and these other incarnations (avatārāḥ) of Viṣṇu (Ramāpati) also endowed with parts of individual souls (jīva), or is it something else?

sanskrit: रामः कृष्णश्च भो विप्र नृसिंहः सूकरस्तथा । एते पूर्णावताराश्च ह्यन्ये जीवांशकान्विताः ॥२॥
transliteration: rāmaḥ kṛṣṇaśca bho vipra nṛsiṃhaḥ sūkarastathā . ete pūrṇāvatārāśca hyanye jīvāṃśakānvitāḥ.
separated_sandhi: rāmaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ ca bho vipra nṛsiṃhaḥ sūkaraḥ tathāete pūrṇāvatārāḥ ca hi anye jīvāṃśakānvitāḥ
english: O Brahmin, Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, Nṛsiṃha, and Sūkara – these are certainly the complete incarnations (avatārāḥ), while others are endowed with portions of individual souls (jīva).

sanskrit: अवताराण्यनेकानि ह्यजस्य परमात्मनः । जीवानां कर्मफलदो ग्रहरूपी जनार्दनः ॥३॥
transliteration: avatārāṇyanekāni hyajasya paramātmanaḥ . jīvānāṃ karmaphalado graharūpī janārdanaḥ.
separated_sandhi: avatārāṇi anekāni hi ajasya paramātmanaḥjīvānām karmaphaladaḥ graharūpī janārdanaḥ
english: Indeed, there are many incarnations (avatārāṇi) of the unborn Supreme Self (paramātman). The Lord Janārdana, in the form of planets (graha), bestows the results of (karma) actions upon living beings (jīva).

sanskrit: दैत्यानां बलनाशाय देवानां बलवृद्धये । धर्मसंस्तापनार्थाय ग्रहाज्जाताः शुभाः क्रमात् ॥४॥
transliteration: daityānāṃ balanāśāya devānāṃ balavṛddhaye . dharmasaṃstāpanārthāya grahājjātāḥ śubhāḥ kramāt.
separated_sandhi: daityānām balanāśāya devānām balavṛddhayedharmasaṃstāpanārthāya grahāt jātāḥ śubhāḥ kramāt
english: For the destruction of the demons' power, for the increase of the gods' power, and for the purpose of firmly establishing natural law (dharma), auspicious beings are born successively from the planets.

sanskrit: रामोऽवतारः सूर्यस्य चन्द्रस्य यदुनायकः । नृसिंहो भूमिपुत्रस्य बुद्धः सोमसुतस्य च ॥५॥
transliteration: rāmo'vatāraḥ sūryasya candrasya yadunāyakaḥ . nṛsiṃho bhūmiputrasya buddhaḥ somasutasya ca.
separated_sandhi: rāmaḥ avatāraḥ sūryasya candrasya yadunāyakaḥnṛsiṃhaḥ bhūmiputrasya buddhaḥ somasutasya ca
english: Rama is the incarnation (avatāra) of the Sun. The leader of the Yadus (Krishna) is the incarnation of the Moon. Nṛsiṃha is the incarnation of the son of Earth (Mars), and Buddha is the incarnation of the son of Soma (Mercury).

sanskrit: वामनो विबुधेज्यस्य भार्गवो भार्गवस्य च । कूर्मो भास्करपुत्रस्य सैंहिकेयस्य सूकरः ॥६॥
transliteration: vāmano vibudhejyasya bhārgavo bhārgavasya ca . kūrmo bhāskaraputrasya saiṃhikeyasya sūkaraḥ.
separated_sandhi: vāmanaḥ vibudhejyasya bhārgavaḥ bhārgavasya cakūrmaḥ bhāskaraputrasya saiṃhikeyasya sūkaraḥ
english: Vāmana is the incarnation (avatāra) of the one worshipped by the gods (Jupiter). Bhārgava (Paraśurāma) is the incarnation of Bhārgava (Venus), and Kūrma is the incarnation of the son of the Sun (Saturn). Sūkara (Varāha) is the incarnation of the son of Siṃhikā (Rāhu).

sanskrit: केतोर्मीनावतारश्च ये चान्ये तेऽपि खेटजाः । परात्मांशोऽधिको येषु ते सर्वे खेचराभिधः ॥७॥
transliteration: ketormīnāvatāraśca ye cānye te'pi kheṭajāḥ . parātmāṃśo'dhiko yeṣu te sarve khecarābhidhaḥ.
separated_sandhi: Ketoḥ mīna-avatāraḥ ca ye ca anye te api kheṭa-jāḥparātma-aṃśaḥ adhikaḥ yeṣu te sarve khecara-abhidhaḥ
english: Ketu, as the fish incarnation, and all other beings born from the planets – those in whom a predominant portion of the Supreme Self (paramātman) resides are all designated as celestial beings (khecara).

sanskrit: जीवांशोह्यधिको येषु जीवास्ते चै प्रकीर्तितः । सूर्यादिभो ग्रहेभ्यश्च परमात्मांशनिःसृताः ॥८॥
transliteration: jīvāṃśohyadhiko yeṣu jīvāste cai prakīrtitaḥ . sūryādibho grahebhyaśca paramātmāṃśaniḥsṛtāḥ.
separated_sandhi: jīva-aṃśaḥ hi adhikaḥ yeṣu jīvāḥ te ca i prakīrtitaḥsūrya-ādibhyaḥ grahebhyah ca parātma-aṃśa-niḥsṛtāḥ
english: Indeed, those in whom the portion of the individual soul (jīva) is predominant are declared to be individual souls (jīva). Moreover, portions of the Supreme Self (paramātman) emanate from the Sun and other planets.

sanskrit: रामकृष्णादयः सर्व ह्यवतारा भवन्ति चै । तत्रैव ते विलीयन्ते पुनः कार्योत्तर सदा ॥९॥
transliteration: rāmakṛṣṇādayaḥ sarva hyavatārā bhavanti cai . tatraiva te vilīyante punaḥ kāryottara sadā.
separated_sandhi: rāma-kṛṣṇa-ādayaḥ sarva hi avatārāḥ bhavanti cai tatra eva te vilīyante punaḥ kārya-uttara sadā
english: Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and all other such incarnations (avatāra) manifest. They always merge back into that very source after their particular task is accomplished.

sanskrit: जीवांशनिःसृतास्तेषां तेभ्यो जाता नरादयः । तेऽपि तत्रैव लीयन्ते तेऽव्यक्ते समयन्ति हि ॥१०॥
transliteration: jīvāṃśaniḥsṛtāsteṣāṃ tebhyo jātā narādayaḥ . te'pi tatraiva līyante te'vyakte samayanti hi.
separated_sandhi: jīvāṃśaniḥsṛtāḥ teṣām tebhyaḥ jātāḥ narādayaḥ teapi tatra eva līyante te avyakte samayanti hi
english: From the emanated portions of living beings (jīva), humans and other creatures are born. They too eventually dissolve back into that very source, and indeed, they merge into the unmanifest (avyakta).

sanskrit: इदं ते कथितं विप्र सर्वं यस्मिन् भवेदिति । भूतान्यपि भविष्यन्ति तत्तज्जातन्ति तद्विदः ॥११॥
transliteration: idaṃ te kathitaṃ vipra sarvaṃ yasmin bhavediti . bhūtānyapi bhaviṣyanti tattajjātanti tadvidaḥ.
separated_sandhi: idam te kathitam vipra sarvam yasmin bhavet itibhūtāni api bhaviṣyanti tattat jātanti tadvidaḥ
english: O Brahmin, I have explained this entire process to you, through which all existence manifests. Beings (already existing) will continue to arise (in the future); those who know this (tadvidaḥ) understand that various things are born.

sanskrit: विना तज्ज्यैतिषं नान्यो ज्ञातुं शक्नोति कर्हिचित् । तत्मादवश्यमध्येयं भाह्मणेश्च विशेषतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vinā tajjyaitiṣaṃ nānyo jñātuṃ śaknoti karhicit . tatmādavaśyamadhyeyaṃ bhāhmaṇeśca viśeṣataḥ.
separated_sandhi: vinā tad jyautiṣam na anyaḥ jñātum śaknoti karhicittasmāt avaśyam adhyeyam brāhmaṇaiḥ ca viśeṣataḥ
english: Without that Jyotisha (astrology/astronomy), no other person can ever know (these truths). Therefore, it must certainly be studied, especially by Brahmins.

sanskrit: यो नरः शास्त्रमज्ञात्वा ज्यौतिषं खलु निन्दति । रौरवं नरकं भुक्त्वा चान्धत्वं चान्यजन्मनि ॥१३॥
transliteration: yo naraḥ śāstramajñātvā jyautiṣaṃ khalu nindati . rauravaṃ narakaṃ bhuktvā cāndhatvaṃ cānyajanmani.
separated_sandhi: yaḥ naraḥ śāstram ajñātvā jyautiṣam khalu nindatirauravam narakam bhuktvā ca andhatvam ca anyajanmani
english: The person who, without knowing the science of astrology (jyotiṣa), truly criticizes it, will, after experiencing the Raurava hell, also suffer blindness in another birth.

sanskrit: कथितं भवता प्रेम्णा ग्रहावतरणं मुने । तेषं गुणस्वरूपाद्यं कृपया कथ्यतां पुनः ॥१॥
transliteration: kathitaṃ bhavatā premṇā grahāvataraṇaṃ mune . teṣaṃ guṇasvarūpādyaṃ kṛpayā kathyatāṃ punaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kathitam bhavatā premṇā grahāvataraṇam muneteṣām guṇasvarūpādyam kṛpayā kathyatām punaḥ
english: O sage (muni), you have lovingly described the descent of the planets (graha). Now, please kindly explain their qualities, characteristics, and other aspects again.

sanskrit: श‍ृणु विप्र प्रवक्ष्यामि भग्रहाणां परिस्थितिम् । आकाशे यानि दृश्यन्ते ज्योतिर्बिम्बात्यनेकशः ॥२॥
transliteration: śa‍ृṇu vipra pravakṣyāmi bhagrahāṇāṃ paristhitim . ākāśe yāni dṛśyante jyotirbimbātyanekaśaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śṛṇu vipra pravakṣyāmi bhagrahāṇām paristhitimākāśe yāni dṛśyante jyotirbimbāni anekaśaḥ
english: Listen, O Brahmin (vipra)! I will now explain the situation of the fixed stars (bha) and planets (graha). There are many luminous celestial bodies (jyotirbimba) visible in the sky.

sanskrit: तेषु नक्षत्रसंज्ञानि ग्रहसंज्ञानि कानिचित् । तानि नक्षत्रनामानि स्थिरस्थानानि यानि चै ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣu nakṣatrasaṃjñāni grahasaṃjñāni kānicit . tāni nakṣatranāmāni sthirasthānāni yāni cai.
separated_sandhi: teṣu nakṣatrasaṃjñāni grahasaṃjñāni kānicittāni nakṣatranāmāni sthirasthānāni yāni ca
english: Among these, some are designated as lunar mansions (nakṣatra) and some as planets (graha). Those that have fixed positions are indeed called lunar mansions (nakṣatra).

sanskrit: गच्छन्तो भानि गृह्णन्ति सततं ये तु ते ग्रहः । भचक्रस्य नगश्व्यंशा अश्विन्यादिसमाह्वयाः ॥४॥
transliteration: gacchanto bhāni gṛhṇanti satataṃ ye tu te grahaḥ . bhacakrasya nagaśvyaṃśā aśvinyādisamāhvayāḥ.
separated_sandhi: gacchantaḥ bhāni gṛhṇanti satatam ye tu te grahaḥbhacakrasya nagaśvyaṃśāḥ aśvinyādisamāhvayāḥ
english: But those celestial bodies (graha) that constantly move, occupying the positions of the fixed stars (bha), they are the planets. The lunar mansions (nakṣatra) of the zodiac (bhacakra) are twenty-seven divisions, beginning with Ashvinī.

sanskrit: तद्द्वादशविभागास्तु तुल्य मेषादिसंज्ञकाः । प्रसिद्धा राशयः सन्ति ग्रहास्त्वर्कादिसंज्ञकाः ॥५॥
transliteration: taddvādaśavibhāgāstu tulya meṣādisaṃjñakāḥ . prasiddhā rāśayaḥ santi grahāstvarkādisaṃjñakāḥ.
separated_sandhi: tat dvādaśavibhāgāḥ tu tulya meṣādisaṃjñakāḥprasiddhāḥ rāśayaḥ santi grahāḥ tu arkādisaṃjñakāḥ
english: These twelve equal divisions, named after Aries (Meṣa) and so on, are the well-known zodiacal signs (rāśi). The planets (graha) are similarly named after the Sun (Arka) and so on.

sanskrit: राशीनामुदयो लग्नं तद्वशादेव जन्मिनाम् । ग्रहयोग्वियोगाभ्यां फलं चिन्त्यं शुभाशुभम् ॥६॥
transliteration: rāśīnāmudayo lagnaṃ tadvaśādeva janminām . grahayogviyogābhyāṃ phalaṃ cintyaṃ śubhāśubham.
separated_sandhi: rāśīnām udayaḥ lagnaṃ tadvaśāt eva janmināmgrahayogaviyogābhyām phalam cintyam śubhāśubham
english: The rising of the zodiacal signs (rāśi) is the Ascendant (Lagna). Based solely on this, the results (phala), both auspicious and inauspicious, for living beings are to be considered through the conjunctions (yoga) and disjunctions (viyoga) of planets (graha).

sanskrit: संज्ञा नक्षत्रवृन्दानां ज्ञेयाः सामान्यशास्त्रतः । एतच्छास्त्रानुसारेण राशिकीटफलं ब्रुवे ॥७॥
transliteration: saṃjñā nakṣatravṛndānāṃ jñeyāḥ sāmānyaśāstrataḥ . etacchāstrānusāreṇa rāśikīṭaphalaṃ bruve.
separated_sandhi: saṃjñāḥ nakṣatravṛndānām jñeyāḥ sāmānyaśāstrataḥetat śāstrānusāreṇa rāśikīṭaphalam bruve
english: The names of the constellations (nakṣatra) are to be known from general scriptures. Now, according to this text, I will state the results pertaining to the zodiacal signs (rāśi).

sanskrit: यस्मिन् काले यतः खेटा यान्ति दृग्गणितैकताम् । तत एव स्फुटाः कार्याः दिक्कालौ च स्फुटौ विद ॥८॥
transliteration: yasmin kāle yataḥ kheṭā yānti dṛggaṇitaikatām . tata eva sphuṭāḥ kāryāḥ dikkālau ca sphuṭau vida.
separated_sandhi: yasmin kāle yataḥ kheṭāḥ yānti dṛggaṇitaikatāmtataḥ eva sphuṭāḥ kāryāḥ dikkālau ca sphuṭau vid
english: At whatever time and from whatever (starting point) the planets (kheṭa) attain agreement between observation (dṛk) and calculation (gaṇita), then, O learned one (vid)! the accurate (sphuṭa) positions and times (dikkāla) should be determined.

sanskrit: स्वस्वदेशोद्भवैः साध्यं लग्नं राश्युदयैः स्फुटम् । अथादौ वच्मि खेटानां जातिरूपगुणानहम् ॥९॥
transliteration: svasvadeśodbhavaiḥ sādhyaṃ lagnaṃ rāśyudayaiḥ sphuṭam . athādau vacmi kheṭānāṃ jātirūpaguṇānaham.
separated_sandhi: svasvadeśodbhavaiḥ sādhyaṃ lagnaṃ rāśyudayaiḥsphuṭam atha ādau vacmi kheṭānām jātirūpaguṇān aham
english: The accurate (sphuṭa) Ascendant (Lagna) is to be determined by the risings of the zodiacal signs (rāśi) using calculations based on one's own country's (local) methods. Now, first, I (aham) will describe the caste (jāti), appearance (rūpa), and qualities (guṇa) of the planets (kheṭa).

sanskrit: अथ खेटा रविश्चन्द्रो मङ्गलश्च बुधस्तथा । गुरुः शुक्रः शनी राहुः केतुश्चैते यथाक्रमम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: atha kheṭā raviścandro maṅgalaśca budhastathā . guruḥ śukraḥ śanī rāhuḥ ketuścaite yathākramam.
separated_sandhi: atha kheṭāḥ raviḥ candraḥ maṅgalaḥ ca budhaḥ tathāguruḥ śukraḥ śanī rāhuḥ ketuḥ ca ete yathākramam
english: Now, the planets are, in order: the Sun, the Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu.

sanskrit: तत्रार्कशनिभूपुत्राः क्षीणेन्दुराहुकेतवः । क्रूराः शेषग्रहा सौम्याः क्रूरः क्रूरयुतो बुधः ॥११॥
transliteration: tatrārkaśanibhūputrāḥ kṣīṇendurāhuketavaḥ . krūrāḥ śeṣagrahā saumyāḥ krūraḥ krūrayuto budhaḥ.
separated_sandhi: tatra arkaśanibhūputrāḥ kṣīṇendurāhuketavaḥ krūrāḥśeṣagrahāḥ saumyāḥ krūraḥ krūrayutaḥ budhaḥ
english: Among these, the Sun, Saturn, Mars, the waning Moon, Rahu, and Ketu are considered malefic. The remaining planets are benefic. However, Mercury becomes malefic when conjoined with a malefic planet.

sanskrit: सर्वात्मा च दिवानाथो मनः कुमुदबान्धवः । सत्त्वं कुजो बुधैः प्रोक्तो बुधो वाणीप्रदायकः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sarvātmā ca divānātho manaḥ kumudabāndhavaḥ . sattvaṃ kujo budhaiḥ prokto budho vāṇīpradāyakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: sarvātmā ca divānāthaḥ manaḥ kumudabāndhavaḥsattvam kujaḥ budhaiḥ proktaḥ budhaḥ vāṇīpradāyakaḥ
english: The Sun is the soul of all (ātman) and the lord of the day. The Moon is the mind and the friend of water lilies. Mars is declared by the wise as courage (sattva). Mercury is the bestower of speech.

sanskrit: देवेज्यो ज्ञानसुखदो भृगुर्वीर्यप्रदयकः । ऋषिभिः प्राक्तनैः प्रोक्तश्छायासूनुश्च दुःखदः ॥१३॥
transliteration: devejyo jñānasukhado bhṛgurvīryapradayakaḥ . ṛṣibhiḥ prāktanaiḥ proktaśchāyāsūnuśca duḥkhadaḥ.
separated_sandhi: devejyaḥ jñānasukhadaḥ bhṛguḥ vīryapradāyakaḥṛṣibhiḥ prāktanaiḥ proktaḥ chāyāsūnuḥ ca duḥkhadaḥ
english: Jupiter (devejya) grants knowledge and happiness. Venus (Bhṛgu) bestows valor. Saturn, the son of Chaya (Chāyāsūnu), is declared by the ancient sages to be the giver of sorrow.

sanskrit: रविचन्द्रौ तु राजानौ नेता ज्ञेयो धरात्मजः । बुधो राजकुमारश्च सचिवौ गुरुभार्गवौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: ravicandrau tu rājānau netā jñeyo dharātmajaḥ . budho rājakumāraśca sacivau gurubhārgavau.
separated_sandhi: ravicandrau tu rājānau netā jñeyaḥ dharātmajaḥbudhaḥ rājakumāraḥ ca sacivau gurubhārgavau
english: The Sun and Moon are indeed the kings. Mars, the son of Earth (dharātmaja), should be known as the commander. Mercury is the prince, and Jupiter and Venus (Gurubhārgavau) are the two ministers.

sanskrit: प्रेष्यको रविपुत्रश्च सेना स्वर्भानुपुच्छकौ । एवं क्रमेण वै विप्र सूर्यादीन् प्रविचिन्तयेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: preṣyako raviputraśca senā svarbhānupucchakau . evaṃ krameṇa vai vipra sūryādīn pravicintayet.
separated_sandhi: preṣyakaḥ raviputraḥ ca senā svarbhānu-pucchakauevam krameṇa vai vipra sūryādīn pravicintayet
english: O Brahmin, one should contemplate the planets, starting with the Sun, in their proper order. Thus, the servant and son of the Sun (Saturn), Mars (Senā), and the two nodes, Rāhu and Ketu (Svarbhānu-Pucchakau), are to be considered in this sequence.

sanskrit: रक्तश्यामो दिवाधीशो गौरगात्रो निशाकरः । नात्युच्चाङ्गः कुजो रक्तो दूर्वाश्यामो बुधस्तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: raktaśyāmo divādhīśo gauragātro niśākaraḥ . nātyuccāṅgaḥ kujo rakto dūrvāśyāmo budhastathā.
separated_sandhi: raktaśyāmaḥ divādhīśaḥ gauragātraḥ niśākaraḥ naati-ucca-aṅgaḥ kujaḥ raktaḥ dūrvāśyāmaḥ budhaḥ tathā
english: The lord of the day (Sun) is reddish-dark. The maker of night (Moon) has a fair complexion. Mars (Kuja) is not very tall-bodied and is red. Similarly, Mercury is dark like "dūrvā" grass.

sanskrit: गौरगात्रो गुरुर्ज्ञेयः शुक्रः श्यावस्तथैव च । कृष्णदेहो रवेः पुत्रो ज्ञायते द्विजसत्तम ॥१७॥
transliteration: gauragātro gururjñeyaḥ śukraḥ śyāvastathaiva ca . kṛṣṇadeho raveḥ putro jñāyate dvijasattama.
separated_sandhi: gauragātraḥ guruḥ jñeyaḥ śukraḥ śyāvaḥ tathā evaca kṛṣṇadehaḥ raveḥ putraḥ jñāyate dvijasattama
english: Jupiter (Guru) should be known as fair-bodied. Venus (Śukra) is similarly dark brown. O best of Brahmins, the son of the Sun (Saturn) is known to have a dark body.

sanskrit: वह्न्यम्बुशिखिजा विष्णुविढौजः शचिका द्विज । सूर्यादीनां खगानां च देवा ज्ञेयाः क्रमेण च ॥१८॥
transliteration: vahnyambuśikhijā viṣṇuviḍhaujaḥ śacikā dvija . sūryādīnāṃ khagānāṃ ca devā jñeyāḥ krameṇa ca.
separated_sandhi: vahni ambu śikhija viṣṇu viḍaujaḥ śacikā dvijasūryādīnām khagānām ca devāḥ jñeyāḥ krameṇa ca
english: O Brahmin, Agni (fire), Varuṇa (water), Skanda (Śikhija), Viṣṇu, Indra (Viḍaujas), and Śacī are the deities to be known, in order, for the planets (khaga) beginning with the Sun.

sanskrit: क्लीवौ द्वौ सौम्यसौरी च युवतीन्दुभृगू द्विज । नराः शेषाश्च विज्ञेया भानुर्भौमो गुरुस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: klīvau dvau saumyasaurī ca yuvatīndubhṛgū dvija . narāḥ śeṣāśca vijñeyā bhānurbhaumo gurustathā.
separated_sandhi: klīvau dvau saumyasauraī ca yuvatī indubhṛgū dvijanarāḥ śeṣāḥ ca vijñeyāḥ bhānuḥ bhaumaḥ guruḥ tathā
english: O Brahmin, Mercury (Saumya) and Saturn (Sauri) are the two neuter planets. The Moon (Indu) and Venus (Bhṛgu) are the two female planets. The remaining ones - the Sun (Bhānu), Mars (Bhauma), and Jupiter (Guru) - should similarly be known as male.

sanskrit: अग्निभूमिनभस्तोयवायवः क्रमतो द्विज । भौमादीनां ग्रहाणां च तत्त्वानीति यथाक्रमम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: agnibhūminabhastoyavāyavaḥ kramato dvija . bhaumādīnāṃ grahāṇāṃ ca tattvānīti yathākramam.
separated_sandhi: agnibhūminabhastoyavāyavaḥ kramataḥ dvijabhaumādīnām grahāṇām ca tattvāni iti yathākramam
english: O twice-born (dvija), fire, earth, ether, water, and air are, respectively, the fundamental elements (tattva) associated with Mars and the other planets, in their due order.

sanskrit: गुरुशुक्रौ विप्रवर्णौ कुजार्कौ क्षत्रियौ द्विज । शशिसोम्यौ वैश्यवर्णौ शनिः शूद्रो द्विजोत्तम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: guruśukrau vipravarṇau kujārkau kṣatriyau dvija . śaśisomyau vaiśyavarṇau śaniḥ śūdro dvijottam.
separated_sandhi: guruśukrau vipravarṇau kujārkau kṣatriyau dvijaśaśisomyau vaiśyavarṇau śaniḥ śūdraḥ dvijottama
english: O twice-born (dvija), Jupiter and Venus are of the Brahmin (vipra) caste; Mars and the Sun are of the Kshatriya caste. O best among twice-born (dvijottama), the Moon and Mercury are of the Vaishya caste, and Saturn is of the Shudra caste.

sanskrit: जीवसूर्येन्द्रवः सत्त्वं बुधशुक्रौ रजस्तथा । सूर्यपुत्रभरापुत्रौ तमःप्रकृतिकौ द्विज ॥२२॥
transliteration: jīvasūryendravaḥ sattvaṃ budhaśukrau rajastathā . sūryaputrabharāputrau tamaḥprakṛtikau dvija.
separated_sandhi: jīvasūryendavaḥ sattvam budhaśukrau rajas tathāsūryaputrabharāputrau tamaḥprakṛtikau dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija), Jupiter, the Sun, and the Moon possess the quality of Sattva; Mercury and Venus possess Rajas; and Saturn (the Sun's son) and Mars (the Earth's son) are of Tamasic nature (prakṛti).

sanskrit: मधुपिङ्गलदृक्सूर्यश्चतुरस्रः शुचिर्द्विज । पित्तप्रकृतिको धीमान् पुमानल्पकचो द्विज ॥२३॥
transliteration: madhupiṅgaladṛksūryaścaturasraḥ śucirdvija . pittaprakṛtiko dhīmān pumānalpakaco dvija.
separated_sandhi: madhupiṅgaladṛksūryaḥ caturasraḥ śuciḥ dvijapittaprakṛtikaḥ dhīmān pumān alpakacaḥ dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija), the Sun (Sūrya) has honey-colored and tawny eyes, a square body, and is pure. He is intelligent, masculine, of Pitta constitution (prakṛti), and has sparse hair.

sanskrit: बहुवातकफः प्राज्ञश्चन्द्रो वृत्ततनुर्द्विज । शुभदृङ्मधुवाक्यश्च चञ्चलो मदनातुरः ॥२४॥
transliteration: bahuvātakaphaḥ prājñaścandro vṛttatanurdvija . śubhadṛṅmadhuvākyaśca cañcalo madanāturaḥ.
separated_sandhi: bahuvātakaphaḥ prājñaḥ candraḥ vṛttatanuḥ dvijaśubhadṛk madhuvākyaḥ ca cañcalaḥ madanāturaḥ
english: O twice-born (dvija), the Moon (Chandra) has an abundance of Vata and Kapha (humors), is wise, and has a round body. He has auspicious eyes, speaks sweetly, and is restless and lovesick.

sanskrit: क्रूरो रक्तेक्षणो भौमश्चपलोदारमूर्तिकः । पित्तप्रकृतिकः क्रोधी कृशमध्यतनुर्द्विज ॥२५॥
transliteration: krūro raktekṣaṇo bhaumaścapalodāramūrtikaḥ . pittaprakṛtikaḥ krodhī kṛśamadhyatanurdvija.
separated_sandhi: krūraḥ rakta-īkṣaṇaḥ bhaumaḥ capalaḥ udāra-mūrtikaḥpitta-prakṛtikaḥ krodhī kṛśa-madhya-tanuḥ dvija
english: O brahmin (dvija), Mars (bhaumaḥ) is cruel, red-eyed, fickle, and possesses a noble physique. He has a fiery (pitta) constitution (prakṛti), is prone to anger, and has a slender waist and body.

sanskrit: वपुःश्रेष्ठः श्लिष्टवाक्च ह्यतिहास्यरुचिर्बुधः । पित्तवान् कफवान् विप्र मारुतप्रकृतिस्तथा ॥२६॥
transliteration: vapuḥśreṣṭhaḥ śliṣṭavākca hyatihāsyarucirbudhaḥ . pittavān kaphavān vipra mārutaprakṛtistathā.
separated_sandhi: vapuḥ-śreṣṭhaḥ śliṣṭa-vāc ca hi ati-hāsya-ruciḥ budhaḥpitta-vat kapha-vat vipra maruta-prakṛtiḥ tathā
english: O brahmin (vipra), Mercury (budhaḥ) indeed possesses an excellent physique, is eloquent, and very fond of humor. Moreover, he has a pitta (fiery) and kapha (phlegmatic) dosha, and a vāta (maruta) constitution (prakṛti).

sanskrit: बृहद्गात्रो गुरुश्चैव पिङ्गलो मूर्द्धजेक्षणे । कफप्रकृतिको धीमान् सर्वशास्त्रविशारदः ॥२७॥
transliteration: bṛhadgātro guruścaiva piṅgalo mūrddhajekṣaṇe . kaphaprakṛtiko dhīmān sarvaśāstraviśāradaḥ.
separated_sandhi: bṛhat-gātraḥ guruḥ ca eva piṅgalaḥ mūrdhaja-īkṣaṇekapha-prakṛtikaḥ dhīmat sarva-śāstra-viśāradaḥ
english: Jupiter (guruḥ) has a large physique and is indeed tawny in his hair and eyes. He has a kapha (phlegmatic) constitution (prakṛti), is intelligent, and an expert in all scriptures.

sanskrit: सुखि कान्तवपु श्रेष्ठः सुलोचनो भृगोः सुतः । काव्यकर्ता कफाधिक्योऽनिलात्मा वक्रमूर्धजः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sukhi kāntavapu śreṣṭhaḥ sulocano bhṛgoḥ sutaḥ . kāvyakartā kaphādhikyo'nilātmā vakramūrdhajaḥ.
separated_sandhi: sukhin kānta-vapus śreṣṭhaḥ su-locanaḥ bhṛgoḥ sutaḥkāvya-kartṛ kapha-ādhikyaḥ anila-ātman vakra-mūrdhajaḥ
english: Venus (bhṛgoḥ sutaḥ) is happy, possesses a charming and excellent physique, and has beautiful eyes. He is a composer of poetry, has an excess of kapha (phlegm), a vāta (anila) constitution (ātman), and curly hair.

sanskrit: कृश्दीर्घतनुः शौरिः पिङ्गदृष्ट्यनिलात्मकः । स्थूलदन्तोऽलसः पंगुः खररोमकचो द्विज ॥२९॥
transliteration: kṛśdīrghatanuḥ śauriḥ piṅgadṛṣṭyanilātmakaḥ . sthūladanto'lasaḥ paṃguḥ khararomakaco dvija.
separated_sandhi: kṛśa-dīrgha-tanuḥ śauriḥ piṅga-dṛṣṭi-anila-ātmakaḥsthūla-dantaḥ alasaḥ paṅguḥ khara-roman-kacaḥ dvija
english: O brahmin (dvija), Saturn (śauriḥ) has a lean and tall body, tawny eyes, and a vāta (anila) constitution (ātman). He has large teeth, is lazy, lame, and has rough body and head hair.

sanskrit: धूम्राकारो नीलतनुर्वनस्थोऽपि भयंकरः । वातप्रकृतिको धीमान् स्वर्भानुस्तत्समः शिखी ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhūmrākāro nīlatanurvanastho'pi bhayaṃkaraḥ . vātaprakṛtiko dhīmān svarbhānustatsamaḥ śikhī.
separated_sandhi: dhūmrākāraḥ nīlatanuḥ vanasthaḥ api bhayaṃkaraḥvātapr̥kṛtikaḥ dhīmān svarbhānuḥ tatsamaḥ śikhī
english: Ketu (śikhī) has a smoky appearance and a blue body. Even when residing in the forest, he is formidable. He possesses a vāta (vāta) constitution and is intelligent; he is similar to Svarbhānu (Rāhu).

sanskrit: अस्थि रक्तस्तथा मज्जा त्वग् वसा वीर्यमेव च । स्नायुरेषामधीशाश्च क्रमात् सूर्यादयो द्विज ॥३१॥
transliteration: asthi raktastathā majjā tvag vasā vīryameva ca . snāyureṣāmadhīśāśca kramāt sūryādayo dvija.
separated_sandhi: asthi raktaḥ tathā majjā tvak vasā vīryam eva casnāyuḥ eṣām adhīśāḥ ca kramāt sūryādayaḥ dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija)! The Sun and other planets (sūryādayaḥ) are, in respective order (kramāt), the lords of bone, blood, marrow, skin, fat, semen (vīrya), and sinew.

sanskrit: देवालयजलं वह्निक्रीडादीनां तथैव च । कोशशय्योत्कराणान्तु नाथां सूर्यादयः क्रमात् ॥३२॥
transliteration: devālayajalaṃ vahnikrīḍādīnāṃ tathaiva ca . kośaśayyotkarāṇāntu nāthāṃ sūryādayaḥ kramāt.
separated_sandhi: devālaya-jalam vahni-krīḍā-ādīnām tathā eva cakośa-śayyā-utkarāṇām tu nāthāḥ sūrya-ādayaḥ kramāt
english: The Sun and other planets (sūryādayaḥ), in order (kramāt), are the lords of temple water; of fire-games and other such activities; and similarly (tathaiva ca), of treasury, beds, and heaps of goods.

sanskrit: अयनक्षणवारर्तुमासपक्षसमा द्विज । सूर्यादीनां क्रमाज्ज्ञेया निर्विशंकं द्विजोत्तम ॥३३॥
transliteration: ayanakṣaṇavārartumāsapakṣasamā dvija . sūryādīnāṃ kramājjñeyā nirviśaṃkaṃ dvijottama.
separated_sandhi: ayana kṣaṇa vāra ṛtu māsa pakṣa samā dvijasūryādīnām kramāt jñeyā nirviśaṅkam dvijottama
english: O twice-born (dvija), O best of twice-born (dvijottama)! The Ayana (half-year), kṣaṇa (moment), day of the week (vāra), season (ṛtu), month (māsa), fortnight (pakṣa), and year (samā) should be known, without any doubt, to correspond respectively to the Sun and other planets (sūryādayaḥ).

sanskrit: कटुक्षारतिक्तमिश्रमधुराम्लकषायकाः । क्रमेण सर्वे विज्ञेयाः सूर्यादीनां रसा इति ॥३४॥
transliteration: kaṭukṣāratiktamiśramadhurāmlakaṣāyakāḥ . krameṇa sarve vijñeyāḥ sūryādīnāṃ rasā iti.
separated_sandhi: kaṭu kṣāra tikta miśra madhura amla kaṣāyakāḥkrameṇa sarve vijñeyāḥ sūryādīnām rasāḥ iti
english: Pungent (kaṭu), saline (kṣāra), bitter (tikta), mixed (miśra), sweet (madhura), sour (amla), and astringent (kaṣāya) – these are declared as the tastes (rasāḥ). All of them should be known to belong to the Sun and other planets (sūryādayaḥ) in their respective order.

sanskrit: बुधेज्यौ बलिनौ पूर्वे रविभौमौ च दक्षिणे । पश्चिमे सूर्यपुत्रश्च सितचन्द्रौ तथोत्तरे ॥३५॥
transliteration: budhejyau balinau pūrve ravibhaumau ca dakṣiṇe . paścime sūryaputraśca sitacandrau tathottare.
separated_sandhi: budhejyaU balinau pūrve ravibhaumau ca dakṣiṇepaścime sūryaputraḥ ca sitacandrau tathā uttare
english: Mercury and Jupiter are strong in the east. The Sun and Mars are strong in the south. Saturn (sūryaputra) is strong in the west, and similarly, Venus and the Moon are strong in the north.

sanskrit: निशायां बलिनश्चन्द्रकुजसौरा भवन्ति हि । सर्वदा ज्ञो बली ज्ञेयो दिने शेषा द्विजोत्तम ॥३६॥
transliteration: niśāyāṃ balinaścandrakujasaurā bhavanti hi . sarvadā jño balī jñeyo dine śeṣā dvijottama.
separated_sandhi: niśāyām balinaḥ candrakujasaurāḥ bhavanti hisarvadā jñaḥ balī jñeyaḥ dine śeṣāḥ dvijottama
english: Indeed, the Moon, Mars, and Saturn are strong at night. O best among the twice-born (dvija), Mercury (jña) should be known to be always strong, while the remaining planets are strong during the day.

sanskrit: कृष्णे च बलिनः क्रूराः सौम्या वीर्ययुताः सिते । सौम्यायने सौम्यखेटो बली याम्यायनेऽपरः ॥३७॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇe ca balinaḥ krūrāḥ saumyā vīryayutāḥ site . saumyāyane saumyakheṭo balī yāmyāyane'paraḥ.
separated_sandhi: kṛṣṇe ca balinaḥ krūrāḥ saumyāḥ vīryayutāḥ sitesaumyāyane saumyakheṭaḥ balī yāmyāyane aparaḥ
english: The malefic planets are strong during the dark half of the lunar month (kṛṣṇa), and the benefic planets are powerful during the bright half (sita). A benefic planet is strong during the Sun's northern course (saumyāyana), while a malefic one is strong during its southern course (yāmyāyana).

sanskrit: वर्षमासाहहोराणां पतयो बलिनस्तथा । शमंबुगुशुचंराद्या वृद्धितो वीर्यवत्तरः ॥३८॥
transliteration: varṣamāsāhahorāṇāṃ patayo balinastathā . śamaṃbuguśucaṃrādyā vṛddhito vīryavattaraḥ.
separated_sandhi: varṣamāsāhhorāṇām patayaḥ balinaḥ tathāśamaṃbuguśucaṃrādyāḥ vṛddhitaḥ vīryavattarāḥ
english: Similarly, the lords of the year, month, day, and hora (hourly division) are strong. The planets, beginning with Saturn (śani), Mars (maṅgala), Mercury (budha), Jupiter (guru), Venus (śukra), Moon (candra), and Sun (ravi), become successively more powerful in that increasing order.

sanskrit: सूर्ये जनयति स्थूलान् दुर्भगान् सूर्यपुत्रकः । क्षीरोपेतांस्तथा चन्द्रः कटुकाद्यान् धरासुतः ॥३९॥
transliteration: sūrye janayati sthūlān durbhagān sūryaputrakaḥ . kṣīropetāṃstathā candraḥ kaṭukādyān dharāsutaḥ.
separated_sandhi: sūrye janayati sthūlān durbhagān sūryaputrakaḥkṣīra upetān tathā candraḥ kaṭuka ādyān dharāsutaḥ
english: The Sun produces corpulent and unfortunate individuals. Saturn (sūryaputraka) also causes them. Similarly, the Moon generates those endowed with milk, and Mars (dharāsuta) causes those associated with pungent and other bitter tastes.

sanskrit: पुष्पवृक्षं भृगोः पुत्रे गुरुज्ञौ सफलाफलौ । नीरसान् सूर्यपुत्रश्च एवं ज्ञेयाः खगा द्विज ॥४०॥
transliteration: puṣpavṛkṣaṃ bhṛgoḥ putre gurujñau saphalāphalau . nīrasān sūryaputraśca evaṃ jñeyāḥ khagā dvija.
separated_sandhi: puṣpavṛkṣam bhṛgoḥ putre gurujñau saphalāphalaunīrasān sūryaputraḥ ca evam jñeyāḥ khagā dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija), the son of Bhṛgu (Venus) is associated with flowering trees. Jupiter and Mercury are known to yield both fruitful and fruitless results. The son of Sūrya (Saturn) is considered unproductive. These planets should be understood in this manner.

sanskrit: राहुश्चाण्डालजातिश्च केतुर्जात्यन्तरस्तथा । शिखिस्वर्भानुमन्दानां वल्मीकः स्थानमुच्यते ॥४१॥
transliteration: rāhuścāṇḍālajātiśca keturjātyantarastathā . śikhisvarbhānumandānāṃ valmīkaḥ sthānamucyate.
separated_sandhi: rāhuḥ caṇḍālajātiḥ ca ketuḥ jātyantaraḥ tathāśikhisvarbhānumandānām valmīkaḥ sthānam ucyate
english: Rāhu is of the Caṇḍāla caste, and Ketu is similarly of a different caste. The anthill (valmīka) is said to be the abode (sthāna) of Ketu (śikhin), Rāhu (svarbhānu), and Saturn (manda).

sanskrit: चित्रकन्था फनीन्द्रस्य केतुश्छिद्रयुतो द्विज! । सीसं रहोर्नीलमणिः केतोर्ज्ञेयो द्विजोत्तम ॥४२॥
transliteration: citrakanthā phanīndrasya ketuśchidrayuto dvija! . sīsaṃ rahornīlamaṇiḥ ketorjñeyo dvijottama.
separated_sandhi: citrakanthā phanīndrasya ketuḥ chidrayutaḥ dvijasīsam rahoḥ nīlamaṇiḥ ketoḥ jñeyaḥ dvijottama
english: A variegated garment is associated with the lord of snakes (Rāhu). Ketu, O twice-born (dvija), is characterized by holes or perforations. Lead is known to be the metal of Rāhu, and a sapphire (nīlamaṇi) is known to be the gem of Ketu, O best of twice-born (dvijottama).

sanskrit: गुरोः पीताम्बरं विप्र भृगोः क्षौमं तथैव च । रक्तक्षौमं भास्करस्य इन्दोः क्षौमं सितं द्विज ॥४३॥
transliteration: guroḥ pītāmbaraṃ vipra bhṛgoḥ kṣaumaṃ tathaiva ca . raktakṣaumaṃ bhāskarasya indoḥ kṣaumaṃ sitaṃ dvija.
separated_sandhi: guroḥ pītāmbaram vipra bhṛgoḥ kṣaumam tathā eva caraktakṣaumam bhāskarasya indoḥ kṣaumam sitam dvija
english: O brahmin (vipra), a yellow garment (pītāmbara) is associated with Jupiter. Similarly, a silk garment is associated with Venus. A red silk garment is associated with the Sun, and a white silk garment is associated with the Moon, O twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: बुधस्य कृष्णक्षौमं तु रक्तवस्त्रं कुजस्य च । वस्त्रं चित्रं शनेर्विप्र पट्तवस्त्रं तथैव च ॥४४॥
transliteration: budhasya kṛṣṇakṣaumaṃ tu raktavastraṃ kujasya ca . vastraṃ citraṃ śanervipra paṭtavastraṃ tathaiva ca.
separated_sandhi: budhasya kṛṣṇakṣaumam tu raktavastram kujasya cavastram citram śaneḥ vipra paṭṭavastram tathā eva ca
english: But a black silk garment (kṛṣṇakṣauma) is for Mercury, and a red garment (raktavastra) is for Mars. O brahmin (vipra), a variegated garment and similarly a fine silk cloth (paṭṭavastra) are prescribed for Saturn.

sanskrit: भृगोरृतुर्वसन्तश्च कुजभान्वोश्च ग्रीष्मकः । चन्द्रस्य वर्षा विज्ञेया शरच्चैव तथा विदः ॥४५॥
transliteration: bhṛgorṛturvasantaśca kujabhānvośca grīṣmakaḥ . candrasya varṣā vijñeyā śaraccaiva tathā vidaḥ.
separated_sandhi: bhṛgoḥ ṛtuḥ vasantaḥ ca kujabhānvoh ca grīṣmakaḥcandrasya varṣā vijñeyā śarat ca eva tathā vidaḥ
english: Spring is attributed to Venus. Summer belongs to Mars and the Sun. Similarly, the rainy season and autumn should be understood as belonging to the Moon, according to the wise.

sanskrit: हेमन्तोऽपि गुरोर्ज्ञेयः शनेस्तु शिशिरो द्विज । अष्टौ मासाश्च स्वर्भानोः केतोर्मासत्रयं द्विज ॥४६॥
transliteration: hemanto'pi gurorjñeyaḥ śanestu śiśiro dvija . aṣṭau māsāśca svarbhānoḥ ketormāsatrayaṃ dvija.
separated_sandhi: hemantaḥ api guroḥ jñeyaḥ śaneḥ tu śiśiraḥ dvijaaṣṭau māsāḥ ca svarbhānoḥ ketoḥ māsatrayam dvija
english: Winter (hemanta) should be known as belonging to Jupiter. But late winter (śiśira) belongs to Saturn, O twice-born (dvija). Furthermore, eight months are attributed to Rahu (svarbhānu), and three months to Ketu, O twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: राह्वारपंगुचन्द्रश्च विज्ञेया धातुखेचराः । मूलग्रहौ सूर्यशुक्रौ अपरा जीवसंज्ञकाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: rāhvārapaṃgucandraśca vijñeyā dhātukhecarāḥ . mūlagrahau sūryaśukrau aparā jīvasaṃjñakāḥ.
separated_sandhi: rāhu āra paṅgu candraḥ ca vijñeyāḥ dhātukhecarāḥmūlagrahau sūryaśukrau aparāḥ jīvasaṃjñakāḥ
english: Rahu, Mars, Saturn, and the Moon are to be understood as the celestial bodies (khecara) signifying minerals and metals (dhātu). The Sun and Venus are considered the root-signifying planets (mūla). The remaining planets are designated as those signifying living beings (jīva).

sanskrit: ग्रहेषु मन्दो वृद्धोऽस्ति आयुर्वृद्धिप्रदायकः । नैसर्गिके बहुसमान् ददाति द्विजसत्तम ॥४८॥
transliteration: graheṣu mando vṛddho'sti āyurvṛddhipradāyakaḥ . naisargike bahusamān dadāti dvijasattama.
separated_sandhi: graheṣu mandaḥ vṛddhaḥ asti āyurvṛddhipradāyakaḥnaisargike bahusamān dadāti dvijasattama
english: Among the planets, Saturn (manda) is the oldest and bestower of increased longevity. In the natural dasha system, it grants many years, O best among the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: मेषो वृषो मृगः कन्या कर्को मीनस्तथा तुला । सूर्यादीनां क्रमादेते कथिता उच्चराश्यः ॥४९॥
transliteration: meṣo vṛṣo mṛgaḥ kanyā karko mīnastathā tulā . sūryādīnāṃ kramādete kathitā uccarāśyaḥ.
separated_sandhi: meṣaḥ vṛṣaḥ mṛgaḥ kanyā karkaḥ mīnaḥ tathā tulāsūryādīnām kramāt ete kathitāḥ uccarāśayaḥ
english: Aries, Taurus, Capricorn, Virgo, Cancer, Pisces, and Libra – these are declared, in sequence, to be the exaltation (ucca) signs for the Sun and the other planets.

sanskrit: भागा दश त्रयोऽष्टाश्व्यस्तिथ्योऽक्षा भमिता नखाः । उच्चात् सप्तमभं नीचं तैरेवांशैः प्रकीर्तितम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: bhāgā daśa trayo'ṣṭāśvyastithyo'kṣā bhamitā nakhāḥ . uccāt saptamabhaṃ nīcaṃ tairevāṃśaiḥ prakīrtitam.
separated_sandhi: bhāgāḥ daśa trayaḥ aṣṭāśvyaḥ tithayaḥ akṣāḥ bhamitāḥ nakhāḥ| uccāt saptamabham nīcam taiḥ eva aṃśaiḥ prakīrtitam
english: The degrees for (planetary) exaltation (ucca) are declared as: ten (for the Sun), three (for the Moon), twenty-eight (for Mars), fifteen (for Mercury), five (for Jupiter), nine (for Venus), and twenty (for Saturn). The seventh sign from the exaltation sign is declared as the debilitation (nīca) sign, with the very same degrees (within that sign).

sanskrit: रवेः सिम्हे नखांशाश्च त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् । उच्चमिन्दोर्वृषे त्र्यंशास्त्रिकोणमपरेंऽशकाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: raveḥ simhe nakhāṃśāśca trikoṇamapare svabham . uccamindorvṛṣe tryaṃśāstrikoṇamapareṃ'śakāḥ.
separated_sandhi: raveḥ siṃhe nakhāṃśāḥ ca trikoṇam apare svabham |uccam indoḥ vṛṣe tryaṃśāḥ trikoṇam apare aṃśakāḥ
english: For the Sun, twenty degrees (nakhāṃśāḥ) in Leo (siṃhe) are its mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham). For the Moon, its exaltation (ucca) is in Taurus (vṛṣe) at three degrees (tryaṃśāḥ), and the remaining degrees (aṃśakāḥ) (in Taurus) are its mūlatrikoṇa.

sanskrit: मेषेऽर्कांशास्तु भौमस्य त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् । उच्चं बुधस्य कन्यायामुक्तं पञ्चदशांशकाः ॥५२॥
transliteration: meṣe'rkāṃśāstu bhaumasya trikoṇamapare svabham . uccaṃ budhasya kanyāyāmuktaṃ pañcadaśāṃśakāḥ.
separated_sandhi: meṣe arkāṃśāḥ tu bhaumasya trikoṇam apare svabham| uccam budhasya kanyāyām uktam pañcadaśāṃśakāḥ
english: But for Mars, twelve degrees (arkāṃśāḥ) in Aries (meṣe) are its mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham). For Mercury, exaltation (ucca) is declared to be in Virgo (kanyāyām) at fifteen degrees (pañcadaśāṃśakāḥ).

sanskrit: ततः पञ्चांशकाः प्रोक्तं त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् । चापे दशांशा जीवस्य त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ pañcāṃśakāḥ proktaṃ trikoṇamapare svabham . cāpe daśāṃśā jīvasya trikoṇamapare svabham.
separated_sandhi: tataḥ pañcāṃśakāḥ proktam trikoṇam apare svabham| cāpe daśāṃśāḥ jīvasya trikoṇam apare svabham
english: Thereafter, five degrees (pañcāṃśakāḥ) (in Virgo) are declared as (Mercury's) mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham). For Jupiter, ten degrees (daśāṃśāḥ) in Sagittarius (cāpe) are its mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham).

sanskrit: तुले शुक्रस्य तिथ्यंशास्त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् । शनेः कुम्भे नखांशाश्च त्रिकोणमपरे स्वभम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: tule śukrasya tithyaṃśāstrikoṇamapare svabham . śaneḥ kumbhe nakhāṃśāśca trikoṇamapare svabham.
separated_sandhi: tule śukrasya tithyaṃśāḥ trikoṇam apare svabham |śaneḥ kumbhe nakhāṃśāḥ ca trikoṇam apare svabham
english: For Venus, fifteen degrees (tithyaṃśāḥ) in Libra (tule) are its mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham). And for Saturn, twenty degrees (nakhāṃśāḥ) in Aquarius (kumbhe) are its mūlatrikoṇa. The remaining (degrees of the sign) are its own house (svabham).

sanskrit: त्रिकोणात् स्वात्सुखस्वाऽन्त्यधीधर्मायुःस्वतुङ्गपाः । सुहृदो रिपवश्वान्वे समाश्चोभयलक्षणाः ॥५५॥
transliteration: trikoṇāt svātsukhasvā'ntyadhīdharmāyuḥsvatuṅgapāḥ . suhṛdo ripavaśvānve samāścobhayalakṣaṇāḥ.
separated_sandhi: trikoṇāt svāt sukha sva antya dhī dharma āyuḥ sva tuṅgapāḥ suhṛdaḥ ripavaḥ ca anye samāḥ ca ubhaya lakṣaṇāḥ
english: The lords of the trine houses (5th and 9th), one's own house (1st), and the 2nd, 4th, 8th, and 12th houses from a planet, as well as the lord of its exaltation sign, are considered friends. The lords of the remaining houses are enemies, while others are neutrals possessing characteristics of both.

sanskrit: दशवन्ध्वायसहजस्वान्त्यस्थास्तु परस्परम् । तत्काले मित्रतां यान्ति रिपवोऽन्यत्र संस्थिताः ॥५६॥
transliteration: daśavandhvāyasahajasvāntyasthāstu parasparam . tatkāle mitratāṃ yānti ripavo'nyatra saṃsthitāḥ.
separated_sandhi: daśa bandhu āya sahaja sva antya sthāḥ tu parasparamtatkāle mitratām yānti ripavaḥ anyatra saṃsthitāḥ
english: Planets situated in the 10th, 4th, 11th, 3rd, 2nd, and 12th houses from a particular planet become temporary friends with it. Planets located in other houses become its temporary enemies.

sanskrit: तत्काले च निसर्गे च मित्रं चेदधिमित्रकम् । मित्रं मित्रसमत्वे तु शत्रुः शत्रुसमत्वके ॥५७॥
transliteration: tatkāle ca nisarge ca mitraṃ cedadhimitrakam . mitraṃ mitrasamatve tu śatruḥ śatrusamatvake.
separated_sandhi: tatkāle ca nisarge ca mitram cet adhimitrakammitram mitra samatve tu śatruḥ śatru samatvake
english: If a planet is a friend both naturally and temporarily, it is considered a great friend (adhimitrakam). If it is a friend in one context and neutral in the other, it is a friend. If it is an enemy in one context and neutral in the other, it is an enemy.

sanskrit: समो मित्ररिपुत्वे तु शत्रुत्वे त्वधिशत्रुता । एवं विविच्य दैवज्ञो जातकस्य फलं वदेत् ॥५८॥
transliteration: samo mitrariputve tu śatrutve tvadhiśatrutā . evaṃ vivicya daivajño jātakasya phalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: samaḥ mitra riputve tu śatrutve tu adhiśatrutāevam vivicya daivajñaḥ jātaka sya phalam vadet
english: If a planet is a friend in one context and an enemy in the other, it is considered neutral. If it is an enemy both naturally and temporarily, it is a great enemy (adhiśatrutā). Thus, an astrologer (daivajña) should analyze these (relationships) and declare the results for a native's (jātaka) horoscope.

sanskrit: स्वोच्चे शुभं फलं पूर्ण त्रिकोणे पादवर्जितम् । स्वर्क्षेऽर्धं मित्रगेहे तु पादमात्रं प्रकीर्तितम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: svocce śubhaṃ phalaṃ pūrṇa trikoṇe pādavarjitam . svarkṣe'rdhaṃ mitragehe tu pādamātraṃ prakīrtitam.
separated_sandhi: sva ucce śubham phalam pūrṇam trikoṇe pāda varjitamsva ṛkṣe ardham mitra gehe tu pāda mātram prakīrtitam
english: When a planet is in its sign of exaltation, it gives full auspicious results. In a trine house, it yields results reduced by one quarter (i.e., three-quarters). In its own sign, it gives half the results. However, when in a friend's house, only one-quarter of the results are declared.

sanskrit: पादार्धं समभे प्रोक्तं शून्यं नीचास्तशत्रुभे । तद्वद्दुष्टफलं ब्रूयद् व्यत्ययेन विचक्षणः ॥६०॥
transliteration: pādārdhaṃ samabhe proktaṃ śūnyaṃ nīcāstaśatrubhe . tadvadduṣṭaphalaṃ brūyad vyatyayena vicakṣaṇaḥ.
separated_sandhi: pādārdham samabhe proktam śūnyam nīcāstaśatrubhetadvat duṣṭaphalam brūyāt vyatyayena vicakṣaṇaḥ
english: Half strength is declared for a planet in a neutral sign, and zero strength when it is debilitated, combust, or located in an enemy's sign. An expert should similarly declare malefic results based on these conditions, and conversely, auspicious results.

sanskrit: त्र्यंशाढ्यविश्वभागैश्च चतुर्भैः सहितो रविः । धूमो नाम महादोषः सर्वकर्मविनाशकः ॥६१॥
transliteration: tryaṃśāḍhyaviśvabhāgaiśca caturbhaiḥ sahito raviḥ . dhūmo nāma mahādoṣaḥ sarvakarmavināśakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: tryaṃśāḍhyaviśvabhāgaiḥ ca caturbhaiḥ sahitaḥraviḥ dhūmaḥ nāma mahādoṣaḥ sarvakarmavināśakaḥ
english: The Sun, when joined with four signs, thirteen degrees, and twenty minutes, is called Dhūma, a great affliction that destroys all undertakings (karma).

sanskrit: धूमो मण्डलतः शुद्धो व्यतीपातोऽत्र दोषदः । सषद्भोऽत्र व्यतीपातः परिवेषोऽतिदोषकृत् ॥६२॥
transliteration: dhūmo maṇḍalataḥ śuddho vyatīpāto'tra doṣadaḥ . saṣadbho'tra vyatīpātaḥ pariveṣo'tidoṣakṛt.
separated_sandhi: dhūmaḥ maṇḍalataḥ śuddhaḥ vyatīpātaḥ atra doṣadaḥsaṣaḍbhaḥ atra vyatīpātaḥ pariveṣaḥ atidoṣakṛt
english: Dhūma is calculated from the Sun's longitude. Vyātipāta (360° - Dhūma) is a malefic point. The Vyātipāta, when increased by six signs (180 degrees), becomes Pariveṣa, which is exceedingly malefic.

sanskrit: परिवेषश्च्युतश्चक्रादिन्द्रचापस्तु दोषदः । वित्र्यंशास्यष्टिभागाध्यश्चापः केतुखगोऽशुभः ॥६३॥
transliteration: pariveṣaścyutaścakrādindracāpastu doṣadaḥ . vitryaṃśāsyaṣṭibhāgādhyaścāpaḥ ketukhago'śubhaḥ.
separated_sandhi: pariveṣaḥ cyutaḥ cakrāt indracāpaḥ tu doṣadaḥvitryaṃśāsyaṣṭibhāgādhyaḥ cāpaḥ ketukhagaḥ aśubhaḥ
english: Indracāpa is obtained when Pariveṣa is subtracted by 180 degrees. This Indracāpa is malefic. The planet Ketu, which is an arc endowed with divisions of three parts and eight degrees, is inauspicious.

sanskrit: एकराशियुतः केतुः सूर्यतुल्यः प्रजायते । अप्रकाशग्रहाश्चैते पापा दोषप्रदाः स्मृताः ॥६४॥
transliteration: ekarāśiyutaḥ ketuḥ sūryatulyaḥ prajāyate . aprakāśagrahāścaite pāpā doṣapradāḥ smṛtāḥ.
separated_sandhi: ekarāśiyutaḥ ketuḥ sūryatulyaḥ prajāyateaprakāśagrahāḥ ca ete pāpāḥ doṣapradāḥ smṛtāḥ
english: When Ketu (the upagraha) is associated with a single sign, it becomes comparable in influence to the Sun. Indeed, these non-luminous planets are all considered malefic and givers of afflictions.

sanskrit: सूर्येन्दुलग्नगेष्वेषु वंशायुर्ज्ञाननाशनम् । इति धूमादिदोषाणां स्थितिः पद्मासनोदिता ॥६५॥
transliteration: sūryendulagnageṣveṣu vaṃśāyurjñānanāśanam . iti dhūmādidoṣāṇāṃ sthitiḥ padmāsanoditā.
separated_sandhi: sūryendulagnageṣu eṣu vaṃśāyurjñānanāśanamiti dhūmādidoṣāṇām sthitiḥ padmāsanoditā
english: When these defects are present in the Sun, Moon, or Ascendant, they cause the destruction of lineage, lifespan, and knowledge. Such is the nature of the defects like Dhūma, as declared by Brahmā.

sanskrit: रविवारादिशन्यन्तं गुलिकादि निरूप्यते । दिवसानष्टधा भक्त्वा वारेशाद् गणेयत् क्रमात् ॥६६॥
transliteration: ravivārādiśanyantaṃ gulikādi nirūpyate . divasānaṣṭadhā bhaktvā vāreśād gaṇeyat kramāt.
separated_sandhi: ravivārādiśanyantam gulikādi nirūpyatedivasān aṣṭadhā bhaktvā vāreśāt gaṇayet kramāt
english: From Sunday onwards up to Saturday, Gulika and other malefic points are determined. One should divide the days into eight parts and count them sequentially from the lord of the day.

sanskrit: अष्ट्मोंऽशो निरीशः स्याच्छन्यंशो गुलिकःस्मृतः । रात्रिमप्यष्टधा कृत्वा वारेशात् पञ्चमादितः ॥६७॥
transliteration: aṣṭmoṃ'śo nirīśaḥ syācchanyaṃśo gulikaḥsmṛtaḥ . rātrimapyaṣṭadhā kṛtvā vāreśāt pañcamāditaḥ.
separated_sandhi: aṣṭamaḥ aṃśaḥ nirīśaḥ syāt śanyaṃśaḥ gulikaḥ smṛtaḥrātrim api aṣṭadhā kṛtvā vāreśāt pañcamāditaḥ
english: The eighth portion is considered lordless. The portion belonging to Saturn is called Gulika. Dividing the night also into eight parts, one should count from the fifth [planet] from the lord of the day.

sanskrit: गणयेदष्टमः खण्डो निष्यतिः परिकीर्तितः । श्न्यंशो गुलिकः प्रोक्तो रव्यंशः कालसंज्ञकः ॥६८॥
transliteration: gaṇayedaṣṭamaḥ khaṇḍo niṣyatiḥ parikīrtitaḥ . śnyaṃśo gulikaḥ prokto ravyaṃśaḥ kālasaṃjñakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: aṣṭamaḥ khaṇḍaḥ niṣyatiḥ parikīrtitaḥ śanyaṃśaḥgulikaḥ proktaḥ ravyaṃśaḥ kālasaṃjñakaḥ
english: The eighth segment is declared to be Niṣyati. The portion of Saturn is said to be Gulika, and the portion of the Sun is named Kāla.

sanskrit: भौमांशो मृत्युरादिष्टो गुर्वंशो यमघण्ट्कः । सोम्यांशोऽर्धप्रहरकः स्वस्वदेशोद्भवः स्फुटः ॥६९॥
transliteration: bhaumāṃśo mṛtyurādiṣṭo gurvaṃśo yamaghaṇṭkaḥ . somyāṃśo'rdhapraharakaḥ svasvadeśodbhavaḥ sphuṭaḥ.
separated_sandhi: bhaumāṃśaḥ mṛtyuḥ ādiṣṭaḥ gurvaṃśaḥ yamaghaṇṭakaḥsaumyāṃśaḥ ardhapraharakaḥ svasvadeśodbhavaḥ sphuṭaḥ
english: The portion of Mars is declared to be Mṛtyu. The portion of Jupiter is Yamaghaṇṭaka. The portion of Mercury is Ardhapraharaka. These are clearly reckoned according to one's own country or region.

sanskrit: गुलिकेष्टवशाल्लग्नं स्फुटं यत् स्वस्वदेशजम् । गुलिकं प्रोच्यते तस्माज्जातकस्य फलं वदेत् ॥७०॥
transliteration: gulikeṣṭavaśāllagnaṃ sphuṭaṃ yat svasvadeśajam . gulikaṃ procyate tasmājjātakasya phalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: gulikeṣṭavaśāt lagnam sphuṭam yat svasvadeśajamgulikam procyate tasmāt jātakasya phalam vadet
english: That precise ascendant (lagna) which is derived according to the local calculation (svasvadeśajam) based on the specific (iṣṭa) Gulika time, is therefore called Gulika (Maandi Lagna). From this, one should declare the results for the native (jātaka).

sanskrit: भांशपादसमैः प्राणैश्चराद्यर्कत्रिकोणभात् । उदयादिष्टकालान्तं यद्भं प्राणपदं हि तत् ॥७१॥
transliteration: bhāṃśapādasamaiḥ prāṇaiścarādyarkatrikoṇabhāt . udayādiṣṭakālāntaṃ yadbhaṃ prāṇapadaṃ hi tat.
separated_sandhi: bhāṃśapādasamaiḥ prāṇaiḥ carādyarkatrikoṇabhātudayāt iṣṭakālāntam yat bham prāṇapadam hi tat
english: The sign (bha) which is reached by counting "prāṇa" units (time units), equivalent to the quarters of degrees of signs (bhāṃśapāda), starting from the sign in trine to the Sun (considering movable, fixed, and dual signs, "carādi"), from the time of sunrise up to the desired moment (iṣṭakāla), is indeed the Prāṇapada.

sanskrit: स्वेष्टकालं पलीकृत्य तिथ्याप्तं भादिकं च यत् । चरागद्विभसंस्थेऽर्के भनौ युङ् नवमे सुते ॥७२॥
transliteration: sveṣṭakālaṃ palīkṛtya tithyāptaṃ bhādikaṃ ca yat . carāgadvibhasaṃsthe'rke bhanau yuṅ navame sute.
separated_sandhi: sveṣṭakālam palīkṛtya tithyāptam bhādikam ca yatcarāgadvibhasaṃsthe arke bhanau yuṅ navame sute
english: Having converted one's specific time (iṣṭakāla) into "palas" (time units), and that which is obtained, starting with signs (bhādi), by dividing this by the "tithi" (lunar day): when the Sun is situated in a movable, fixed, or dual sign, this should be added to the Sun, and to the ninth and fifth houses.

sanskrit: स्फुटं प्राणपदाख्यं तल्लग्नं ज्ञेयं द्विजोत्तम । लग्नाद् द्विकोणे तुर्ये च राज्ये प्राणपदं तदा ॥७३॥
transliteration: sphuṭaṃ prāṇapadākhyaṃ tallagnaṃ jñeyaṃ dvijottama . lagnād dvikoṇe turye ca rājye prāṇapadaṃ tadā.
separated_sandhi: sphuṭam prāṇapadākhyam tat lagnam jñeyam dvijottamalagnāt dvikoṇe turye ca rājye prāṇapadam tadā
english: O best among the twice-born (dvijottama), that precise ascendant (lagna) named Prāṇapada should be understood. Then, if this Prāṇapada is located in a trine (5th or 9th), fourth, or tenth house from the ascendant (lagna)...

sanskrit: शुभं जन्म विजानीयात्तथैवैकादशेऽपि च । अन्यस्थाने स्थितं चेत् स्यात् तदा जन्माशुभं वदेत् ॥७४॥
transliteration: śubhaṃ janma vijānīyāttathaivaikādaśe'pi ca . anyasthāne sthitaṃ cet syāt tadā janmāśubhaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: śubham janma vijānīyāt tathaiva ekādaśe api caanyasthāne sthitam cet syāt tadā janma aśubham vadet
english: One should know the native's birth (janma) to be auspicious (śubha) [if Prāṇapada is in those specified houses]. Likewise, if it is in the eleventh house as well, the birth is auspicious. If it is located in any other house, then one should declare the native's birth to be inauspicious (aśubha).

sanskrit: अहोरात्रस्य पूर्वान्त्यलोपाद् होराऽवशिष्यते । तस्य विज्ञानमात्रेण जातकर्मफलं वदेत् ॥१॥
transliteration: ahorātrasya pūrvāntyalopād horā'vaśiṣyate . tasya vijñānamātreṇa jātakarmaphalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: ahorātrasya pūrvāntyalopāt horā avaśiṣyatetasya vijñānamātreṇa jātakaraphalam vadet
english: After removing the first and last parts of a day and night, a horā remains. Merely by understanding this (horā), one should predict the results of a person's actions (karma) at the time of birth.

sanskrit: यदव्यक्तात्मको विष्णुः कालरुपो जनार्दनः । तस्याङ्गानि निबोध त्वं क्रमान्मेषादिराश्यः ॥२॥
transliteration: yadavyaktātmako viṣṇuḥ kālarupo janārdanaḥ . tasyāṅgāni nibodha tvaṃ kramānmeṣādirāśyaḥ.
separated_sandhi: yat avyaktātmakaḥ viṣṇuḥ kālarupaḥ janārdanaḥtasya aṅgāni nibodha tvam kramāt meṣādirāśyaḥ
english: You should know his limbs, which are, in order, the zodiac signs beginning with Aries (Meṣa). He is Viṣṇu, whose fundamental nature (ātman) is unmanifest, and who embodies the form of time.

sanskrit: मेषो वृषश्च मिथुनः कर्कसिंहकुमारिकाः । तुलालिऽश्च धनुर्नक्रे कुम्भो मीनस्ततः परम् ॥३॥
transliteration: meṣo vṛṣaśca mithunaḥ karkasiṃhakumārikāḥ . tulāli'śca dhanurnakre kumbho mīnastataḥ param.
separated_sandhi: meṣaḥ vṛṣaḥ ca mithunaḥ karkasiṃhakumārikāḥ tulāaliḥ ca dhanuḥ nakraḥ kumbhaḥ mīnaḥ tataḥ param
english: Aries, Taurus, and Gemini; Cancer, Leo, and Virgo; Libra and Scorpio; Sagittarius, Capricorn; Aquarius, and then Pisces.

sanskrit: शीर्षानने तथा बाहू हृत्क्रोडकटिबस्तयः । गुह्योरुयुगले जानुयुग्मे वै जङ्घके तथा ॥४॥
transliteration: śīrṣānane tathā bāhū hṛtkroḍakaṭibastayaḥ . guhyoruyugale jānuyugme vai jaṅghake tathā.
separated_sandhi: śīrṣānanane tathā bāhū hṛtkroḍakaṭibastayaḥguhyoruyugale jānuyugme vai jaṅghake tathā
english: The head and face, similarly the two arms, then the heart, chest, waist, and bladder regions. Also, the genitals and two thighs, indeed the two knees, and the two shanks.

sanskrit: चरनौ द्वौ तथा मेषात् ज्ञेयाः शीर्षादयः क्रमात् । चरस्थिरद्विस्वभावाः क्रूराक्रूरौ नरस्त्रियौ ॥५॥
transliteration: caranau dvau tathā meṣāt jñeyāḥ śīrṣādayaḥ kramāt . carasthiradvisvabhāvāḥ krūrākrūrau narastriyau.
separated_sandhi: caraṇau dvau tathā meṣāt jñeyāḥ śīrṣādayaḥ kramātcarasthiradvisvabhāvāḥ krūrākrūrau narastriyau
english: The body parts, from the head downwards to the two feet, should be understood sequentially, starting with Aries. These (zodiac signs) are further characterized by movable, fixed, and dual natures; as either malevolent or benevolent; and as masculine or feminine.

sanskrit: पित्तानिलत्रिधात्वौक्यश्लेष्मिकाश्च क्रियादयः । रक्तवर्णो बृहद्गात्रश्चतुष्पाद्रात्रिविक्रमी ॥६॥
transliteration: pittānilatridhātvaukyaśleṣmikāśca kriyādayaḥ . raktavarṇo bṛhadgātraścatuṣpādrātrivikramī.
separated_sandhi: pitta-anila-tri-dhātu-aukya-śleṣmikāḥ ca kriyā-ādayaḥrakta-varṇaḥ bṛhad-gātraḥ catuṣpāt rātri-vikramī
english: Its characteristics include bilious (pitta), windy (anila), a blend of the three humors, and phlegmatic (śleṣma) qualities. It is red-colored, large-bodied, four-footed, and powerful at night.

sanskrit: पूर्ववासी नृपज्ञातिः शैलचारी रजोगुणी । पृष्ठोदयी पावकी च मेषराशिः कुजाधिपः ॥७॥
transliteration: pūrvavāsī nṛpajñātiḥ śailacārī rajoguṇī . pṛṣṭhodayī pāvakī ca meṣarāśiḥ kujādhipaḥ.
separated_sandhi: pūrva-vāsī nṛpa-jñātiḥ śaila-cārī rajo-guṇīpṛṣṭha-udayī pāvakī ca meṣa-rāśiḥ kuja-adhipaḥ
english: Dwelling in the east, of royal kinship, moving in mountains, possessing the quality of passion (rajas), rising from the back, and fiery. This is the sign of Aries (Meṣa), ruled by Mars (Kuja).

sanskrit: श्वेतः शुक्राधिपो दीर्घश्चतुष्पाच्छर्वरीबली । याम्येट् ग्राम्यो वणिग्भूमिरजः पृष्ठोदयो वृषः ॥८॥
transliteration: śvetaḥ śukrādhipo dīrghaścatuṣpāccharvarībalī . yāmyeṭ grāmyo vaṇigbhūmirajaḥ pṛṣṭhodayo vṛṣaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śvetaḥ śukra-adhipaḥ dīrghaḥ catuṣpāt śarvarī-balīyāmya-iṭ grāmyaḥ vaṇik-bhūmi-rajas pṛṣṭha-udayaḥ vṛṣabhaḥ
english: It is white, ruled by Venus (Śukra), long, four-footed, and strong at night. It dwells in the south, is rural, of the merchant caste, earthy, and possesses the quality of passion (rajas), and rises from the back. This is Taurus (Vṛṣabha).

sanskrit: शीर्षोदयी नृमिथुनं सगदं च सवीणकम् । प्रत्यग्वायुर्द्विपाद्रात्रिबली ग्रामव्रजोऽनिली ॥९॥
transliteration: śīrṣodayī nṛmithunaṃ sagadaṃ ca savīṇakam . pratyagvāyurdvipādrātribalī grāmavrajo'nilī.
separated_sandhi: śīrṣa-udayī nṛ-mithunam sa-gadam ca sa-vīṇakampratyak-vāyuḥ dvipāt rātri-balī grāma-vrajaḥ anilī
english: It is a head-rising sign, represented by a pair of humans holding a mace and a lute. It is western, airy, two-footed, strong at night, of a rural nature, and windy.

sanskrit: समगात्रो हरिद्विर्णो मिथुनाख्यो बुधाधिपः । पाटलो वनचारी च ब्राह्मणो निशि वीर्यवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: samagātro haridvirṇo mithunākhyo budhādhipaḥ . pāṭalo vanacārī ca brāhmaṇo niśi vīryavān.
separated_sandhi: sama-gātraḥ harit-varṇaḥ mithuna-ākhyaḥ budha-adhipaḥpāṭalaḥ vana-cārī ca brāhmaṇaḥ niśi vīrya-vān
english: It has a well-proportioned body, is green-colored, and is named Gemini (Mithuna), ruled by Mercury (Budha). It is pale red, a forest-dweller, of the Brahmin caste, and powerful at night.

sanskrit: बहुपादचरः स्थौल्यतनुः सत्त्वगुणी जली । पृष्ठोदयी कर्कराशिर्मृगांकाऽधिपतिः स्मृतः ॥११॥
transliteration: bahupādacaraḥ sthaulyatanuḥ sattvaguṇī jalī . pṛṣṭhodayī karkarāśirmṛgāṃkā'dhipatiḥ smṛtaḥ.
separated_sandhi: bahupādacaraḥ sthaulyatanuḥ sattvaguṇī jalīpṛṣṭhodayī karkarāśiḥ mṛgāṅka adhipatiḥ smṛtaḥ
english: The sign of Cancer (karkarāśiḥ) is considered to have many feet, a bulky body, to be endowed with the quality of sattva, watery, and back-rising (pṛṣṭhodayī). Its ruler is the Moon (mṛgāṅka).

sanskrit: सिंहः सूर्याधिपः सत्त्वी चतुष्पात् क्षत्रियो वनी । शीर्षोदयी बृहद्गात्रःपाण्डुः पूर्वेड् द्युवीर्यवान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: siṃhaḥ sūryādhipaḥ sattvī catuṣpāt kṣatriyo vanī . śīrṣodayī bṛhadgātraḥpāṇḍuḥ pūrveḍ dyuvīryavān.
separated_sandhi: siṃhaḥ sūrya adhipaḥ sattvī catuṣpāt kṣatriyaḥ vanīśīrṣodayī bṛhat gātraḥ pāṇḍuḥ pūrvadik dyuvīryavān
english: The sign of Leo (siṃhaḥ), whose ruler is the Sun (sūrya), is sattvic, four-footed, of the warrior class (kṣatriya), forest-dwelling, head-rising (śīrṣodayī), large-bodied, pale in color, associated with the eastern direction, and strong during the day.

sanskrit: पार्वतीयाथ कन्याख्या राशिर्दिनबलान्विता । शीर्षोदया च मध्यांगा द्विपाद्याम्यचरा च सा ॥१३॥
transliteration: pārvatīyātha kanyākhyā rāśirdinabalānvitā . śīrṣodayā ca madhyāṃgā dvipādyāmyacarā ca sā.
separated_sandhi: pārvatīyā atha kanyā ākhyā rāśiḥ dinabala anvitāśīrṣodayā ca madhya aṅgā dvipāt yāmya carā ca sā
english: Now, the sign named Kanyā (Virgo), which is mountainous, is strong during the day, head-rising (śīrṣodayā), and of medium build. It is two-footed and moves southward.

sanskrit: सा सस्यदहना वैश्या चित्रवर्णा प्रभञ्जिनी । कुमारी तमसा युक्ता बालभावा बुधाधिपा ॥१४॥
transliteration: sā sasyadahanā vaiśyā citravarṇā prabhañjinī . kumārī tamasā yuktā bālabhāvā budhādhipā.
separated_sandhi: sā sasya dahanā vaiśyā citravarṇā prabhañjinīkumārī tamasā yuktā bālabhāvā budha adhipā
english: This sign (Virgo) is associated with agriculture and burning (sasyadahanā), belongs to the merchant class (vaiśya), is multi-colored (citravarṇā), and destructive (prabhañjinī). It is a virgin (kumārī), associated with the quality of tamas, of a childish nature, and ruled by Budha (Mercury).

sanskrit: शीर्षोदयी द्युवीर्याढ्यस्तुलः कृष्णो रजोगुणी । पश्चिमो भूचरो घाती शूद्रो मध्यतनुर्द्विपात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śīrṣodayī dyuvīryāḍhyastulaḥ kṛṣṇo rajoguṇī . paścimo bhūcaro ghātī śūdro madhyatanurdvipāt.
separated_sandhi: śīrṣodayī dyuvīrya āḍhyaḥ tulaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ rajoguṇīpaścimaḥ bhūcaraḥ ghātī śūdraḥ madhyatanuḥ dvipāt
english: The sign of Libra (tulaḥ) is head-rising (śīrṣodayī), very strong during the day, dark in color, and endowed with the quality of rajas. It is western, terrestrial, destructive, of the śūdra class, medium-bodied, and two-footed.

sanskrit: शुक्राऽधिपोऽथ स्वल्पांगो बहुपाद्ब्राह्मणो बिली । सौम्यस्थो दिनवीर्याढ्यः पिशंगो जलभूवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: śukrā'dhipo'tha svalpāṃgo bahupādbrāhmaṇo bilī . saumyastho dinavīryāḍhyaḥ piśaṃgo jalabhūvaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śukra-adhipaḥ atha svalpa-aṅgaḥ bahu-pād brāhmaṇaḥ bilīsaumya-sthaḥ dina-vīrya-āḍhyaḥ piśaṅgaḥ jala-bhūvaḥ
english: The sign ruled by Venus has a small body, is multi-footed, of the Brahmin class, and dwells in cavities. It is of a gentle nature, strong during the day, reddish-brown, and associated with water and earth.

sanskrit: रोमस्वाढ्योऽतितीक्ष्णाग्रो वृश्चिकश्च कुजाधिपः । पृष्ठोदयी त्वथ धनुर्गुरुस्वामी च सात्त्विकः ॥१७॥
transliteration: romasvāḍhyo'titīkṣṇāgro vṛścikaśca kujādhipaḥ . pṛṣṭhodayī tvatha dhanurgurusvāmī ca sāttvikaḥ.
separated_sandhi: roman-svāḍhyaḥ ati-tīkṣṇa-agraḥ vṛścikaḥ ca kuja-adhipaḥpṛṣṭha-udayī tu atha dhanuḥ guru-svāmī ca sāttvikaḥ
english: Scorpio (vṛścika), whose lord is Mars (kuja), is rich in hair and very sharp-pointed, and is back-rising. Then, Sagittarius (dhanu), whose lord is Jupiter (guru), is of a pure (sāttvika) nature.

sanskrit: पिंगलो निशिवीर्याढ्यः पावकः क्षत्रियो द्विपाद् । आदावन्ते चतुष्पादः समगात्रो धनुर्धरः ॥१८॥
transliteration: piṃgalo niśivīryāḍhyaḥ pāvakaḥ kṣatriyo dvipād . ādāvante catuṣpādaḥ samagātro dhanurdharaḥ.
separated_sandhi: piṅgalaḥ niśi-vīrya-āḍhyaḥ pāvakaḥ kṣatriyaḥ dvipādādau ante catuṣ-pādaḥ sama-gātraḥ dhanuḥ-dharaḥ
english: It (Sagittarius) is reddish-brown, strong at night, fiery, of the warrior (kṣatriya) class, and bipedal at the beginning while quadrupedal at the end. It has a well-proportioned body and is an archer (dhanurdhara).

sanskrit: पूर्वस्थो वसुधाचारी तेजस्वी ब्रह्मणा कृतः । मन्दाधिपस्तमी भौमी याम्येट् च निशि वीर्यवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: pūrvastho vasudhācārī tejasvī brahmaṇā kṛtaḥ . mandādhipastamī bhaumī yāmyeṭ ca niśi vīryavān.
separated_sandhi: pūrva-sthaḥ vasudhā-cārī tejasvī brahmaṇā kṛtaḥmanda-adhipaḥ tamī bhaumī yāmya-iṭ ca niśi vīryavān
english: It (Sagittarius) is situated in the east, moves on earth, is lustrous, and was created by Brahma. The sign ruled by Saturn is dark, earthy, oriented towards the south, and powerful at night.

sanskrit: पृष्ठोदयी बृहद्गात्रः कर्बुरो वनभूचरः । आदौ चतुष्पदोन्ते तु विपदो जलगो मतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhodayī bṛhadgātraḥ karburo vanabhūcaraḥ . ādau catuṣpadonte tu vipado jalago mataḥ.
separated_sandhi: pṛṣṭha-udayī bṛhat-gātraḥ karburaḥ vana-bhū-caraḥādau catuṣ-padaḥ ante tu vipādaḥ jala-gaḥ mataḥ
english: It (Capricorn) is back-rising, large-bodied, variegated, and dwells in forests and on earth. It is considered quadrupedal at the beginning and footless at the end, and water-dwelling.

sanskrit: कुम्भः कुम्भी नरो बभ्रुवर्णो मध्यतनुर्द्विपात् । द्युवीर्यो जलमध्यस्थो वातशीर्षोदयी तमः ॥२१॥
transliteration: kumbhaḥ kumbhī naro babhruvarṇo madhyatanurdvipāt . dyuvīryo jalamadhyastho vātaśīrṣodayī tamaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kumbhaḥ kumbhī naraḥ babhruvarṇaḥ madhyatanuḥ dvipātdyuvīryaḥ jalamadhyasthaḥ vātaśīrṣodayī tamaḥ
english: Aquarius (kumbha) is represented by a pot and a pot-bearer, a man of brown color, with a medium-sized body, two-footed, strong during the day, situated in the middle of water, rising head-first (śīrṣodaya), and having a dark (tamasic) nature.

sanskrit: शूद्रः पश्चिमदेशस्य स्वामी दैवाकरिः स्मृतः । मीनौ पुच्छास्यसंलग्नौ मीनराशिर्दिवाबली ॥२२॥
transliteration: śūdraḥ paścimadeśasya svāmī daivākariḥ smṛtaḥ . mīnau pucchāsyasaṃlagnau mīnarāśirdivābalī.
separated_sandhi: śūdraḥ paścimadeśasya svāmī daivākariḥ smṛtaḥmīnau pucchāsyasaṃlagnau mīnarāśiḥ divābalī
english: Pisces (mīna) is associated with the śūdra varna and is the ruler of the western region. Its ruler is remembered as the preceptor of the gods (daivākari). The sign of Pisces depicts two fish joined at their tails and mouths, and it is strong during the day.

sanskrit: जली सत्त्वगुणाढ्यश्च स्वस्थो जलचरो द्विजः । अपदो मध्यदेही च सौम्यस्थो ह्युभयोदयी ॥२३॥
transliteration: jalī sattvaguṇāḍhyaśca svastho jalacaro dvijaḥ . apado madhyadehī ca saumyastho hyubhayodayī.
separated_sandhi: jalī sattvaguṇāḍhyaḥ ca svasthaḥ jalacaraḥ dvijaḥapadaḥ madhyadehī ca saumyasthaḥ hi ubhayodayī
english: It is watery, rich in the quality of goodness (sattva guna), healthy, aquatic, a 'twice-born' (dvija, referring to fish in this context), footless, and possesses a medium-sized body. It is of a gentle nature and indeed rises both ways (ubhayodaya).

sanskrit: सुराचार्याधिपश्चेति राशीनां गदिता गुणाः । त्रिंशद्भागात्मकानां च स्थूलसूक्ष्मफलाय च ॥२४॥
transliteration: surācāryādhipaśceti rāśīnāṃ gaditā guṇāḥ . triṃśadbhāgātmakānāṃ ca sthūlasūkṣmaphalāya ca.
separated_sandhi: surācāryādhipaḥ ca iti rāśīnām gaditā guṇāḥtriṃśadbhāgātmakānām ca sthūlasūkṣmaphalāya ca
english: And so, the preceptor of the gods (surācārya) is the ruler (adhipa). Thus, the qualities of the zodiacal signs (rāśi), each consisting of thirty degrees, have been declared for both gross and subtle outcomes.

sanskrit: अथातः सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि श‍ृणुष्व मुनिपुंगव । जन्मलग्नं च संशोध्य निषेकं परिशोधयेत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: athātaḥ sampravakṣyāmi śa‍ृṇuṣva munipuṃgava . janmalagnaṃ ca saṃśodhya niṣekaṃ pariśodhayet.
separated_sandhi: atha ataḥ sampravakṣyāmi śṛṇuṣva munipuṅgavajanmalagnaṃ ca saṃśodhya niṣekaṃ pariśodhayet
english: Now, therefore, I shall explain thoroughly; listen, O chief among sages (munipuṅgava). One should first correct (saṃśodhya) the birth ascendant (janmalagna) and then rectify the moment of conception (niṣeka).

sanskrit: तदहं सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि मैत्रेय त्वं विधारय । जन्मलग्नात् परिज्ञानं निषेकं सर्व्जन्तु यत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tadahaṃ sampravakṣyāmi maitreya tvaṃ vidhāraya . janmalagnāt parijñānaṃ niṣekaṃ sarvjantu yat.
separated_sandhi: tad aham sampravakṣyāmi maitreya tvam vidhārayajanmalagnāt parijñānam niṣekam sarvajantu yat
english: Therefore, O Maitreya, I will now fully explain this to you; please grasp it well. This concerns the knowledge of conception for all living beings, derived from the natal ascendant.

sanskrit: यस्मिन् भावे स्थितो मन्दस्तस्य मान्देर्यदन्तरम् । लग्नभाग्यान्तरं योज्यं यच्च राश्यादि जायते ॥२७॥
transliteration: yasmin bhāve sthito mandastasya mānderyadantaram . lagnabhāgyāntaraṃ yojyaṃ yacca rāśyādi jāyate.
separated_sandhi: yasmin bhāve sthitaḥ mandaḥ tasya māndeḥ yat antaramlagnabhāgyāntaram yojyam yat ca rāśyādi jāyate
english: In whichever house Saturn (manda) is situated, the difference between that house and its māndi point should be added to the difference between the ascendant and the fortunepoint (bhāgya). The resulting sign (rāśyādi) and other divisions should then be noted.

sanskrit: मासादि तन्मितं ज्ञेयं जन्मतः प्रक् निषेकजम् । यद्यदृश्यदलेङ्गेशस्तदेन्दोर्भुक्तभागयुक् ॥२८॥
transliteration: māsādi tanmitaṃ jñeyaṃ janmataḥ prak niṣekajam . yadyadṛśyadaleṅgeśastadendorbhuktabhāgayuk.
separated_sandhi: māsādi tatmitam jñeyam janmataḥ prāk niṣekajamyadi adṛśyadale iṅgeśaḥ tad indoḥ bhuktabhāgayuk
english: That resulting measure (rāśyādi) should be understood as the duration, in months and other divisions, from conception until birth. If the lord of the conception ascendant (iṅgeśa) is located in the invisible half of the zodiac, then the traversed degrees of the Moon (indoḥ) should be included in the calculation.

sanskrit: तत्काले साधयेल्लग्नं शोधयेत् पूर्ववत्तनुम् । तस्माच्छुभाशुभं वाच्यं गर्भस्थस्य विशेषतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatkāle sādhayellagnaṃ śodhayet pūrvavattanum . tasmācchubhāśubhaṃ vācyaṃ garbhasthasya viśeṣataḥ.
separated_sandhi: tatkāle sādhayet lagnam śodhāyet pūrvavat tanumtasmāt śubhāśubham vācyam garbhasthasya viśeṣataḥ
english: At that very time (of conception), one should ascertain the ascendant and rectify it as previously instructed. Therefore, based on this rectified ascendant, one should predict the auspicious and inauspicious (results), especially for the child in the womb.

sanskrit: शुभाशुभं वदेत् पित्रोर्जीवनं मरणं तथा । एवं निषेकलग्नेन सम्यग् ज्ञेयं स्वकल्पनात् ॥३०॥
transliteration: śubhāśubhaṃ vadet pitrorjīvanaṃ maraṇaṃ tathā . evaṃ niṣekalagnena samyag jñeyaṃ svakalpanāt.
separated_sandhi: śubhāśubham vadet pitroḥ jīvanam maraṇam tathāevam niṣekalagnena samyak jñeyam svakalpanāt
english: One should predict the auspicious and inauspicious results, as well as the life and death of the parents. Thus, by utilizing the conception ascendant, all these matters should be properly understood through one's own astrological reasoning.

sanskrit: अथाहं सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि तवाग्रे द्विजसत्तम । भावहोराघटीसंज्ञलग्नानीति पृथक् पृथक् ॥१॥
transliteration: athāhaṃ sampravakṣyāmi tavāgre dvijasattama . bhāvahorāghaṭīsaṃjñalagnānīti pṛthak pṛthak.
separated_sandhi: atha aham sampravakṣyāmi tava agre dvijasattamabhāvahorāghaṭīsaṃjñalagnāni iti pṛthak pṛthak
english: Now, O best among the twice-born (dvija), I will fully explain to you the ascendants known as Bhāva, Horā, and Ghaṭī, each distinctly.

sanskrit: सूर्योदयं समारभ्य घटिकानां तु पंचकम् । प्रयाति जन्मपर्यन्तं भावलग्नं तदेव हि ॥२॥
transliteration: sūryodayaṃ samārabhya ghaṭikānāṃ tu paṃcakam . prayāti janmaparyantaṃ bhāvalagnaṃ tadeva hi.
separated_sandhi: sūryodayam samārabhy ghaṭikānām tu pañcakamprayāti janmaparyantam bhāvalagnam tat eva hi
english: Starting from sunrise, a duration counted in units of five ghaṭīs (ghaṭika) proceeds up to the time of birth. That very principle is indeed the Bhāva-lagna.

sanskrit: इष्टं घट्यादिकं भक्त्वा पंचभिर्भादिजं फलम् । योज्यमौदयिके सूर्ये भावलग्नं स्फुटं च तत् ॥३॥
transliteration: iṣṭaṃ ghaṭyādikaṃ bhaktvā paṃcabhirbhādijaṃ phalam . yojyamaudayike sūrye bhāvalagnaṃ sphuṭaṃ ca tat.
separated_sandhi: iṣṭaghaṭyādikam bhaktvā pañcabhiḥ bhādijam phalamyojyam audayike sūrye bhāvalagnam sphuṭam ca tat
english: Divide the specified time (from sunrise to birth), in ghaṭīs (ghaṭika) and other units, by five. The resulting value (in signs and degrees) should then be added to the Sun's position at sunrise. That, indeed, is the precise Bhāva-lagna.

sanskrit: तथा सार्धद्विघटिकामितादर्कोदयाद् द्विज । प्रयाति लग्नं तन्नाम होरालग्नं प्रचक्षते ॥४॥
transliteration: tathā sārdhadvighaṭikāmitādarkodayād dvija . prayāti lagnaṃ tannāma horālagnaṃ pracakṣate.
separated_sandhi: tathā sārdhadvighaṭikāmita arkodayāt dvijaprayāti lagnam tat nāma horālagnam pracakṣate
english: Similarly, O twice-born (dvija), starting from sunrise, an ascendant proceeds, measured by two and a half ghaṭīs (ghaṭika). They call that ascendant, by name, the Horā-lagna.

sanskrit: इष्टघट्यादिकं द्विघ्नं पञ्चाप्तं भादिकं च यत् । योज्यमौदयिके भानौ होरालग्नं स्फुटं हि तत् ॥५॥
transliteration: iṣṭaghaṭyādikaṃ dvighnaṃ pañcāptaṃ bhādikaṃ ca yat . yojyamaudayike bhānau horālagnaṃ sphuṭaṃ hi tat.
separated_sandhi: iṣṭaghaṭyādikam dvighnam pañcāptam bhādikam ca yatyojyam audayike bhānau horālagnam sphuṭam hi tat
english: The specified time (from sunrise to birth) in ghaṭīs (ghaṭika) and so on, multiplied by two and then divided by five, which (result) is expressed in signs and other units, should be added to the Sun's position at sunrise. That, indeed, is the precise Horā-lagna.
