separated_sandhi: dvi-varṣam pañca-rekhāsu ṣaḍ-rekhāsu catuḥ-samā |ṣaḍ-varṣam sapta-rekhāsu vasavaḥ aṣṭāsu vatsarāḥ
english: Two years are for positions with five auspicious points. For six points, four years are allotted. Six years are given for seven points, and eight years (the number of Vasus) for eight auspicious points.

sanskrit: एवं यदागतायुः स्यात् सर्वखेटसमुद्भवम् । तदर्धं स्फुटमायुः स्यादष्टवर्गभवं नृणाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: evaṃ yadāgatāyuḥ syāt sarvakheṭasamudbhavam . tadardhaṃ sphuṭamāyuḥ syādaṣṭavargabhavaṃ nṛṇām.
separated_sandhi: evam yadā āgatāyuḥ syāt sarvakheṭasamudbhavam tatardham sphuṭam āyuḥ syāt aṣṭavargabhavam nṛṇām
english: Thus, when a lifespan derived from all planets has been calculated, half of that lifespan is considered the precise (actual) lifespan for human beings, originating from the aṣṭavarga (system).

sanskrit: द्वादशारं लिखेच्चकं जन्मलग्नादिभैर्युतम् । सर्वाष्टकफलान्यत्र संयोज्य प्रतिभं न्यसेत् ॥१॥
transliteration: dvādaśāraṃ likheccakaṃ janmalagnādibhairyutam . sarvāṣṭakaphalānyatra saṃyojya pratibhaṃ nyaset.
separated_sandhi: dvādaśāram likhet cakram janmalagnādibhaiḥ yutamsarvāṣṭakaphālāni atra saṃyojya pratibham nyaset
english: One should draw a twelve-spoked chart, marking the twelve signs (bhāvas) starting from the birth ascendant. Then, after compiling the total aṣṭavarga scores, one should place them in each sign (bhāva) of this chart.

sanskrit: समुदायाभिधानोऽयमष्टवर्गः प्रकथ्यते । अतः फलानि जातानां विज्ञेयानि द्विजोत्तम ॥२॥
transliteration: samudāyābhidhāno'yamaṣṭavargaḥ prakathyate . ataḥ phalāni jātānāṃ vijñeyāni dvijottama.
separated_sandhi: samudāyābhidhānaḥ ayam aṣṭavargaḥ prakathyateataḥ phalāni jātānām vijñeyāni dvijottama
english: This system is referred to as 'Samudāya Aṣṭavarga' (aggregate of eight divisions). Therefore, O best of brahmins (dvijottama), the destinies of individuals should be understood from it.

sanskrit: त्रिंशाधिकफला ये स्यू राशयस्ते शुभप्रदाः । पञ्चविंशादित्रिंशान्तफला मध्यफला स्मृताः ॥३॥
transliteration: triṃśādhikaphalā ye syū rāśayaste śubhapradāḥ . pañcaviṃśāditriṃśāntaphalā madhyaphalā smṛtāḥ.
separated_sandhi: triṃśādhikaphālāḥ ye syuḥ rāśayaḥ te śubhapradāḥpañcaviṃśāditriṃśāntaphalāḥ madhyaphalāḥ smṛtāḥ
english: Those signs (rāśi) which have scores greater than thirty are auspicious. Signs with scores ranging from twenty-five to thirty are considered to yield moderate results.

sanskrit: अतः क्षीणफला ये ते राशयः कष्टदुःखदा । शुभे श्रेष्ठफलान् राशीन् योजयेन्मतिमान्नरः ॥४॥
transliteration: ataḥ kṣīṇaphalā ye te rāśayaḥ kaṣṭaduḥkhadā . śubhe śreṣṭhaphalān rāśīn yojayenmatimānnaraḥ.
separated_sandhi: ataḥ kṣīṇaphalāḥ ye te rāśayaḥ kaṣṭaduḥkhadāḥśubhe śreṣṭhaphalān rāśīn yojayet matimān naraḥ
english: Therefore, those zodiac signs that yield meager results are productive of trouble and sorrow. A wise person should utilize signs that produce excellent results for auspicious undertakings.

sanskrit: कष्टराशीन् सुकार्येषु वर्जयेद् द्विजसत्तम । श्रेष्ठराशिगतः खेटः शुभोऽन्यत्राऽशुभप्रदः ॥५॥
transliteration: kaṣṭarāśīn sukāryeṣu varjayed dvijasattama . śreṣṭharāśigataḥ kheṭaḥ śubho'nyatrā'śubhapradaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kaṣṭarāśīn sukāryeṣu varjayet dvijasattamaśreṣṭharāśigataḥ kheṭaḥ śubhaḥ anyatra aśubhapradaḥ
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), one should avoid troublesome zodiac signs for auspicious undertakings. A planet situated in an excellent zodiac sign is auspicious, whereas in other signs, it bestows inauspicious results.

sanskrit: तन्वादिव्ययपर्यन्तं दृष्ट्वा भाव्फलानि वै । अधिके शोभनं ज्ञेयं हीने हानिं विनिर्दिशेत् ॥६॥
transliteration: tanvādivyayaparyantaṃ dṛṣṭvā bhāvphalāni vai . adhike śobhanaṃ jñeyaṃ hīne hāniṃ vinirdiśet.
separated_sandhi: tanvādi vyayaparyantam dṛṣṭvā bhāva phalāni vaiadhike śobhanam jñeyam hīne hānim vinirdiśet
english: After considering the results of the astrological houses (bhāvas), from the first (tanu) up to the twelfth (vyaya), one should understand that an excess indicates auspicious results, and a deficiency points to loss.

sanskrit: मध्ये मध्यफलं ब्रूयाद् तत्तद्भावसमुद्भवम् । मध्यात् फलाधिके लाभो लाभात् क्षीणगतोव्ययः ॥७॥
transliteration: madhye madhyaphalaṃ brūyād tattadbhāvasamudbhavam . madhyāt phalādhike lābho lābhāt kṣīṇagatovyayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: madhye madhyaphalam brūyāt tattadbhāvasamudbhavammadhyāt phala adhike lābhaḥ lābhāt kṣīṇagataḥ vyayaḥ
english: One should predict a moderate result (phala) arising from each respective house (bhāva) when conditions are moderate. If the result is greater than moderate, it indicates gain (lābha); and if it diminishes from gain, it signifies loss (vyaya).

sanskrit: लग्नं फलाधिकं यस्य भोगवानर्थवान् हि सः । विपरीतेन दारिद्र्यं भवत्येव न संशयः ॥८॥
transliteration: lagnaṃ phalādhikaṃ yasya bhogavānarthavān hi saḥ . viparītena dāridryaṃ bhavatyeva na saṃśayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: lagnaṃ phalādhikaṃ yasya bhogavān arthavān hi saḥ| viparītena dāridryaṃ bhavati eva na saṃśayaḥ
english: Indeed, a person whose ascendant (lagna) is strong and productive of good results will be one who enjoys pleasures and possesses wealth. Conversely, if the ascendant is weak, poverty will certainly occur; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: दशावदिह भावानां कृत्वा खंडत्रयं बुधः । पश्येत् पापसमारूढं खंडे कष्टकरं वदेत् ॥९॥
transliteration: daśāvadiha bhāvānāṃ kṛtvā khaṃḍatrayaṃ budhaḥ . paśyet pāpasamārūḍhaṃ khaṃḍe kaṣṭakaraṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: daśāvat iha bhāvānām kṛtvā khaṇḍatrayam budhaḥ| paśyet pāpasamārūḍham khaṇḍe kaṣṭakaram vadet
english: Here, the wise astrologer (budhaḥ) should divide the astrological houses into three sections, treating them similarly to how planetary periods (dasha) are considered. If he observes a section afflicted by malefic planets, he should declare that particular section to be troublesome.

sanskrit: सौम्यैर्युक्तं शुभं ब्रूयान्मिश्रैर्मिश्रफलं यथा । क्रमाद् बाल्याद्यवस्थासु खंडत्रयफलं वदेत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: saumyairyuktaṃ śubhaṃ brūyānmiśrairmiśraphalaṃ yathā . kramād bālyādyavasthāsu khaṃḍatrayaphalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: saumyaiḥ yuktam śubham brūyāt miśraiḥ miśraphalam yathā| kramāt bālyādi avasthāsu khaṇḍatrayaphalam vadet
english: If a division is associated with benefic planets, one should declare it to yield auspicious results. If it is associated with a mixture of benefic and malefic planets, one should declare it to yield mixed results. Similarly, the wise astrologer should successively declare the results of these three divisions for various life stages, such as childhood and so forth.

sanskrit: रेखाभिः सप्तभिर्युक्ते मासेमृत्युभयं नृणाम् । सुवर्णं विंशतिपलं दद्यात् द्वौ तिलपर्वतौ ॥११॥
transliteration: rekhābhiḥ saptabhiryukte māsemṛtyubhayaṃ nṛṇām . suvarṇaṃ viṃśatipalaṃ dadyāt dvau tilaparvatau.
separated_sandhi: rekhābhiḥ saptabhiḥ yukte māse mṛtyubhayam nṛṇām| suvarṇam viṃśatipalam dadyāt dvau tilaparvatau
english: For people, if a month is associated with seven specific indications (rekhābhiḥ), there will be a fear of death. In such a situation, one should offer twenty palas of gold and two mounds of sesame seeds.

sanskrit: रेखाभिरष्टभिर्युक्ते मासे मृत्युबशो नरः । असत्फलविनाशाय दद्यात् कर्पूरजां तुलाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: rekhābhiraṣṭabhiryukte māse mṛtyubaśo naraḥ . asatphalavināśāya dadyāt karpūrajāṃ tulām.
separated_sandhi: rekhābhiḥ aṣṭabhiḥ yukte māse mṛtyuvaśaḥ naraḥasatphalavināśāya dadyāt karpūrajāṃ tulām
english: If a month is associated with eight lines, a person in that month becomes subject to death. To destroy these inauspicious outcomes, one should donate a balance made of camphor.

sanskrit: रेखाभिर्मवभियुक्ते मासे सर्पभयं वदेत् । अश्वैश्चतुर्भिः संयुक्तं रथं दद्याच्छुभाप्तये ॥१३॥
transliteration: rekhābhirmavabhiyukte māse sarpabhayaṃ vadet . aśvaiścaturbhiḥ saṃyuktaṃ rathaṃ dadyācchubhāptaye.
separated_sandhi: rekhābhiḥ navabhiḥ yukte māse sarpabhayam vadetaśvaiḥ caturbhiḥ saṃyuktaṃ ratham dadyāt śubhāptaye
english: If a month is associated with nine lines, one should predict danger from snakes. To achieve auspicious results, one should donate a chariot yoked with four horses.

sanskrit: रेखाभिर्दशभिर्युक्ते मासे शस्त्रभयं तथा । दद्याच्छुभफलावाप्त्यै कवचं वज्रसंयुतम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: rekhābhirdaśabhiryukte māse śastrabhayaṃ tathā . dadyācchubhaphalāvāptyai kavacaṃ vajrasaṃyutam.
separated_sandhi: rekhābhiḥ daśabhiḥ yukte māse śastrabhayam tathādadyāt śubhaphalāvāptyai kavacam vajrasaṃyuktam
english: If a month is associated with ten lines, there is also danger from weapons. To obtain auspicious results, one should donate an armor studded with diamonds.

sanskrit: अभिशापभयं यत्र रेखा रुद्रसमा द्विज । दिक्पलस्वर्णघटितां प्रदद्यात् प्रतिमां विधोः ॥१५॥
transliteration: abhiśāpabhayaṃ yatra rekhā rudrasamā dvija . dikpalasvarṇaghaṭitāṃ pradadyāt pratimāṃ vidhoḥ.
separated_sandhi: abhiśāpabhayam yatra rekhā rudrasamā dvijadikpalasvarṇaghaṭitām pradadyāt pratimām vidhoḥ
english: O twice-born one (dvija), when there is danger from curses (abhīśāpa-bhaya) and the lines are eleven (Rudra-like), one should offer an image of the Moon (Vidhu) made of gold associated with the guardians of the directions (dikpalas).

sanskrit: युक्ते द्वादशरेखाभिर्जले मृत्युभयं वदेत् । सशस्यभूमिः विप्राय दत्वा शुभफलं भवेत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yukte dvādaśarekhābhirjale mṛtyubhayaṃ vadet . saśasyabhūmiḥ viprāya datvā śubhaphalaṃ bhavet.
separated_sandhi: yukte dvādaśarekhābhiḥ jale mṛtyubhayam vadet |saśasyabhūmiḥ viprāya datvā śubhaphalam bhavet
english: If [a configuration is] associated with twelve lines, one should predict danger of death by water. By donating fertile land to a Brāhmaṇa (vipra), an auspicious outcome (phala) will arise.

sanskrit: विश्वप्रमितरेखाभिर्व्याघ्रान्मृत्युभयं तथा । विष्णोर्हिरण्यगर्भस्य दानं कुर्याच्चुभाप्तये ॥१७॥
transliteration: viśvapramitarekhābhirvyāghrānmṛtyubhayaṃ tathā . viṣṇorhiraṇyagarbhasya dānaṃ kuryāccubhāptaye.
separated_sandhi: viśvapramitarekhābhiḥ vyāghrān mṛtyubhayam tathā| viṣṇoḥ hiraṇyagarbhasya dānam kuryāt śubhāptaye
english: If [a configuration is marked] by lines equivalent to 'viśva' (thirteen), similarly, one should predict fear of death from tigers. To achieve auspiciousness, one should make a donation (dāna) in the name of Viṣṇu or Hiraṇyagarbha.

sanskrit: शत्रप्रमितरेखाभिर्युक्ते मासे मृतेर्भयम् । वराहप्रतिमां दद्यात् कनकेन विनिर्मिताम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śatrapramitarekhābhiryukte māse mṛterbhayam . varāhapratimāṃ dadyāt kanakena vinirmitām.
separated_sandhi: śatrupramitarekhābhiḥ yukte māse mṛteḥ bhayam| varāhapratimām dadyāt kanakena vinirmitām
english: If [a configuration is] associated with lines indicating the number 'śatru' (six), or when such a month occurs, one should predict fear of death. One should donate an idol of Varāha, made of gold.

sanskrit: तिथितिश्च नृपाद् भीतिर्दद्यात् तत्र गजं द्विज । रिष्टं षोडशभिर्दद्यात् मूर्तिं कल्पतरोस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tithitiśca nṛpād bhītirdadyāt tatra gajaṃ dvija . riṣṭaṃ ṣoḍaśabhirdadyāt mūrtiṃ kalpatarostathā.
separated_sandhi: tithitiḥ ca nṛpāt bhītiḥ dadyāt tatra gajam dvija| riṣṭam ṣoḍaśabhiḥ dadyāt mūrtim kalpataroḥ tathā
english: When the lunar day (tithi) [number fifteen] is indicated, there is fear from the king. In that situation, O Brāhmaṇa (dvija), one should donate an elephant. And similarly, when a calamity (riṣṭa) is indicated by sixteen [factors], one should donate a statue of the wish-fulfilling tree (kalpataru).

sanskrit: सप्तेन्दुभिर्व्याधिभयं दद्यात् धेनुं गुडं तथा । कलहोऽष्टेन्दुभिर्दद्याद् रत्नगोभूरिरण्यकम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: saptendubhirvyādhibhayaṃ dadyāt dhenuṃ guḍaṃ tathā . kalaho'ṣṭendubhirdadyād ratnagobhūriraṇyakam.
separated_sandhi: sapta indubhiḥ vyādhibhayam dadyāt dhenum guḍam tathākalahaḥ aṣṭa indubhiḥ dadyāt ratnago-bhūri-hiraṇyakam
english: When the time period indicated by 'seven moons' (71) arrives, there will be a fear of illness. One should then donate a cow and jaggery. When the time period indicated by 'eight moons' (81) arrives, there will be quarrels. One should then donate jewels, cows, land, and gold.

sanskrit: अङ्केन्दुभिः प्रवासः स्याच्छान्तिं कुर्याद् विधानतः । विंशत्या बुद्धिनाशः स्याद् गणेशं तत्र पूजयेत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: aṅkendubhiḥ pravāsaḥ syācchāntiṃ kuryād vidhānataḥ . viṃśatyā buddhināśaḥ syād gaṇeśaṃ tatra pūjayet.
separated_sandhi: aṅka indubhiḥ pravāsaḥ syāt śāntim kuryāt vidhānataḥviṃśatyā buddhināśaḥ syāt gaṇeśam tatra pūjayet
english: At (the age or period of) 91, there will be travel or absence from home; one should perform propitiatory rites according to the prescribed methods. At 20, there will be a loss of intellect or impaired judgment; then, one should worship Gaṇeśa.

sanskrit: रोगपीडैकविंशत्या दद्याद् धान्यस्य पर्वतम् । यमाश्विभिर्बन्धुपीडा दद्यादादर्शकं द्विज ॥२२॥
transliteration: rogapīḍaikaviṃśatyā dadyād dhānyasya parvatam . yamāśvibhirbandhupīḍā dadyādādarśakaṃ dvija.
separated_sandhi: rogapīḍā ekaviṃśatyā dadyāt dhānyasya parvatamyama aśvibhiḥ bandhupīḍā dadyāt ādarśakam dvija
english: At (the age or period of) 21, there will be affliction by disease; one should donate a mountain of grain. O Brahmin (dvija), at 22, there will be trouble from relatives; one should donate a mirror.

sanskrit: त्रयोविंशत्रिसंयुक्ते मासे क्लेशमवाप्नुयात् । सौवर्णीं प्रतिमां दद्याद्रवेः सप्तपलैर्बुधः ॥२३॥
transliteration: trayoviṃśatrisaṃyukte māse kleśamavāpnuyāt . sauvarṇīṃ pratimāṃ dadyādraveḥ saptapalairbudhaḥ.
separated_sandhi: trayo-viṃśat-tri-saṃyukte māse kleśam avāpnuyātsauvarṇīm pratimām dadyāt raveḥ saptapalaiḥ budhaḥ
english: In the third and twenty-third year (or period), one will experience distress. A wise person should donate a golden idol of the Sun weighing seven palas.

sanskrit: वेदाश्विभिर्बन्धुहीनो दद्याद् गिदशकं नृपः । सर्वरोगविनाशार्थं जपहोमादिकं चरेत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vedāśvibhirbandhuhīno dadyād gidaśakaṃ nṛpaḥ . sarvarogavināśārthaṃ japahomādikaṃ caret.
separated_sandhi: vedāśvibhiḥ bandhuhīnaḥ dadyāt gidaśakamnṛpaḥ sarvarogavināśārtham japahomādikaṃ caret
english: If one is deprived of relatives due to the influence of six (factors or periods), a patron should donate seventy (units of value). For the complete destruction of all diseases, one should perform chanting (japa), fire sacrifices (homa), and similar rites.

sanskrit: धीहानिः पञ्चविंशत्या पूज्या वागीश्वरी तदा । षड्विंशत्याऽर्थहानिः स्यात् स्वर्णं दद्याद्विचक्षणः ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhīhāniḥ pañcaviṃśatyā pūjyā vāgīśvarī tadā . ṣaḍviṃśatyā'rthahāniḥ syāt svarṇaṃ dadyādvicakṣaṇaḥ.
separated_sandhi: dhīhāniḥ pañcaviṃśatyā pūjyā vāgīśvarī tadāṣaḍviṃśatyā arthahāniḥ syāt svarṇaṃ dadyāt vicakṣaṇaḥ
english: When there is a loss of intelligence (dhīhāni) during the twenty-fifth (period or year), then Vāgīśvarī (Sarasvatī) should be worshipped. When there is a loss of wealth (arthahāni) during the twenty-sixth (period or year), a wise person should offer gold.

sanskrit: तथा च सप्तविंशत्या श्रीसुक्तं तत्र संजपेत् । अष्टविंशतिसंयुक्ते मासे मासे हानिश्च सर्वथा ॥२६॥
transliteration: tathā ca saptaviṃśatyā śrīsuktaṃ tatra saṃjapet . aṣṭaviṃśatisaṃyukte māse māse hāniśca sarvathā.
separated_sandhi: tathā ca saptaviṃśatyā śrīsūktaṃ tatra saṃjapetaṣṭaviṃśatisaṃyukte māse māse hāniḥ ca sarvathā
english: And similarly, at the twenty-seventh (period or year), one should properly chant the Śrīsūkta. If the twenty-eighth (period or year) is conjoined, there will be complete loss (hāni) every single month.

sanskrit: सूर्यहोमश्च विधिना कर्त्तव्यः शुभकांक्षिभिः । एकोनत्रिंशता चापि चिन्तव्याकुलितो भवेत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sūryahomaśca vidhinā karttavyaḥ śubhakāṃkṣibhiḥ . ekonatriṃśatā cāpi cintavyākulito bhavet.
separated_sandhi: sūryahomaḥ ca vidhinā kartavyaḥ śubhakāṅkṣibhiḥekonatṛṃśatā ca api cintāvyākulitaḥ bhavet
english: And a fire sacrifice (homa) to Sūrya (the Sun god) should be performed according to the prescribed rites by those desiring auspiciousness. Also, at the twenty-ninth (period or year), one would become greatly agitated by worries.

sanskrit: घृतवस्त्रसुवर्णानि तत्र दद्यात् विचक्षणः । त्रिंशता पूर्णधान्याप्तिरिति जातकनिर्णयः ॥२८॥
transliteration: ghṛtavastrasuvarṇāni tatra dadyāt vicakṣaṇaḥ . triṃśatā pūrṇadhānyāptiriti jātakanirṇayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: ghṛtavastrasuvarṇāni tatra dadyāt vicakṣaṇaḥtriṃśatā pūrṇadhānyāptiḥ iti jātakanirṇayaḥ
english: A discerning person (vicakṣaṇa) should offer ghee, clothes, and gold in such circumstances. The attainment of complete prosperity is indicated by (a score of) thirty – this is the determination in natal astrology (jātakanirṇaya).

sanskrit: त्रिंशाधिकामी रेखाभिर्धनपुत्रसुखाप्तयः । चत्वारिंशाधिकाभिश्च पुण्यश्रीरुपचीयते ॥२९॥
transliteration: triṃśādhikāmī rekhābhirdhanaputrasukhāptayaḥ . catvāriṃśādhikābhiśca puṇyaśrīrupacīyate.
separated_sandhi: triṃśādhikāḥ amī rekhābhiḥ dhanaputrasukhāptayaḥcatvāriṃśādhikābhiḥ ca puṇyaśrīḥ upacīyate
english: Those (scorings) that are more than thirty, through their specific points (rekhā), bring about the attainment of wealth, progeny, and happiness. And through those (scorings) exceeding forty, the prosperity of merit (puṇyaśrī) is increased.

sanskrit: अष्टवर्गेण ये शुद्धास्ते शुद्धाः सर्वकर्मसु । अतोऽष्टवर्गसंशुद्धिरनेष्या सर्वकर्मसु ॥३०॥
transliteration: aṣṭavargeṇa ye śuddhāste śuddhāḥ sarvakarmasu . ato'ṣṭavargasaṃśuddhiraneṣyā sarvakarmasu.
separated_sandhi: aṣṭavargeṇa ye śuddhāḥ te śuddhāḥ sarvakarmasuataḥ aṣṭavargasaṃśuddhiḥ aneṣyā sarvakarmasu
english: Those (indications) which are deemed auspicious through the aṣṭakavarga (system) are considered auspicious in all actions (karma). Therefore, the purification or auspiciousness (saṃśuddhi) of the aṣṭakavarga is indispensable for all actions (karma).

sanskrit: तावद्गोचरमन्वेष्यं यावन्न प्राप्यतेऽष्टकम् । अष्टवर्गे तु सम्प्राप्ते गोचरं विफलं भवेत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tāvadgocaramanveṣyaṃ yāvanna prāpyate'ṣṭakam . aṣṭavarge tu samprāpte gocaraṃ viphalaṃ bhavet.
separated_sandhi: tāvat gocaram anveṣyam yāvat na prāpyate aṣṭakamaṣṭavarge tu samprāpte gocaram viphalam bhavet
english: One should investigate planetary transits (gocara) only as long as the eightfold system (aṣṭaka) is not fully understood. But once the aṣṭakavarga is completely understood, the consideration of transits (gocara) becomes ineffective.

sanskrit: अथ रश्मीन् प्रवक्ष्यामि ग्रहाणां द्विजसत्तम । दिन् नवेष्विषुसप्ताष्टशराः स्वोच्चे करो रवेः ॥१॥
transliteration: atha raśmīn pravakṣyāmi grahāṇāṃ dvijasattama . din naveṣviṣusaptāṣṭaśarāḥ svocce karo raveḥ.
separated_sandhi: atha raśmīn pravakṣyāmi grahāṇām dvijasattama dinnaveṣu iṣu sapta aṣṭa śarāḥ svocce karaḥ raveḥ
english: Now, O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama), I will explain the rays of the planets. When they are in their exaltation (svocca), the rays are: ten for the Sun, nine for the Moon, five for Mars, seven for Mercury, eight for Jupiter, and five for Venus.

sanskrit: नीच खं चान्तरे प्रोक्ता रश्मयश्चानुपात्तः । नीचोनं तु ग्रहं भार्धाधिकं चक्राद्विशोधयेत् ॥२॥
transliteration: nīca khaṃ cāntare proktā raśmayaścānupāttaḥ . nīconaṃ tu grahaṃ bhārdhādhikaṃ cakrādviśodhayet.
separated_sandhi: nīca kham ca antare proktāḥ raśmayaḥ ca anupātaḥnīca-ūnam tu graham bhā-ardha-adhikam cakrāt viśodhayet
english: The rays are declared to be zero (kham) when a planet is in its debilitation (nīca) point, and proportional in between. One should subtract the debilitation point (nīca) from the planet's position. If this resulting arc is greater than half the zodiac (180 degrees), one should subtract it from the full zodiac (360 degrees).

sanskrit: स्वीयरश्मिहतं षड्भिर्भजेत् स्यू रश्मयः स्वकाः । अपरैरत्र संस्कारविशेषः कथितो यथा ॥३॥
transliteration: svīyaraśmihataṃ ṣaḍbhirbhajet syū raśmayaḥ svakāḥ . aparairatra saṃskāraviśeṣaḥ kathito yathā.
separated_sandhi: svīya-raśmi-hatam ṣaḍbhiḥ bhajet syuḥ raśmayaḥsvakāḥ aparaiḥ atra saṃskāra-viśeṣaḥ kathitaḥ yathā
english: One should multiply this (arc from debilitation) by the planet's own (maximum) rays (from the list in 141111) and then divide it by six (signs, i.e., 180 degrees). These would be its own calculated rays. Furthermore, a special modification (saṃskāra) is mentioned here by other (authorities), as follows.

sanskrit: स्वोच्चभे ते त्रिगुणिताः स्वत्रिकोणे द्विसंगुणाः । स्वमे त्रिघ्ना द्विसंभक्ता अधिमित्रगृहे तथा ॥४॥
transliteration: svoccabhe te triguṇitāḥ svatrikoṇe dvisaṃguṇāḥ . svame trighnā dvisaṃbhaktā adhimitragṛhe tathā.
separated_sandhi: sva-ucca-bhe te triguṇitāḥ sva-trikoṇe dvi-saṃguṇāḥsvame trighnāḥ dvi-saṃbhaktāḥ adhi-mitra-gṛhe tathā
english: Those (calculated rays) are multiplied by three when the planet is in its exaltation sign (svoccabha). They are multiplied by two in its own trine sign (svatrikona). When in its own sign (svakshetra), they are multiplied by three and divided by two. Likewise, the same applies when the planet is in the sign of an intimate friend (adhimitragṛha).

sanskrit: वेदघ्ना रामसंभक्ता मित्रमे षड्गुणास्ततः । पञ्चभक्तास्तथा शत्रुगृहे चेद् दलिताः कराः ॥५॥
transliteration: vedaghnā rāmasaṃbhaktā mitrame ṣaḍguṇāstataḥ . pañcabhaktāstathā śatrugṛhe ced dalitāḥ karāḥ.
separated_sandhi: veda-ghnāḥ rāma-saṃbhaktāḥ mitrame ṣaṭ-guṇāḥ tataḥpañca-bhaktāḥ tathā śatru-gṛhe cet dalitāḥ karāḥ
english: The planetary rays are multiplied by four and then divided by three when a planet is in a friendly house. Thereafter, they are multiplied by six and divided by five. Similarly, if the rays are in an enemy's house, they are reduced.

sanskrit: अधिशत्रुगृहे द्विघ्नाः पञ्चभक्ताः समे समाः । शनिशुक्रौ विना ताराग्रहा अस्ते गता यदि ॥६॥
transliteration: adhiśatrugṛhe dvighnāḥ pañcabhaktāḥ same samāḥ . śaniśukrau vinā tārāgrahā aste gatā yadi.
separated_sandhi: adhi-śatru-gṛhe dvi-ghnāḥ pañca-bhaktāḥ samesamāḥ śani-śukrau vinā tārā-grahāḥ aste gatāḥ yadi
english: If a planet is in an exceedingly inimical house, its rays are multiplied by two and then divided by five. If it is in a neutral house, its rays remain unchanged. If stellar planets (grahas), excluding Saturn (śani) and Venus (śukra), are combust (aste gatāḥ), then...

sanskrit: विरश्मयो भवन्त्येवं ज्ञेयाः स्पष्टकरा द्विज । रश्मियोगवशादेवं फलं वाच्यं विचक्षणैः ॥७॥
transliteration: viraśmayo bhavantyevaṃ jñeyāḥ spaṣṭakarā dvija . raśmiyogavaśādevaṃ phalaṃ vācyaṃ vicakṣaṇaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: vi-raśmayaḥ bhavanti evam jñeyāḥ spaṣṭa-karāḥ dvijaraśmi-yoga-vaśāt evam phalam vācyam vicakṣaṇaiḥ
english: If stellar planets (grahas), excluding Saturn (śani) and Venus (śukra), are combust, they become rayless. O twice-born (dvija), thus the specific rays should be known. In this way, discerning astrologers should declare the results based on the combined influence of these rays.

sanskrit: एकादि पञ्चपर्यन्तं रश्मिसंख्या भवेद्यदि । दरिद्रा दुःखसंतप्ता अपि जाताः कुलोत्तमे ॥८॥
transliteration: ekādi pañcaparyantaṃ raśmisaṃkhyā bhavedyadi . daridrā duḥkhasaṃtaptā api jātāḥ kulottame.
separated_sandhi: eka-ādi pañca-paryantam raśmi-saṃkhyā bhavet yadidaridrāḥ duḥkha-saṃtaptāḥ api jātāḥ kula-uttame
english: If the number of rays (raśmi-saṃkhyā) ranges from one to five, then even those born in a noble family (kula) become impoverished and afflicted by sorrow.

sanskrit: परतो दशकं यावत् निर्धना भारवासकाः । स्त्रीपुत्रगृहहीनाश्च जायन्ते मनुजा भुवि ॥९॥
transliteration: parato daśakaṃ yāvat nirdhanā bhāravāsakāḥ . strīputragṛhahīnāśca jāyante manujā bhuvi.
separated_sandhi: parataḥ daśakam yāvat nirdhanāḥ bhāravāsakāḥstrīputragṛhahīnāḥ ca jāyante manujāḥ bhuvi
english: When there are ten rays, human beings on earth become impoverished, burdened, and without wives, sons, or homes.

sanskrit: अकादशस्वल्पपुत्राः स्वल्पवित्ताश्च मानवाः । द्वादशस्वल्पवित्ताश्च धूर्ता मूर्खाश्च निर्बलाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: akādaśasvalpaputrāḥ svalpavittāśca mānavāḥ . dvādaśasvalpavittāśca dhūrtā mūrkhāśca nirbalāḥ.
separated_sandhi: ekādaśasu svalpaputrāḥ svalpavittāḥ ca mānavāḥdvādaśasu svalpavittāḥ ca dhūrtāḥ mūrkhāḥ ca nirbalāḥ
english: When there are eleven rays, human beings have few children and little wealth. When there are twelve rays, they possess little wealth, are cunning, foolish, and weak.

sanskrit: चौर्यकर्मरता नित्यं चेत् त्रयोदश रश्मयः । चतुर्दशसु धर्मात्मा कुटुम्बानां च पोषकाः ॥११॥
transliteration: cauryakarmaratā nityaṃ cet trayodaśa raśmayaḥ . caturdaśasu dharmātmā kuṭumbānāṃ ca poṣakāḥ.
separated_sandhi: cauryakarmaratāḥ nityam cet trayodaśa raśmayaḥcaturdaśasu dharmātmā kuṭumbānām ca poṣakāḥ
english: If there are thirteen rays, people are constantly engaged in acts of theft. When there are fourteen rays, they are virtuous (dharmātmā) and supporters of their families.

sanskrit: कुलोचितक्रियासक्तो धनविद्यासमन्वितः । रश्मिभिः पञ्चदशभिः सर्वविद्यागुणान्वितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: kulocitakriyāsakto dhanavidyāsamanvitaḥ . raśmibhiḥ pañcadaśabhiḥ sarvavidyāguṇānvitaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kulocitakriyāsaktaḥ dhanavidyāsammanvitaḥraśmibhiḥ pañcadaśabhiḥ sarvavidyāguṇānvitaḥ
english: With fifteen rays, one is engaged in actions appropriate to one's lineage, endowed with wealth and knowledge, and possesses all kinds of knowledge and virtues.

sanskrit: स्ववंशमुख्यो धनवानित्याह भगवान् विधिः । परतश्च कुलेशाना बहुभृत्या कुटुम्बिनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: svavaṃśamukhyo dhanavānityāha bhagavān vidhiḥ . parataśca kuleśānā bahubhṛtyā kuṭumbinaḥ.
separated_sandhi: sva-vaṃśa-mukhyaḥ dhanavān iti āha bhagavānvidhiḥ parataḥ ca kuleśānā bahu-bhṛtyā kuṭumbinaḥ
english: The revered Brahma (Bhagavān Vidhi) declares that such a person is the chief of their own lineage and wealthy. Furthermore, they will be lords over their family, possessing many servants and a large household.

sanskrit: कीर्तिमन्तो जनैः पूर्णाः सर्वे च सुखिनः क्रमात् । पञ्चाशज्जनपालश्चेदेकविंशतिरश्मयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kīrtimanto janaiḥ pūrṇāḥ sarve ca sukhinaḥ kramāt . pañcāśajjanapālaścedekaviṃśatiraśmayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kīrtimantaḥ janaiḥ pūrṇāḥ sarve ca sukhinaḥ kramātpañcāśat-jana-pālaḥ cet eka-viṃśatiḥ raśmayaḥ
english: They are all renowned, surrounded by people, and progressively attain happiness. If someone is a ruler (pāla) of fifty people, then there are twenty-one rays (raśmayaḥ) (associated with them/this condition).

sanskrit: दानशीलः कृपायुक्तो द्वाविंशतिसुरश्मिषु । सुखयुक् सौम्यशीलश्चेत् त्रयोविंशतिरश्मयः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dānaśīlaḥ kṛpāyukto dvāviṃśatisuraśmiṣu . sukhayuk saumyaśīlaścet trayoviṃśatiraśmayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: dāna-śīlaḥ kṛpā-yuktaḥ dvā-viṃśati-suraśmiṣusukha-yuk saumya-śīlaḥ cet trayo-viṃśatiḥ raśmayaḥ
english: When there are twenty-two divine rays, a person is generous (dāna-śīla) and compassionate. If a person is cheerful and of a gentle disposition, then there are twenty-three rays.

sanskrit: आत्रिंशत् परतः श्रीमान् सर्वसत्त्वसमन्वितः । राजप्रियश्च तेजस्वी जनैश्च बहुभिर्वृतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ātriṃśat parataḥ śrīmān sarvasattvasamanvitaḥ . rājapriyaśca tejasvī janaiśca bahubhirvṛtaḥ.
separated_sandhi: ā-triṃśat parataḥ śrīmān sarva-sattva-samanvitaḥrāja-priyaḥ ca tejasvī janaiḥ ca bahubhiḥ vṛtaḥ
english: From the thirtieth [ray/point/year] onwards, such a person becomes glorious, endowed with all good qualities (sattva), beloved by the king, radiant, and surrounded by many people.

sanskrit: अत ऊर्ध्वं तु सामन्तश्चत्वारिंशत् करावधि । जनानां शतमारब्य सहस्रावधिपोषकः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ata ūrdhvaṃ tu sāmantaścatvāriṃśat karāvadhi . janānāṃ śatamārabya sahasrāvadhipoṣakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: ataḥ ūrdhvam tu sāmantaḥ catvāriṃśat kara-avadhijanānām śatam ārabya sahasra-avadhi-poṣakaḥ
english: Above this (level), a feudal chief (sāmanta) governs up to forty units of military strength or administration (kara), and supports from a hundred up to a thousand people.

sanskrit: अत ऊर्ध्वन्तु भूपालः पंचाशत् करिणावधि । तत ऊर्ध्वकरैर्विप्र चक्रवर्ती नृपो भवेत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ata ūrdhvantu bhūpālaḥ paṃcāśat kariṇāvadhi . tata ūrdhvakarairvipra cakravartī nṛpo bhavet.
separated_sandhi: ataḥ ūrdhvam tu bhūpālaḥ pañcāśat kariṇā-avadhitataḥ ūrdhva-karaiḥ vipra cakravartī nṛpaḥ bhavet
english: Above this (level), a king (bhūpāla) commands up to fifty elephants. O Brahmin (vipra), one with more "kara" (units of military strength or revenue) than that becomes a universal monarch (cakravartī).

sanskrit: एवं प्रसूतिकालोत्थनभोगकरसम्भवम् । कुलक्रमनुसारेण जातकस्य फलं वदेत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: evaṃ prasūtikālotthanabhogakarasambhavam . kulakramanusāreṇa jātakasya phalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: evam prasūti-kāla-utthana-bhoga-kara-sambhavamkula-krama-anusāreṇa jātakasya phalam vadet
english: Thus, one should declare the results (phalam) for the native (jātaka), according to family tradition, based on what is produced by the experiences (bhoga) and influences (kara) arising at the time of birth.

sanskrit: क्षत्रियश्चक्रवर्ती वैश्यो राजा प्रजायते । शूद्रश्च सधनो विप्रो यज्ञकर्मक्रियारतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: kṣatriyaścakravartī vaiśyo rājā prajāyate . śūdraśca sadhano vipro yajñakarmakriyārataḥ.
separated_sandhi: kṣatriyaḥ cakravartī vaiśyaḥ rājā prajāyate śūdraḥca sa-dhanaḥ vipraḥ yajña-karma-kriyā-rataḥ
english: A Kṣatriya becomes a universal monarch (cakravartī), and a Vaiśya becomes a king. A Śūdra becomes wealthy, and a Brahmin (vipra) becomes devoted to the actions (kriyā) of Vedic ritual (yajña) and ritual (karma).

sanskrit: उच्चाभिमुखखेटस्य कराः पुष्टफलप्रदाः । नीचाभिमुखखेटस्य ततो न्यूनफलप्र्दाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: uccābhimukhakheṭasya karāḥ puṣṭaphalapradāḥ . nīcābhimukhakheṭasya tato nyūnaphalaprdāḥ.
separated_sandhi: uccābhimukhakheṭasya karāḥ puṣṭaphalapradāḥnīcābhimukhakheṭasya tataḥ nyūnaphalapradāḥ
english: The rays of a planet facing its exaltation sign yield strong and beneficial results. Conversely, the rays of a planet facing its debilitation sign give lesser results.

sanskrit: सर्वेषामेव खेटानामेवं रश्मिवशाद्द्विज । शुभं वाऽप्यशुभं चापि फलं भवति देहिनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sarveṣāmeva kheṭānāmevaṃ raśmivaśāddvija . śubhaṃ vā'pyaśubhaṃ cāpi phalaṃ bhavati dehinām.
separated_sandhi: sarveṣām eva kheṭānām evam raśmivaśāt dvija śubhamvā api aśubham ca api phalam bhavati dehinām
english: O twice-born (dvija), thus, it is by the power of the rays of all planets that either auspicious or inauspicious results manifest for embodied beings.

sanskrit: रश्मिज्ञानं विना सम्यक् न फलं ज्ञातुमर्हति । तस्माद्रश्मीन् प्रसाध्यैव फलं वाच्यं विचक्षणैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: raśmijñānaṃ vinā samyak na phalaṃ jñātumarhati . tasmādraśmīn prasādhyaiva phalaṃ vācyaṃ vicakṣaṇaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: raśmijñānam vinā samyak na phalam jñātum arhatitasmāt raśmīn prasādhya eva phalam vācyam vicakṣaṇaiḥ
english: Without a thorough understanding of the planetary rays, one cannot correctly ascertain the results. Therefore, the wise, having precisely calculated these rays, should declare the outcomes.

sanskrit: अथोच्यते मया विप्र रहस्यं ज्ञानमुत्तमम् । जगतामुपकाराय यत् प्रोक्तं ब्रह्मण स्वयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: athocyate mayā vipra rahasyaṃ jñānamuttamam . jagatāmupakārāya yat proktaṃ brahmaṇa svayam.
separated_sandhi: atha ucyate mayā vipra rahasyam jñānam uttamamjagatām upakārāya yat proktam brahmaṇā svayam
english: Now, O Brahmin (vipra), an excellent and secret knowledge (jñāna) is being declared by me, which was spoken by Brahmā himself for the benefit of all beings.

sanskrit: चक्रं सुदर्शनं नाम यद्वशात् प्रस्फुटं फलम् । नृणां तन्वादिभावानां ज्ञातुं शक्नोति दैववित् ॥२॥
transliteration: cakraṃ sudarśanaṃ nāma yadvaśāt prasphuṭaṃ phalam . nṛṇāṃ tanvādibhāvānāṃ jñātuṃ śaknoti daivavit.
separated_sandhi: cakram sudarśanam nāma yat vaśāt prasphuṭam phalamnṛṇām tanvādibhāvānām jñātum śaknoti daivavit
english: An astrologer (daivavit) can understand the clear and distinct results concerning humans, specifically regarding their natal houses (bhāvas) starting from the Ascendant (tanu), by means of the cakra (wheel) named Sudarśana.

sanskrit: जन्मतो मृत्युपर्यन्तं वर्षमासदिनोद्भवम् । शुभं वाऽप्यशुभं सर्वं तच्छृणुष्वैकमानसः ॥३॥
transliteration: janmato mṛtyuparyantaṃ varṣamāsadinodbhavam . śubhaṃ vā'pyaśubhaṃ sarvaṃ tacchṛṇuṣvaikamānasaḥ.
separated_sandhi: janmataḥ mṛtyuparyantam varṣamāsadinodbhavam śubhamvā api aśubham sarvam tat śṛṇuṣva ekamānasaḥ
english: Now, listen with complete focus (ekamānasa) to all the results, whether auspicious or inauspicious, that manifest over years, months, and days, spanning from birth until death.

sanskrit: एककेन्द्रोद्भवं रम्यं लिखेद् वृत्तत्रयं द्विज । द्वादशारं च तत् कुर्याद् भवेदेवं सुदर्शनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ekakendrodbhavaṃ ramyaṃ likhed vṛttatrayaṃ dvija . dvādaśāraṃ ca tat kuryād bhavedevaṃ sudarśanam.
separated_sandhi: ekakendrodbhavam ramyam likhet vṛttatrayam dvijadvādaśāram ca tat kuryāt bhavet evam sudarśanam
english: O twice-born (dvija), one should draw three beautiful circles originating from a single center. And one should make that (diagram) with twelve spokes; in this way, it becomes the Sudarśana (cakra).

sanskrit: तत्राद्यवृत्ते लग्नाद्या भावा लेख्याः सखेचराः । तदूर्ध्ववृत्ते चन्द्राच्च भवाः खेटसमन्विताः ॥५॥
transliteration: tatrādyavṛtte lagnādyā bhāvā lekhyāḥ sakhecarāḥ . tadūrdhvavṛtte candrācca bhavāḥ kheṭasamanvitāḥ.
separated_sandhi: tatra ādyavṛtte lagnādyāḥ bhāvāḥ lekhyāḥ sakhecarāḥtat ūrdhvavṛtte candrāt ca bhavāḥ kheṭasamannitāḥ
english: Therein, in the innermost circle (ādya-vṛtta), the astrological houses (bhāvas) beginning with the Ascendant (lagna) should be inscribed along with their respective planets. And in the circle above that (tadūrdhavṛtta), the houses originating from the Moon should be written, also accompanied by their planets.

sanskrit: तदूर्ध्ववृत्ते सूर्याच्च क्रमात् भवा ग्रहान्विताः । एवमेकैकभावेऽत्र भवेद्भानां त्रयं त्रयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tadūrdhvavṛtte sūryācca kramāt bhavā grahānvitāḥ . evamekaikabhāve'tra bhavedbhānāṃ trayaṃ trayam.
separated_sandhi: tadūrdhvavṛtte sūryāt ca kramāt bhavāḥ grahānvitāḥ| evam ekaikabhāve atra bhavet bhānām trayam trayam
english: In the subsequent zodiacal circle, the houses (bhāvas) associated with planets are determined successively from the Sun's position. Thus, for each individual house (bhāva) here, there will be three divisions (bhānas) each.

sanskrit: अत्र तु प्रथमो भावो लग्नेन्दुरविभिर्युतः । तं प्रकल्प्य तनुं त्वग्रे ज्ञेया भावा धनादयः ॥७॥
transliteration: atra tu prathamo bhāvo lagnenduravibhiryutaḥ . taṃ prakalpya tanuṃ tvagre jñeyā bhāvā dhanādayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: atra tu prathamaḥ bhāvaḥ lagnenduravibhiḥ yutaḥ |tam prakalpya tanum tu agre jñeyāḥ bhāvāḥ dhanādayaḥ
english: Here, the first house (bhāva) is understood to be associated with the Ascendant (lagna), the Moon (indu), and the Sun (ravi). Establishing that (first house) as the Ascendant (tanu), the subsequent houses (bhāvas) like wealth (dhana) and others should then be known.

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र ग्रहस्थित्या ज्ञेयं तत्तत्फलं बुधैः । तनुभावे शुभः सूर्यो ज्ञेयोऽन्यत्राशुभप्रदः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatra tatra grahasthityā jñeyaṃ tattatphalaṃ budhaiḥ . tanubhāve śubhaḥ sūryo jñeyo'nyatrāśubhapradaḥ.
separated_sandhi: tatra tatra grahasthityā jñeyam tattatphalam budhaiḥ| tanubhāve śubhaḥ sūryaḥ jñeyaḥ anyatra aśubhapradhaḥ
english: The wise (budhaiḥ) should know the respective results in each house by the position of planets. In the Ascendant house (tanu bhāva), the Sun (sūrya) is known to be auspicious; elsewhere, it gives inauspicious results.

sanskrit: पापोऽपि स्वोच्चराशिस्थो न भवत्यशुभप्रदः । एवं शुभाऽशुभं दृष्ट्वा तत्तद्भावफलं वदेत् ॥९॥
transliteration: pāpo'pi svoccarāśistho na bhavatyaśubhapradaḥ . evaṃ śubhā'śubhaṃ dṛṣṭvā tattadbhāvaphalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: pāpaḥ api svoccarāśisthaḥ na bhavati aśubhapradhaḥ| evam śubhāśubham dṛṣṭvā tattadbhāvapahalam vadet
english: Even a malefic planet, when situated in its exaltation sign, does not give inauspicious results. Thus, having observed the auspicious and inauspicious factors, one should declare the results of the respective houses.

sanskrit: यो भावः स्वामिसौम्याभ्यां युक्तो दृष्टोऽयमेधते । पापैर्दृष्टो युतो यो वा तस्य हानिः प्रजायते ॥१०॥
transliteration: yo bhāvaḥ svāmisaumyābhyāṃ yukto dṛṣṭo'yamedhate . pāpairdṛṣṭo yuto yo vā tasya hāniḥ prajāyate.
separated_sandhi: yaḥ bhāvaḥ svāmisaumyābhyām yuktaḥ dṛṣṭaḥ ayam edhate| pāpaiḥ dṛṣṭaḥ yutaḥ yaḥ vā tasya hāniḥ prajāyate
english: Whichever astrological house (bhāva) is associated with or aspected by its lord and benefics, that house thrives. Conversely, whichever house is aspected by or associated with malefics, its decline occurs.

sanskrit: ज्ञेयं सग्रहभावस्य ग्रहयोगसमं फलम् । अग्रहस्य तु भावस्य ग्रहदृष्टिसमं फलम् ॥११॥
transliteration: jñeyaṃ sagrahabhāvasya grahayogasamaṃ phalam . agrahasya tu bhāvasya grahadṛṣṭisamaṃ phalam.
separated_sandhi: jñeyam sagrahabhāvasya grahayogasamaṃ phalam| agrahasya tu bhāvasya grahadṛṣṭisamaṃ phalam
english: The result for an astrological house (bhāva) that contains planets should be understood as equivalent to the combination of those planets. However, for a house that does not contain planets, the result should be understood as equivalent to the aspects (dṛṣṭi) of planets on it.

sanskrit: शुभैरेव शुभं पापैर्शुभं मिश्रखेचरैः । शुभाधिके शुभं ज्ञेयमशुभं त्वशुभाधिके ॥१२॥
transliteration: śubhaireva śubhaṃ pāpairśubhaṃ miśrakhecaraiḥ . śubhādhike śubhaṃ jñeyamaśubhaṃ tvaśubhādhike.
separated_sandhi: śubhaiḥ eva śubham pāpaiḥ aśubham miśrakhecaraiḥ| śubhādhike śubham jñeyam aśubham tu aśubhādhike
english: Only auspicious results are to be expected from benefics, inauspicious results from malefics, and mixed results from a combination of planets. Auspicious results should be known when benefics are dominant, and inauspicious results when malefics are dominant.

sanskrit: एवं भावेषु खेटानां योगं दृष्टिं विलोक्य च । तारतम्येन वाच्यानि फलानि द्विजसत्तम ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṃ bhāveṣu kheṭānāṃ yogaṃ dṛṣṭiṃ vilokya ca . tāratamyena vācyāni phalāni dvijasattama.
separated_sandhi: evam bhāveṣu kheṭānām yogam dṛṣṭim vilokya ca| tāratamyena vācyāni phalāni dvijasattama
english: Thus, O best among the twice-born (dvija-sattama), observing the conjunctions and aspects of planets in the various houses, the results should be declared proportionally.

sanskrit: यत्र नैव ग्रहः कश्चिन्न दृष्टि कस्यचिद् भवेत् । तदा तद्भावजं ज्ञेयं तत्स्वामिवशतः फलम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yatra naiva grahaḥ kaścinna dṛṣṭi kasyacid bhavet . tadā tadbhāvajaṃ jñeyaṃ tatsvāmivaśataḥ phalam.
separated_sandhi: yatra na eva grahaḥ kaścit na dṛṣṭiḥ kasyacit bhavettadā tatbhāvajaṃ jñeyam tatsvāmivaśataḥ phalam
english: If there is no planet in a house (bhāva) and no planet aspects it, then the outcome (phalam) related to that house should be understood based on the influence of its lord.

sanskrit: शुभोऽपि शुभवर्गेषु ह्यधिकेष्वशुभप्रदः । पापोऽपि शुभवर्गेषु ह्यधिकेषु शुभप्रदः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śubho'pi śubhavargeṣu hyadhikeṣvaśubhapradaḥ . pāpo'pi śubhavargeṣu hyadhikeṣu śubhapradaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śubhaḥ api śubhavargeṣu hi adhikeṣu aśubhapradaḥpāpaḥ api śubhavargeṣu hi adhikeṣu śubhapradaḥ
english: Even a benefic planet, though situated in an abundance of auspicious divisions (vargas), yields inauspicious results. Conversely, even a malefic planet, when in an abundance of auspicious divisions, yields auspicious results.

sanskrit: स्वभोच्चस्य शुभस्यात्र वर्गा ज्ञेयाः शुभवाहाः । शत्रोः क्रूरस्य नीचस्य षड्वर्गा अशुभप्रदाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: svabhoccasya śubhasyātra vargā jñeyāḥ śubhavāhāḥ . śatroḥ krūrasya nīcasya ṣaḍvargā aśubhapradāḥ.
separated_sandhi: svabhoccasya śubhasya atra vargāḥ jñeyāḥ śubhavāhāḥśatroḥ krūrasya nīcasya ṣaḍvargāḥ aśubhapradaḥ
english: Here, the divisional strengths (vargas) of a planet in its own sign or exaltation, and those of an auspicious planet, are to be understood as bringing forth auspiciousness. Conversely, the ṣaḍvargas (six divisions) of an inimical, malefic, or debilitated planet produce inauspicious results.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वेषु खेटेषु भवेष्वपि द्विजोत्तम । शुभाशुभत्वं सञ्चिन्य्त ततस्तत्फलमादिशेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṃ sarveṣu kheṭeṣu bhaveṣvapi dvijottama . śubhāśubhatvaṃ sañcinyta tatastatphalamādiśet.
separated_sandhi: evaṃ sarveṣu kheṭeṣu bhaveṣu api dvijottamaśubhāśubhatvaṃ sañcintya tatas tatphalam ādiśet
english: O best among the twice-born (dvijottama)! In this manner, having thoroughly considered the auspicious and inauspicious natures in all planets and houses, one should then declare their outcomes.

sanskrit: यदा सुदर्शनादेव फलं सिद्ध्यति देहनाम् । तदा किं मुनिभिः सर्वैर्लग्नादेव फलं स्मृतम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yadā sudarśanādeva phalaṃ siddhyati dehanām . tadā kiṃ munibhiḥ sarvairlagnādeva phalaṃ smṛtam.
separated_sandhi: yadā sudarśanāt eva phalam siddhyati dehinām |tadā kim munibhiḥ sarvaiḥ lagnāt eva phalam smṛtam
english: If results for embodied beings are achieved solely from Sudarshana, then why has the result been taught by all sages to be derived solely from the ascendant (Lagna)?

sanskrit: इति म संशयो जातस्तं छेत्तुमर्हति । पृथग्भगौ यदाऽर्केन्दू लग्नादन्यत्र संस्थितौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: iti ma saṃśayo jātastaṃ chettumarhati . pṛthagbhagau yadā'rkendū lagnādanyatra saṃsthitau.
separated_sandhi: iti me saṃśayaḥ jātaḥ tam chettum arhati |pṛthakbhagau yadā arka-indū lagnāt anyatra saṃsthitau
english: Thus, this doubt has arisen in me, and you ought to dispel it. When the Sun (arka) and Moon (indu) are situated separately from the ascendant (Lagna) in different houses,

sanskrit: तदा सुदर्शनाच्चक्रात् फलं वाच्यं विचक्षणैः । एकभे द्वौ त्रयो वा चेत् तदा लग्नात् फलं वदेत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tadā sudarśanāccakrāt phalaṃ vācyaṃ vicakṣaṇaiḥ . ekabhe dvau trayo vā cet tadā lagnāt phalaṃ vadet.
separated_sandhi: tadā sudarśanāt chakrāt phalam vācyam vicakṣaṇaiḥ |ekabhe dvau trayaḥ vā cet tadā lagnāt phalam vadet
english: Then, results should be declared by the discerning ones from the Sudarshana (chakra). However, if two or three (planets) are in one house, then one should declare results from the ascendant (Lagna).

sanskrit: अथ विप्र प्रवक्ष्येऽहं प्रतिवर्षादिजं फलम् । अस्मात् सुदर्शनादेव दशान्तरदशावशात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: atha vipra pravakṣye'haṃ prativarṣādijaṃ phalam . asmāt sudarśanādeva daśāntaradaśāvaśāt.
separated_sandhi: atha vipra pravakṣye aham prativarṣādijam phalam| asmāt sudarśanāt eva daśāntaradaśāvaśāt
english: Now, O Brahmin, I will declare the annual results (phala) originating from this very Sudarshana (chakra), specifically through the influence of the planetary periods (daśā) and sub-periods (antaradaśā).

sanskrit: तन्वाद्यैर्वर्षमास्सार्धद्विकधस्रान् प्रवर्तयेत् । भवेशादिद्वादशानां दशा वर्षेषु कल्पयेत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tanvādyairvarṣamāssārdhadvikadhasrān pravartayet . bhaveśādidvādaśānāṃ daśā varṣeṣu kalpayet.
separated_sandhi: tanu-ādyaiḥ varṣamāsa-sārdhadvikadhasrān pravartayet| bhāva-īśa-ādi-dvādaśānām daśā varṣeṣu kalpayet
english: Starting from the ascendant (lagna) and other houses, one should calculate the two-and-a-half-day durations for the year-months. One should also determine the planetary periods (daśā) in terms of years for the twelve lords of houses, beginning with the lord of the first house.

sanskrit: तदाद्यन्तर्दशास्तद्वन्मासादौ तद्बलैः फलम् । तं तं भावं प्रकल्प्याङ्कं तत्तत्तन्वादिजं द्विज ॥२३॥
transliteration: tadādyantardaśāstadvanmāsādau tadbalaiḥ phalam . taṃ taṃ bhāvaṃ prakalpyāṅkaṃ tattattanvādijaṃ dvija.
separated_sandhi: tad-ādi-antardaśāḥ tadvat māsa-ādau tad-balaiḥ phalam |tam tam bhāvam prakalpya aṅkam tat-tat-tanu-ādi-jam dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija), similarly, one should calculate the sub-periods (antardaśā) starting from the main period, from the beginning of the months. The results (phalam) are determined by the strengths of those (planets). Having specifically considered each house (bhāva) and performed a calculation (aṅka), one should determine that which originates from the respective ascendant (lagna) and so on.

sanskrit: तत् तल्लग्नात् केन्द्रकोणाष्टमे सौम्याः शुभप्रदाः । यत्र भावे सैंहिकेयो भवेत् तद्भावहानिकृत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tat tallagnāt kendrakoṇāṣṭame saumyāḥ śubhapradāḥ . yatra bhāve saiṃhikeyo bhavet tadbhāvahānikṛt.
separated_sandhi: tat tat-lagnāt kendra-koṇa-aṣṭame saumyāḥ śubha-pradāḥ| yatra bhāve saiṃhikeyaḥ bhavet tad-bhāva-hāni-kṛt
english: Benefic planets (saumya), when located in the angular (kendra), trine (koṇa), or eighth houses from the respective ascendant (lagna), bestow auspicious results. Wherever Rāhu (saiṃhikeya) is present in a house (bhāva), he causes harm to that specific house.

sanskrit: पापा वा यत्र बहवस्तत्तद्भावविनाशनम् । विरिष्फारिशुभैः पापैस्त्रिषडायस्थितैः शुभम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: pāpā vā yatra bahavastattadbhāvavināśanam . viriṣphāriśubhaiḥ pāpaistriṣaḍāyasthitaiḥ śubham.
separated_sandhi: pāpāḥ vā yatra bahavaḥ tat-tat-bhāva-vināśanam |vi-riṣpha-ari-śubhaiḥ pāpaiḥ tri-ṣaṭ-āya-sthitaiḥ śubham
english: Where many malefic planets (pāpa) are present in a house (bhāva), that house (bhāva) is ruined. Auspicious results (śubham) occur due to benefic planets (śubha) located in the 6th (ari) and 12th (vyaya) houses, and due to malefic planets (pāpa) situated in the 3rd (tri), 6th (ṣaṭ), and 11th (āya) houses.

sanskrit: एवं प्रत्यब्दमासादौ भावानां फलचिन्तनम् । द्वादशानां दशाऽवृत्त्या दशाश्चायुषि चिन्तयेत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: evaṃ pratyabdamāsādau bhāvānāṃ phalacintanam . dvādaśānāṃ daśā'vṛttyā daśāścāyuṣi cintayet.
separated_sandhi: evaṃ pratyabdam māsa-ādau bhāvānām phala-cintanamdvādaśānām daśā-āvṛttyā daśāḥ ca āyuṣi cintayet
english: In this way, one should analyze the results related to the twelve astrological houses (bhāvas) for each year, month, and so on. One should also consider the daśās (planetary periods) throughout the lifespan, by their cyclical recurrence.

sanskrit: एवं सुदर्शनाच्चक्राद् वर्षमासादिजं फलम् । ज्ञात्वा तथाष्टवर्गोत्थमुभाभ्यां फलनिर्णयः ॥२७॥
transliteration: evaṃ sudarśanāccakrād varṣamāsādijaṃ phalam . jñātvā tathāṣṭavargotthamubhābhyāṃ phalanirṇayaḥ.
separated_sandhi: evaṃ sudarśanāt cakrāt varṣa-māsa-ādi-jam phalam jñātvātathā aṣṭavarga-utthitam ubhābhyām phala-nirṇayaḥ
english: Thus, having ascertained the results generated from the Sudarśana Chakra concerning yearly, monthly, and other time periods, and similarly those arising from the Aṣṭakavarga system, the determination of results (phala) should be made from both approaches.

sanskrit: उभयत्र समत्वे हि सम्पूर्णं तत् फलं वदेत् । विषमत्वे यदाधिक्यं तत्फलं च बलक्रमात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: ubhayatra samatve hi sampūrṇaṃ tat phalaṃ vadet . viṣamatve yadādhikyaṃ tatphalaṃ ca balakramāt.
separated_sandhi: ubhayatra samatve hi sampūrṇam tat phalam vadetviṣamatve yat ādhikyam tat phalam ca balakramāt
english: Indeed, if there is equality in both (systems), one should declare that result (phala) to be complete. If there is inequality, then that result which has a preponderance, based on the order of strength, should be considered.

sanskrit: अथ वक्ष्याम्यहं पञ्चमहापुरुषलक्षणम् । स्वभोच्चगतकेन्द्रस्थैर्बलिभिश्च कुजादिभिः ॥१॥
transliteration: atha vakṣyāmyahaṃ pañcamahāpuruṣalakṣaṇam . svabhoccagatakendrasthairbalibhiśca kujādibhiḥ.
separated_sandhi: atha vakṣyāmi aham pañca-mahāpuruṣa-lakṣaṇamsva-bha-ucca-gata-kendra-sthaiḥ balibhiḥ ca kuja-ādibhiḥ
english: Now I (ahaṃ) will explain the characteristics of the five Mahāpuruṣa (great person) Yogas, which are formed when Mars (kuja) and other planets (Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn) are strong (balibhiḥ), situated in their own signs or exaltation signs, and located in angular houses (kendra).

sanskrit: क्रमशो रुचको भद्रो हंसो मालव्य एव च । शशश्चैते बुधैः सर्वैर्महान्तः पुरुषाः स्मृताः ॥२॥
transliteration: kramaśo rucako bhadro haṃso mālavya eva ca . śaśaścaite budhaiḥ sarvairmahāntaḥ puruṣāḥ smṛtāḥ.
separated_sandhi: kramaśaḥ rucakaḥ bhadraḥ haṃsaḥ mālavyaḥ eva caśaśaḥ ca ete budhaiḥ sarvaiḥ mahāntaḥ puruṣāḥ smṛtāḥ
english: In order, Ruchaka, Bhadra, Haṃsa, Mālavya, and Śaśa - these are considered great persons (puruṣa) by all scholars.

sanskrit: दीर्घाननो महोत्साहो स्वच्छकान्तिर्महाबलः । चारुभ्रूर्नीलकेशश्च सुरुचिश्च रणप्रियः ॥३॥
transliteration: dīrghānano mahotsāho svacchakāntirmahābalaḥ . cārubhrūrnīlakeśaśca suruciśca raṇapriyaḥ.
separated_sandhi: dīrghānanaḥ mahotsāhaḥ svacchakāntiḥ mahābalaḥcārubhrūḥ nīlakeśaḥ ca suruciḥ ca raṇapriyaḥ
english: He has a long face, great enthusiasm, a pure complexion, immense strength, beautiful eyebrows, dark hair, is very agreeable/handsome, and is fond of battle.

sanskrit: रक्तश्यामोऽरिहन्ता च मन्त्रविच्चोरनायकः । क्रूरोभर्ता मनुष्याणां क्षामाऽङ्घ्रिर्द्विजपूजकः ॥४॥
transliteration: raktaśyāmo'rihantā ca mantraviccoranāyakaḥ . krūrobhartā manuṣyāṇāṃ kṣāmā'ṅghrirdvijapūjakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: raktaśyāmaḥ arihantā ca mantravit ca coranāyakaḥkrūraḥ bhartā manuṣyāṇām kṣāmāṅghriḥ dvijapūjakaḥ
english: He is reddish-dark, a destroyer of enemies, a knower of sacred formulas (mantra), a chief of thieves, a cruel master of men, has lean feet, and reveres the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: वीणावज्रधनुःपाशवृषचक्राङ्कितः करे । मन्त्राभिचारकुशली दैर्ध्ये चैव शतांगुलः ॥५॥
transliteration: vīṇāvajradhanuḥpāśavṛṣacakrāṅkitaḥ kare . mantrābhicārakuśalī dairdhye caiva śatāṃgulaḥ.
separated_sandhi: vīṇāvajradhanuṣpāśavṛṣacakrāṅkitaḥ karemantrābhicārakuśalin dairghye ca eva śatāṅgulaḥ
english: His hand is marked with the signs of a vīṇā, a vajra, a bow, a noose, a bull, and a discus. He is skilled in sacred formulas (mantra) and black magic (abhicāra), and is one hundred angulas tall.

sanskrit: मुखदैर्घ्यसमं मध्यं तस्य विज्ञैः प्रकीर्तितम् । तुल्यस्तुलासहस्रेण रुचको द्विजपुङ्गव ॥६॥
transliteration: mukhadairghyasamaṃ madhyaṃ tasya vijñaiḥ prakīrtitam . tulyastulāsahasreṇa rucako dvijapuṅgava.
separated_sandhi: mukhadairghyasamam madhyam tasya vijñaiḥ prakīrtitamtulyaḥ tulāsahasreṇa rucakaḥ dvijapuṅgava
english: Experts declare that his mid-section is equal in length to his face. O best among *dvijas* (dvijapuṅgava), a *Rucaka* [individual] possesses strength equivalent to a thousand *tulās*.

sanskrit: भुनक्ति विन्ध्यसह्याद्रिप्रदेशं सप्ततिं समाः । शत्रेण वह्निना वापि स प्रयाति सुरालयम् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhunakti vindhyasahyādripradeśaṃ saptatiṃ samāḥ . śatreṇa vahninā vāpi sa prayāti surālayam.
separated_sandhi: bhunakti vindhyasahyādripradeśam saptatim samāḥśatreṇa vahninā vā api sa prayāti surālayam
english: He rules the region of the Vindhya and Sahya mountains for seventy years. Then, he attains the abode of the gods (heaven), either by an enemy or by fire.

sanskrit: शार्दूलप्रतिभह् पीनवक्षा गजगतिः पुमान् । पीनाजानुभुजः प्राज्ञश्चतुरस्रश्च योगवित् ॥८॥
transliteration: śārdūlapratibhah pīnavakṣā gajagatiḥ pumān . pīnājānubhujaḥ prājñaścaturasraśca yogavit.
separated_sandhi: śārdūlapratibhaḥ pīnavakṣā gajagatiḥ pumānpīnājānubhujaḥ prājñaḥ caturasraḥ ca yogavit
english: He is a man resembling a tiger, with a stout chest and an elephant-like gait. He has stout knees and arms, is wise, well-proportioned, and an expert in disciplines (yoga).

sanskrit: सात्त्विकः शोभनांघ्रश्च शोभनश्मश्रुसंयुतः । कामी शङ्खगदाचक्रशरकुञ्जरचिह्नकैः ॥९॥
transliteration: sāttvikaḥ śobhanāṃghraśca śobhanaśmaśrusaṃyutaḥ . kāmī śaṅkhagadācakraśarakuñjaracihnakaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: sāttvikaḥ śobhanāṅghriḥ ca śobhanaśmaśrusaṃyutaḥkāmī śaṅkhagadācakraśarakuñjaracihnakaiḥ
english: He is of a pure (sāttvika) nature, possesses beautiful feet, and is endowed with a beautiful moustache. He is passionate and marked with the signs of a conch, mace, discus, arrow, and elephant.

sanskrit: ध्वजलाङ्गलचिह्नैश्च चिह्नितांघ्रिकराम्बुजः । सुनासश्शास्त्रविद् धीरः कृष्णाकुञ्चितकेशभृत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dhvajalāṅgalacihnaiśca cihnitāṃghrikarāmbujaḥ . sunāsaśśāstravid dhīraḥ kṛṣṇākuñcitakeśabhṛt.
separated_sandhi: dhvajalāṅgalacihnaiḥ ca cihnitāṅghrikara-ambujaḥsunāsaḥ śāstravit dhīraḥ kṛṣṇākuñcitakeśabhṛt
english: He whose lotus-like feet and hands are marked with the signs of a flag and a plough, who possesses a beautiful nose, is learned in the scriptures, courageous, and has dark, curly hair.

sanskrit: स्वतन्त्रः सर्वकार्येषु स्वजनप्रीणनक्षमः । ऐश्वर्यं भुज्यते चास्य नित्यं मित्रजनैः परैः ॥११॥
transliteration: svatantraḥ sarvakāryeṣu svajanaprīṇanakṣamaḥ . aiśvaryaṃ bhujyate cāsya nityaṃ mitrajanaiḥ paraiḥ.
separated_sandhi: svatantraḥ sarvakāryeṣu svajanaprīṇanakṣamaḥaiśvaryam bhujyate ca asya nityam mitrajanaaiḥ paraiḥ
english: He is independent in all his actions, capable of gratifying his own people. And prosperity is always enjoyed by his excellent friends.

sanskrit: तुलया तुलितो भारप्रमितः स्त्रीसुतान्वितः । सक्षेमो भूपतिः पाति मध्यदेशं शतं समाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tulayā tulito bhārapramitaḥ strīsutānvitaḥ . sakṣemo bhūpatiḥ pāti madhyadeśaṃ śataṃ samāḥ.
separated_sandhi: tulayā tulitaḥ bhārapramitaḥ strīsutānvitaḥsakṣemaḥ bhūpatiḥ pāti madhyadeśam śatam samāḥ
english: He is measured by a balance, weighed by a certain measure, accompanied by his wife and son. This secure king protects the central region for a hundred years.

sanskrit: हंसो हंसस्वरो गौरः सुमुखोन्नतनासिकः । श्लेष्मलो मधुपिङ्गाक्षो रक्तवर्णनखः सुधीः ॥१३॥
transliteration: haṃso haṃsasvaro gauraḥ sumukhonnatanāsikaḥ . śleṣmalo madhupiṅgākṣo raktavarṇanakhaḥ sudhīḥ.
separated_sandhi: haṃsaḥ haṃsasvaraḥ gauraḥ sumukhaḥ unnatanāsikaḥśleṣmalaḥ madhupiṅgākṣaḥ raktavarṇanakhaḥ sudhīḥ
english: He resembles a swan, possesses a swan-like voice, is fair-complexioned, and has a beautiful, prominent nose. He is phlegmatic (of kapha nature), has honey-yellow eyes, blood-red nails, and is intelligent.

sanskrit: पीनगण्डस्थलो वृत्तशिराः सुचरणो नृपः । मत्स्याऽङ्कुशधनुःशंखकञ्जखट्वाङ्गचिह्नकैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: pīnagaṇḍasthalo vṛttaśirāḥ sucaraṇo nṛpaḥ . matsyā'ṅkuśadhanuḥśaṃkhakañjakhaṭvāṅgacihnakaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: pīṇagaṇḍasthalaḥ vṛttaśirāḥ sucaraṇaḥ nṛpaḥmatsyāṅkuśadhanuḥśaṅkhakañjakhaṭvāṅgacihnakaiḥ
english: A king will have plump cheeks, a round head, and beautiful feet. His hands and feet will be marked with the signs of a fish, a goad, a bow, a conch, a lotus, and a *khaṭvāṅga*.

sanskrit: चिह्नतांघ्रिकरः स्त्रीषु कामार्तो नैति तुष्टताम् । षण्ण्वत्यंगुलो दैर्घ्ये जलक्रीडारतः सुखी ॥१५॥
transliteration: cihnatāṃghrikaraḥ strīṣu kāmārto naiti tuṣṭatām . ṣaṇṇvatyaṃgulo dairghye jalakrīḍārataḥ sukhī.
separated_sandhi: cihnatāṁghrikaraḥ strīṣu kāmārtaḥ na eti tuṣṭatāmṣaṇṇavatyaṅgulaḥ dairghye jalakrīḍārataḥ sukhī
english: With hands and feet marked (as described), he is tormented by desire (kāma) for women and never attains satisfaction. He is ninety-six *aṅgulas* in length, delights in water sports, and is happy.

sanskrit: गङ्गायमुनयोर्मध्यदेशं पाति शतं समाः । वनान्ते निधनं याति भुक्त्वा सर्वसुखं भुवि ॥१६॥
transliteration: gaṅgāyamunayormadhyadeśaṃ pāti śataṃ samāḥ . vanānte nidhanaṃ yāti bhuktvā sarvasukhaṃ bhuvi.
separated_sandhi: gaṅgāyamunayoḥ madhyadeśam pāti śatam samāḥvanānte nidhanam yāti bhuktvā sarvasukham bhuvi
english: He rules the middle region between the Ganga and Yamuna for a hundred years. Having enjoyed all happiness on earth, he meets his end in a forest.

sanskrit: समौष्ठः कृशमध्यश्च चन्द्रकान्तिरुचिः पुमान् । सुगन्धो नातिरक्ताङ्गो न ह्रस्वो नातिदीर्घकः ॥१७॥
transliteration: samauṣṭhaḥ kṛśamadhyaśca candrakāntiruciḥ pumān . sugandho nātiraktāṅgo na hrasvo nātidīrghakaḥ.
separated_sandhi: samauṣṭhaḥ kṛśamadhyaḥ ca candrakāntiruciḥ pumānsugandhaḥ na atiraktāṅgaḥ na hrasvaḥ na atidīrghakaḥ
english: A man with symmetrical lips and a slender waist, having a charm like the moon's radiance. He is fragrant, not exceedingly red-limbed, neither short nor exceedingly tall.

sanskrit: समस्वच्छरदो हस्तिनाद आजानुबाहुधृक् । मुखं विश्वांगुलं दैर्घ्ये विस्तारे च दशाङ्गुलम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: samasvaccharado hastināda ājānubāhudhṛk . mukhaṃ viśvāṃgulaṃ dairghye vistāre ca daśāṅgulam.
separated_sandhi: samāsvacchaśaradaḥ hastinādaḥ ājānubāhudhṛkmukham viṃśāṅgulam dāirghye vistāre ca daśāṅgulam
english: Such a person has a clear and even complexion like autumn, a voice like an elephant's roar, and arms that reach their knees. Their face measures twenty finger-breadths, with its length and width each being ten finger-breadths.

sanskrit: मालव्यो मालवाख्यं च देशं पाति ससिन्धुकम् । सुखं सप्ततिवर्षान्तं भुक्त्वा याति सुलालयम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: mālavyo mālavākhyaṃ ca deśaṃ pāti sasindhukam . sukhaṃ saptativarṣāntaṃ bhuktvā yāti sulālayam.
separated_sandhi: mālavyaḥ mālavākhyam ca deśam pāti sasindhukamsukham saptativarṣāntam bhuktvā yāti sulālayam
english: A person characterized by the Malavya (yoga) protects the country known as Malava, including the region of Sindhu. After happily enjoying their reign for seventy years, they go to a good abode.

sanskrit: तनुद्विजमुखः शूरो नातिह्रस्वः कृशोदरः । मध्ये क्षामः सुजंघश्च मतिमान् पररन्ध्रवित् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tanudvijamukhaḥ śūro nātihrasvaḥ kṛśodaraḥ . madhye kṣāmaḥ sujaṃghaśca matimān pararandhravit.
separated_sandhi: tanudvijamukhaḥ śūraḥ na atihrasvaḥ kṛśodaraḥmadhye kṣāmaḥ sujaṅghaḥ ca matimān pararandhravit
english: This person has slender teeth and a delicate face, is brave, not too short, and thin-bellied. They are slender at the waist, have beautiful thighs, are intelligent, and discern the weaknesses of others.

sanskrit: शक्तो वनाद्रिदुर्गेषु सेनानीर्दन्तुरः शशः । चंचलो धातुवादी च स्त्रीशक्तोऽन्यधानान्वितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śakto vanādridurgeṣu senānīrdanturaḥ śaśaḥ . caṃcalo dhātuvādī ca strīśakto'nyadhānānvitaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śaktaḥ vanādridurgeṣu senānīḥ danturaḥ śaśaḥcañcalaḥ dhātuvādī ca strīśaktaḥ anyadhanānvitaḥ
english: A person with Śaśaka (yoga) is capable in forest and mountain fortresses, a military leader, and has prominent teeth. They are restless, interested in minerals or alchemy, devoted to women, and endowed with the wealth of others.

sanskrit: मालावीणामृदङ्गाऽस्त्ररेखाङ्कितकरांघ्रिकः । भूपोऽयं वसुधा पाति जीवन् खाद्रिसमाः सुखी ॥२२॥
transliteration: mālāvīṇāmṛdaṅgā'strarekhāṅkitakarāṃghrikaḥ . bhūpo'yaṃ vasudhā pāti jīvan khādrisamāḥ sukhī.
separated_sandhi: mālāvīṇāmṛdaṅgāstrekhāṅkitakarāṅghrikaḥ bhūpaḥayam vasudhā pāti jīvan khādri samāḥ sukhī
english: This king, whose hands and feet are adorned with marks of garlands, lutes, drums, and weapons, protects the earth for seventy years, living happily.

sanskrit: अथ पञ्चमहाभूतच्छायाज्ञानं वदामि ते । ज्ञायते येन खेटानां वर्तमानदशा बुधैः ॥१॥
transliteration: atha pañcamahābhūtacchāyājñānaṃ vadāmi te . jñāyate yena kheṭānāṃ vartamānadaśā budhaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: atha pañcamahābhūtacchāyājñānam vadāmi tejñāyate yena kheṭānām vartamānadaśā budhaiḥ
english: Now, I will explain to you the knowledge of the shadows of the five great elements (pañca mahābhūta), by which learned astrologers can understand the current planetary conditions.

sanskrit: शिखिभूखाम्बुवातानामधिपा मङ्गलादयः । तत्तद्बलावशाज्ज्ञेयं तत्तद्भूतभवं फलम् ॥२॥
transliteration: śikhibhūkhāmbuvātānāmadhipā maṅgalādayaḥ . tattadbalāvaśājjñeyaṃ tattadbhūtabhavaṃ phalam.
separated_sandhi: śikhibhūkhāmbuvātānām adhipāḥ maṅgalādayaḥtattadbalāvaśāt jñeyam tattadbhūtabhavam phalam
english: Mars and others are the lords of Fire, Earth, Sky (ether), Water, and Air respectively. Therefore, the results arising from each element (bhūta) should be understood based on the strength of their respective lords.

sanskrit: सबले मङ्गले वह्निस्वभावो जायते नरः । बुधे महीस्वभावः स्यादाकाशप्रकृतिर्गुरौ ॥३॥
transliteration: sabale maṅgale vahnisvabhāvo jāyate naraḥ . budhe mahīsvabhāvaḥ syādākāśaprakṛtirgurau.
separated_sandhi: sabale maṅgale vahnisvabhāvaḥ jāyate naraḥbudhe mahīsvabhāvaḥ syāt ākāśaprakṛtiḥ gurau
english: When Mars is strong, a person's nature (svabhāva) becomes fiery. When Mercury is strong, their nature (svabhāva) is earthy. When Jupiter is strong, their nature (prakṛti) is ethereal.

sanskrit: शुक्रे जलस्वभावश्च मारुतप्रकृतिः शनौ । मिश्रैर्मिश्रस्वभावश्च विज्ञेयो द्विजसत्तम ॥४॥
transliteration: śukre jalasvabhāvaśca mārutaprakṛtiḥ śanau . miśrairmiśrasvabhāvaśca vijñeyo dvijasattama.
separated_sandhi: śukre jalasvabhāvaḥ ca mārutaprakṛtiḥ śanaumiśraiḥ miśrasvabhāvaḥ ca vijñeyaḥ dvijasattama
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), it should be known that Venus has a watery intrinsic nature (svabhāva), and Saturn has an airy intrinsic nature (prakṛti). From combinations, a mixed intrinsic nature (svabhāva) is to be understood.

sanskrit: सूर्ये वह्निस्वभावश्च जलप्रकृतिको विधौ । स्वदशायां ग्रहाश्छायां व्यंजयन्ति स्वभूतजाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: sūrye vahnisvabhāvaśca jalaprakṛtiko vidhau . svadaśāyāṃ grahāśchāyāṃ vyaṃjayanti svabhūtajām.
separated_sandhi: sūrye vahnisvabhāvaḥ ca jalaprakṛtikaḥ vidhausvadaśāyām grahāḥ chāyām vyañjayanti svabhūtjam
english: The Sun has a fiery intrinsic nature (svabhāva), and the Moon has a watery intrinsic nature (prakṛti). During their own planetary periods (daśā), the planets manifest the influence born from their own elemental qualities.

sanskrit: क्षुधार्तश्चपलः शूरः कृशः प्राज्ञोऽतिभक्षणः । तीक्ष्नो गौरतनुर्मानी वह्निप्रकृतिको नरः ॥६॥
transliteration: kṣudhārtaścapalaḥ śūraḥ kṛśaḥ prājño'tibhakṣaṇaḥ . tīkṣno gauratanurmānī vahniprakṛtiko naraḥ.
separated_sandhi: kṣudhārtaḥ capalaḥ śūraḥ kṛśaḥ prājñaḥ atibhakṣaṇaḥtīkṣṇaḥ gauratanuḥ mānī vahni-prakṛtikaḥ naraḥ
english: A person (nara) who possesses a fiery intrinsic nature (prakṛti) is hungry, restless, brave, lean, intelligent, prone to excessive eating, sharp or fierce, fair-bodied, and proud.

sanskrit: कर्पूरोत्पलफ़न्धाढ्यो भोगी स्थिरसुखी बली । क्षमावान् सिंहनादश्च महीप्रकृतिको नरः ॥७॥
transliteration: karpūrotpalapha़ndhāḍhyo bhogī sthirasukhī balī . kṣamāvān siṃhanādaśca mahīprakṛtiko naraḥ.
separated_sandhi: karpūrotpalagandhāḍhyaḥ bhogī sthirasukhī balīkṣamāvān siṃhanādaḥ ca mahīprakṛtikaḥ naraḥ
english: A person (nara) who possesses an earthy intrinsic nature (prakṛti) is fragrant like camphor and lotus, enjoys pleasures, experiences lasting happiness, is strong, patient, and has a commanding voice like a lion's roar.

sanskrit: शब्दार्थवित् सुनीतिज्ञो प्रगल्भो ज्ञानसंयुतः । विवृतास्योऽतिदीर्घश्च व्योमप्रकृतिसम्भवः ॥८॥
transliteration: śabdārthavit sunītijño pragalbho jñānasaṃyutaḥ . vivṛtāsyo'tidīrghaśca vyomaprakṛtisambhavaḥ.
separated_sandhi: śabdārthavit sunītijñaḥ pragalbhaḥ jñānasaṃyutaḥvivṛtāsyaḥ atidīrghaḥ ca vyomaprakṛtisaṃbhavaḥ
english: A person born of the nature of ether (vyoma prakṛti) is knowledgeable about the meanings of words, understands good policy, is confident, endowed with knowledge, has a wide mouth, and is very tall.

sanskrit: कान्तिमान् भारवाही च प्रियवाक् पृथिवीपतिः । बहुमित्रा मृदुर्विद्वान् जलप्रकृतिसम्भवः ॥९॥
transliteration: kāntimān bhāravāhī ca priyavāk pṛthivīpatiḥ . bahumitrā mṛdurvidvān jalaprakṛtisambhavaḥ.
separated_sandhi: kāntimān bhāravāhī ca priyavāk pṛthivīpatiḥbahumitrā mṛduḥ vidvān jalaprakṛtisaṃbhavaḥ
english: A person born of the nature of water (jala prakṛti) is radiant, a bearer of burdens, pleasant-spoken, a ruler (pṛthivīpati), has many friends, is gentle, and learned.

sanskrit: वायुतत्त्वाधिको दाता क्रोधी गौरोऽटनप्रियः । भूपतिश्च दुराधर्षः कृशाङ्गो जायते जनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāyutattvādhiko dātā krodhī gauro'ṭanapriyaḥ . bhūpatiśca durādharṣaḥ kṛśāṅgo jāyate janaḥ.
separated_sandhi: vāyutattvādhikaḥ dātā krodhī gauraḥ aṭanapriyaḥbhūpatiḥ ca durādharṣaḥ kṛśāṅgaḥ jāyate janaḥ
english: A person (jana) born with a predominance of the air element (vāyutattva) becomes generous, wrathful, fair-skinned, fond of wandering, a ruler (bhūpati), unassailable, and lean-bodied.

sanskrit: स्वर्णदीप्तिः शुभा दृष्टिः सर्वकार्यार्थसिद्धिता । विजयो धनलाभश्च वह्निभायां प्रजायते ॥११॥
transliteration: svarṇadīptiḥ śubhā dṛṣṭiḥ sarvakāryārthasiddhitā . vijayo dhanalābhaśca vahnibhāyāṃ prajāyate.
separated_sandhi: svarṇadīptiḥ śubhā dṛṣṭiḥ sarvakāryārthasiddhitāvijayaḥ dhanalābhaḥ ca vahnibhāyām prajāyate
english: Gold-like radiance, auspicious vision, the accomplishment of all tasks and objectives, victory, and gain of wealth manifest in the nature of fire.

sanskrit: इष्टगन्धः शरीरे स्यात् सुस्निग्धनखदन्तता । धर्मार्थसुखलाभश्च भूमिच्छाया यदा भवेत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: iṣṭagandhaḥ śarīre syāt susnigdhanakhadantatā . dharmārthasukhalābhaśca bhūmicchāyā yadā bhavet.
separated_sandhi: iṣṭagandhaḥ śarīre syāt susnigdhakhadantatādharmaarthasukhalābhaḥ ca bhūmicchāyā yadā bhavet
english: When the shadow of the earth (bhūmicchāyā) is present, there will be a pleasant fragrance in the body, and one's nails and teeth will be smooth and well-formed. There will also be a gain of righteousness (dharma), prosperity, and happiness.

sanskrit: स्वच्चा गगनजा छाया वाक्पटुत्वप्रदा भवेत् । सुशब्दश्रवणोद्भूतं सुखं तत्र प्रजायते ॥१३॥
transliteration: svaccā gaganajā chāyā vākpaṭutvapradā bhavet . suśabdaśravaṇodbhūtaṃ sukhaṃ tatra prajāyate.
separated_sandhi: svacchā gaganajā chāyā vākpaṭutvapradā bhavetsuśabdaśravaṇodbhūtam sukham tatra prajāyate
english: When there is a clear, sky-born shadow (gaganajā chāyā), it bestows eloquence. From this, happiness arises there, born from the hearing of auspicious sounds.

sanskrit: मृदुता स्वस्थता देहे जलच्छाया यदा भवेत् । तदाऽभिष्टरसस्वादसुखं भवति देहिनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: mṛdutā svasthatā dehe jalacchāyā yadā bhavet . tadā'bhiṣṭarasasvādasukhaṃ bhavati dehinaḥ.
separated_sandhi: mṛdutā svasthatā dehe jalacchāyā yadā bhavettadā abhiṣṭarasasvādasukham bhavati dehinaḥ
english: When the shadow of water (jalacchāyā) is present, there will be softness and good health in the body. Then, for the embodied being, happiness from the taste of desired flavors (rasa) arises.

sanskrit: मालिन्यं मूढता दैत्यं रोगश्च पवनोद्भवाः । तदा च शोकसन्तापौ वायुच्छाया यदा भवेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: mālinyaṃ mūḍhatā daityaṃ rogaśca pavanodbhavāḥ . tadā ca śokasantāpau vāyucchāyā yadā bhavet.
separated_sandhi: mālinyam mūḍhatā daityam rogaḥ ca pavanodbhavāḥtadā ca śokasantāpau vāyucchāyā yadā bhavet
english: When the shadow of wind (vāyucchāyā) is present, there will be impurity, foolishness, a demonic nature, and diseases arising from imbalances of the wind (pavana) element. Then, there will also be sorrow and distress.

sanskrit: एवं फलं बुधैर्ज्ञेयं सबलेषु कुजादिषु । निर्बलेषु तथा तेषु वक्तव्यं व्यत्ययाद् द्विज ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṃ phalaṃ budhairjñeyaṃ sabaleṣu kujādiṣu . nirbaleṣu tathā teṣu vaktavyaṃ vyatyayād dvija.
separated_sandhi: evam phalam budhaiḥ jñeyam sabaleṣu kujādiṣunirbaleṣu tathā teṣu vaktavyam vyatyayāt dvija
english: O twice-born (dvija), the wise (budhaiḥ) should understand the results (phalam) in this manner (evam): when Mars and the other planets (kujādiṣu) are strong (sabaleṣu), the effects should be declared accordingly. Similarly (tathā), when they (teṣu) are weak (nirbaleṣu), the results should be stated as the opposite (vyatyayāt).

sanskrit: नीचशत्रुभगैश्चापि विपरीतं फलं वदेत् । फलाप्तिरबलैः खेटैः स्वप्नचिन्तासु जायते ॥१७॥
transliteration: nīcaśatrubhagaiścāpi viparītaṃ phalaṃ vadet . phalāptirabalaiḥ kheṭaiḥ svapnacintāsu jāyate.
separated_sandhi: nīcaśatrubhagaiḥ ca api viparītam phalam vadetphalāptiḥ abalaiḥ kheṭaiḥ svapnacintāsu jāyate
english: One should also (ca api) declare an opposite (viparītam) result (phalam) when planets (bhagaiḥ) are in debilitation (nīca) or in an enemy's sign (śatru). The attainment (āptiḥ) of results (phala) through weak (abalaiḥ) planets (kheṭaiḥ) occurs (jāyate) in dreams and anxieties (svapnacintāsu).

sanskrit: तद्दुष्टफलशान्त्यर्थमपि चाज्ञातजन्मनाम् । फलपक्त्या दशा ज्ञेया वर्तमाना नभःसदाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tadduṣṭaphalaśāntyarthamapi cājñātajanmanām . phalapaktyā daśā jñeyā vartamānā nabhaḥsadām.
separated_sandhi: tad duṣṭaphalaśāntyartham api ca ajñātajanmanāmphalapaktyā daśā jñeyā vartamānā nabhaḥsadām
english: And (ca api) for the appeasement (śāntyartham) of those (tad) evil (duṣṭa) results (phala), also for those (nām) whose birth time (janma) is unknown (ajñāta), the current (vartamānā) planetary period (daśā) of the planets (nabhaḥsadām) should be understood (jñeyā) by means of the unfolding of results (phalapaktyā).

sanskrit: अथो गुणवशेनाहं कथयामि फलं द्विज । सत्त्वग्रहोदये जातो भवेत्सत्त्वाधिकः सुधीः ॥१॥
transliteration: atho guṇavaśenāhaṃ kathayāmi phalaṃ dvija . sattvagrahodaye jāto bhavetsattvādhikaḥ sudhīḥ.
separated_sandhi: atho guṇavaśena aham kathayāmi phalam dvijasattvagrahodaye jātaḥ bhavet sattvādhikaḥ sudhīḥ
english: Now (atho), O twice-born (dvija), I (aham) will explain (kathayāmi) the results (phalam) based on (vaśena) the inherent qualities (guṇa) of the planets. A person (jātaḥ) born when a "sattva" (sattva) quality planet (graha) is prominent (udaye) becomes (bhavet) one possessing an abundance of "sattva" (sattvādhikaḥ) and is intelligent (sudhīḥ).

sanskrit: रजःखेटोदये विज्ञो रजोगुणसमन्वितः । तमःखेटोदये मूर्खो भवेज्जातस्तमोऽधिकः ॥२॥
transliteration: rajaḥkheṭodaye vijño rajoguṇasamanvitaḥ . tamaḥkheṭodaye mūrkho bhavejjātastamo'dhikaḥ.
separated_sandhi: rajaḥkheṭa udaye vijñaḥ rajoguṇasamanvitaḥ |tamaḥkheṭa udaye mūrkhaḥ bhavet jātaḥ tamaḥ adhikaḥ
english: If a person is born when a planet indicating the quality of passion (rajas) rises, they will be knowledgeable and endowed with the quality of passion (rajas). If a person is born when a planet indicating the quality of ignorance (tamas) rises, they will be foolish and predominantly influenced by the quality of ignorance (tamas).

sanskrit: गुणसाम्ययुतो जातो गुणसाम्यखगोदये । एवं चतुर्विधा विप्र जायन्तो जन्तवो भुवि ॥३॥
transliteration: guṇasāmyayuto jāto guṇasāmyakhagodaye . evaṃ caturvidhā vipra jāyanto jantavo bhuvi.
separated_sandhi: guṇasāmyayutaḥ jātaḥ guṇasāmyakhaga udaye |evam caturvidhā vipra jāyantaḥ jantavaḥ bhuvi
english: Similarly, a person born when a planet indicating the balance of the qualities (guṇas) rises is endowed with that balance of qualities (guṇas). Thus, O Brahmin, four types of living beings are born on earth.

sanskrit: उत्तमो मध्यमो नीच उदासीन इति क्रमात् । तेषां गुणानहं वक्ष्ये नारदारिप्रभाषितान् ॥४॥
transliteration: uttamo madhyamo nīca udāsīna iti kramāt . teṣāṃ guṇānahaṃ vakṣye nāradāriprabhāṣitān.
separated_sandhi: uttamaḥ madhyamaḥ nīcaḥ udāsīnaḥ iti kramāt| teṣām guṇān aham vakṣye nāradādiprabhāṣitān
english: Superior, mediocre, inferior, and indifferent (udāsīna) - these are the types in sequence. I shall now describe their qualities (guṇas), as expounded by Nārada and other sages.

sanskrit: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च । अलोभः सत्यवादित्वं जने सत्त्वाधिके गुणाः ॥५॥
transliteration: śamo damastapaḥ śaucaṃ kṣāntirārjavameva ca . alobhaḥ satyavāditvaṃ jane sattvādhike guṇāḥ.
separated_sandhi: śamaḥ damaḥ tapaḥ śaucam kṣāntiḥ ārjavam eva ca| alobhaḥ satyavāditvam jane sattvādhike guṇāḥ
english: Calmness, self-restraint, austerity (tapas), purity, forgiveness, as well as straightforwardness, non-greed, and truthfulness - these are the qualities (guṇas) present in a person who is predominantly influenced by the quality of goodness (sattva).

sanskrit: शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाऽप्यपलायनम् । साधूनां रक्षणं चेति गुणा ज्ञेया रजोऽधिके ॥६॥
transliteration: śauryaṃ tejo dhṛtirdākṣyaṃ yuddhe cā'pyapalāyanam . sādhūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ ceti guṇā jñeyā rajo'dhike.
separated_sandhi: śauryaṃ tejaḥ dhṛtiḥ dākṣyaṃ yuddhe ca api apalāyanamsādhūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ ca iti guṇāḥ jñeyāḥ rajaḥ adhike
english: Courage, vigor, fortitude, dexterity, and not retreating in battle, along with the protection of the virtuous – these qualities are to be recognized in one who has an abundance of rajas.

sanskrit: लोभश्चासत्यवादित्वं जाड्यमालस्यमेव च । सेवाकर्मपटुत्वंच गुणा एते तमोऽधिके ॥७॥
transliteration: lobhaścāsatyavāditvaṃ jāḍyamālasyameva ca . sevākarmapaṭutvaṃca guṇā ete tamo'dhike.
separated_sandhi: lobhaḥ ca asatyavāditvaṃ jāḍyaṃ ālasyam eva casevākarma-paṭutvaṃ ca guṇāḥ ete tamaḥ adhike
english: Greed, untruthfulness, dullness, and laziness, along with proficiency in service-related tasks – these qualities are found in one who has an abundance of tamas.

sanskrit: कृषिकर्मणि वाणिज्ये पटुत्वं पशुपालने । सत्यासत्यप्रभाषित्वं गुणसाम्ये गुणा इमे ॥८॥
transliteration: kṛṣikarmaṇi vāṇijye paṭutvaṃ paśupālane . satyāsatyaprabhāṣitvaṃ guṇasāmye guṇā ime.
separated_sandhi: kṛṣikarmaṇi vāṇijye paṭutvaṃ paśupālanesatyāsatya-prabhāṣitvaṃ guṇasāmye guṇāḥ ime
english: Proficiency in agricultural work, commerce, and animal husbandry, along with speaking both truth and untruth – these are the qualities found when the guṇa-s are in a balanced state.

sanskrit: गतैश्च लक्षणैर्लक्ष्य उत्तमो मध्यमोऽधमः । उदासीनश्च विप्रेन्द तं तत्कर्मणि योजयेत् ॥९॥
transliteration: gataiśca lakṣaṇairlakṣya uttamo madhyamo'dhamaḥ . udāsīnaśca viprenda taṃ tatkarmaṇi yojayet.
separated_sandhi: gataiḥ ca lakṣaṇaiḥ lakṣyaḥ uttamaḥ madhyamaḥadhamaḥ udāsīnaḥ ca viprendra taṃ tat-karmaṇi yojayet
english: O excellent brahmin (viprendra), by the characteristics mentioned before, one should identify the superior, the mediocre, the inferior, and also the indifferent person. One should then engage such a person in their appropriate task (karma).

sanskrit: द्वाभ्यामेकोऽधिको यश्च तस्याधिक्यं निगद्यते । अन्यथा गुणसाम्यं च विज्ञेयं द्विजसत्तम ॥१०॥
transliteration: dvābhyāmeko'dhiko yaśca tasyādhikyaṃ nigadyate . anyathā guṇasāmyaṃ ca vijñeyaṃ dvijasattama.
separated_sandhi: dvābhyām ekaḥ adhikaḥ yaḥ ca tasya ādhikyamnigadyate anyathā guṇasāmyam ca vijñeyam dvijasattama
english: If one (planet) is stronger than two (others), its dominance is declared. Otherwise, an equality of qualities should be understood, O best among the twice-born.

sanskrit: सेव्यसेवकयोरेवं कन्यकावरयोरपि । गुणैः सदृशयोरेव प्रीतिर्भवति निश्चला ॥११॥
transliteration: sevyasevakayorevaṃ kanyakāvarayorapi . guṇaiḥ sadṛśayoreva prītirbhavati niścalā.
separated_sandhi: sevyasevakayoḥ evam kanyakāvarayoḥ apiguṇaiḥ sadṛśayoḥ eva prītiḥ bhavati niścalā
english: Similarly, between the master and the servant, and also between the maiden and her chosen groom, steadfast affection arises only when they are similar in qualities.

sanskrit: उदासीनोऽधमस्यैवमुदासीनस्य मध्यमः । मध्यमस्योत्तमो विप्र प्रभवत्याश्रयो मुदे ॥१२॥
transliteration: udāsīno'dhamasyaivamudāsīnasya madhyamaḥ . madhyamasyottamo vipra prabhavatyāśrayo mude.
separated_sandhi: udāsīnaḥ adhamasya evam udāsīnasya madhyamaḥmadhyamasya uttamaḥ vipra prabhavati āśrayaḥ mude
english: Thus, O brahmin, a neutral person (provides) support to an inferior one, a mediocre person to a neutral one, and a superior person to a mediocre one; such support brings joy.

sanskrit: अतोऽवरा वरात् कन्य सेव्यतः सेवकोऽवरः । गुणैस्ततः सुखोत्पत्तिरन्यथा हानिरेव हि ॥१३॥
transliteration: ato'varā varāt kanya sevyataḥ sevako'varaḥ . guṇaistataḥ sukhotpattiranyathā hānireva hi.
separated_sandhi: ataḥ avarā varāt kanya sevyataḥ sevakaḥ avaraḥguṇaiḥ tataḥ sukhotpattiḥ anyathā hāniḥ eva hi
english: Therefore, a maiden (kanyā) is considered subordinate to her chosen groom (vara), and a servant (sevaka) is subordinate to their master (sevya). Thus, happiness arises from suitable qualities; otherwise, there is certainly only loss.

sanskrit: वीर्यं क्षेत्रं प्रसूतेश्च समयः सङ्गतिस्तथा । उत्तमादिगुणे हेतुर्बलवानुत्तरोत्तरम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vīryaṃ kṣetraṃ prasūteśca samayaḥ saṅgatistathā . uttamādiguṇe heturbalavānuttarottaram.
separated_sandhi: vīryam kṣetram prasūteḥ ca samayaḥ saṅgatiḥtathā uttamādiguṇe hetuḥ balavān uttarottaram
english: Potency (vīrya), the field (kṣetra), the time of birth, and also the planetary associations (saṅgati) are the causes for qualities ranging from excellent downwards. Each succeeding factor is progressively more powerful.

sanskrit: अतः प्रसूतिकालस्य सदृशो जातके गुणः । जायते तं परीक्ष्यैव फलं वाच्यं विचक्षणैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ataḥ prasūtikālasya sadṛśo jātake guṇaḥ . jāyate taṃ parīkṣyaiva phalaṃ vācyaṃ vicakṣaṇaiḥ.
separated_sandhi: ataḥ prasūtikālasya sadṛśaḥ jātake guṇaḥ jāyatetam parīkṣya eva phalam vācyam vicakṣaṇaiḥ
english: Therefore, the qualities in a natal chart (jātaka) are analogous to the time of birth. Thus, experts should declare the results only after thoroughly examining it.

sanskrit: कालः सृजति भूतनि पात्यथो संहरत्यपि । इश्वरः सर्वलोकानामव्ययो भगवान् विभु ॥१६॥
transliteration: kālaḥ sṛjati bhūtani pātyatho saṃharatyapi . iśvaraḥ sarvalokānāmavyayo bhagavān vibhu.
separated_sandhi: kālaḥ sṛjati bhūtāni pāti atho saṃharati apiīśvaraḥ sarvalokānām avyayaḥ bhagavān vibhu
english: Time (kāla) creates all beings, sustains them, and also destroys them. He is the imperishable, divine, all-pervading Lord (īśvara) of all worlds.

sanskrit: तच्छक्तिः प्रकृतिः प्रोक्ता मुनिभिस्त्रिगुणात्मिका । तथा विभक्तोऽव्यक्तोऽपि व्यक्तो भवति देहिनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tacchaktiḥ prakṛtiḥ proktā munibhistriguṇātmikā . tathā vibhakto'vyakto'pi vyakto bhavati dehinām.
separated_sandhi: tat śaktiḥ prakṛtiḥ proktā munibhiḥ triguṇātmakātathā vibhaktaḥ avyaktaḥ api vyaktaḥ bhavati dehinām
english: That (Lord's) power (śakti) is declared by the sages to be primordial nature (prakṛti), which is endowed with the three qualities (guṇas). Similarly, though undivided and unmanifest, it becomes manifest to embodied beings.

sanskrit: चतुर्धाऽवयवास्तस्य स्वगुणैश्च चतुर्विधः । जायन्ते ह्युत्तमो मध्ये उदासीनोऽधमः क्रमात् ॥१८॥
transliteration: caturdhā'vayavāstasya svaguṇaiśca caturvidhaḥ . jāyante hyuttamo madhye udāsīno'dhamaḥ kramāt.
separated_sandhi: caturdhā avayavāḥ tasya svaguṇaiḥ ca caturvidhaḥjāyante hi uttamaḥ madhye udāsīnaḥ adhamaḥ kramāt
english: Its constituents are divided into four categories, and by their intrinsic nature (svaguṇaiḥ), it (the entity or its manifestation) is also of four types. Indeed, the excellent, the mediocre, the indifferent, and the lowest manifest in succession.

sanskrit: उत्तमे तूत्तमो जन्तुर्मध्येऽङ्गे च मध्यमः । उदासीने ह्यदासीनो जायते चाऽधमेऽधमः ॥१९॥
transliteration: uttame tūttamo janturmadhye'ṅge ca madhyamaḥ . udāsīne hyadāsīno jāyate cā'dhame'dhamaḥ.
separated_sandhi: uttame tu uttamaḥ jantuḥ madhye aṅge ca madhyamaḥudāsīne hi udāsīnaḥ jāyate ca adhame adhamaḥ
english: Indeed, an excellent being (jantu) manifests in the excellent category. A mediocre one manifests in the middle part (aṅga). An indifferent one indeed manifests in the indifferent category, and a lowest one in the lowest category.

sanskrit: उत्तमाङ्गं शरिस्तस्य मध्यमाङ्गमुरःस्थलम् । जंघाद्वयौदासीनमधमं पदमुच्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: uttamāṅgaṃ śaristasya madhyamāṅgamuraḥsthalam . jaṃghādvayaudāsīnamadhamaṃ padamucyate.
separated_sandhi: uttamāṅgam śiras tasya madhyamāṅgam uraḥsthalamjaṅghādvayam udāsīnam adhamam padam ucyate
english: His head (śiras) is called the excellent limb (aṅga). The chest region (uraḥsthalam) is called the middle limb (aṅga). The pair of thighs (jaṅghādvayam) is called the indifferent (udāsīnam) part, and the foot (padam) is declared the lowest (adhama).

sanskrit: एवं गुणवशादेव कालभेदः प्रजायते । जातिभेदस्तु तद्भेदाज्जायतेऽत्र चराचरे ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṃ guṇavaśādeva kālabhedaḥ prajāyate . jātibhedastu tadbhedājjāyate'tra carācare.
separated_sandhi: evam guṇavaśāt eva kālabhedaḥ prajāyatejātibhedaḥ tu tadbhedāt jāyate atra carācare
english: Thus, the divisions of time (kālabheda) arise solely due to the power of qualities (guṇavaśāt). And indeed, the distinctions of species or class (jātibheda) also arise from those same divisions in this entire movable and immovable creation (carācara).

sanskrit: एवं भगवता सृष्टं विभुना स्वगुणैः समम् । चतुर्विधेन कालेन जगदेतच्चतुर्विधम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṃ bhagavatā sṛṣṭaṃ vibhunā svaguṇaiḥ samam . caturvidhena kālena jagadetaccaturvidham.
separated_sandhi: evam bhagavatā sṛṣṭam vibhunā svaguṇaiḥ samamcaturvidhena kālena jagat etat caturvidham
english: Thus, this four-fold world was created by the all-pervading Lord (Bhagavān), endowed with His own qualities, through time, which is also four-fold.

sanskrit: जन्मकालवशादेवं फलं प्रोक्तं त्वया मुने । यज्जन्मसमयोऽज्ञातो ज्ञेयं तस्य फलं कथम् ॥१॥
transliteration: janmakālavaśādevaṃ phalaṃ proktaṃ tvayā mune . yajjanmasamayo'jñāto jñeyaṃ tasya phalaṃ katham.
separated_sandhi: janmakālavaśāt evam phalam proktam tvayā mune yatjanmasamayaḥ ajñātaḥ jñeyam tasya phalam katham
english: O sage, you have thus stated that the results are determined by the time of birth. But if someone's birth time is unknown, how can their results be ascertained?

sanskrit: शुभं वाऽप्यशुभं वापि मनुजस्य पुराकृतम् । अस्ति कश्चिदुपायश्चेत् तं भवान् वक्तुमर्हति ॥२॥
